commit_message:
Optimize watch face start up performance

In addition to the API change to support lazy Icons, this patch
makes ResourceDisplayTextWithIndex lazily evaluate the string
which is a decent load performace win for complex watchfaces and
it delays user Style schema validation until after the first frame has been rendered, to avoid this lower priority task competing for cpu/gpu resources.

Relnote: We've added optional support for constructing UserStyleSettings and UserStyleOptions with lazily evaluated Icons.  This is a performance win because it moves Icon construction off the critical path of watch face initialization.
Test: Manual test with perfetto to confirm that lazy icon init is a perf win
Bug: 365522217
Change-Id: Iaf43d401f17a1cca4d1f3a48e363e14fa19c8bcd
llm_reason: The commit optimizes watch face startup performance by making ResourceDisplayTextWithIndex evaluate strings lazily and delaying schema validation until after the first frame is rendered. This reduces CPU contention during initialization.

commit_message:
Move ComplexLayoutState to LayoutNode

There's no reason to have two data stores for the same information.
This remove ComplexLayoutState and unifies the logic into
LayoutNode. The average performance for measure/layout is
approximately 20% better based on CheckboxesInRowsTestCase.

This also changes how the varargs Layout works. Instead
of tagging with ParentData and inserting LayoutNodes, it
adds a DataNode for each composable child. This makes
the search for measurables a little faster and composition
just a little more efficient.

Test: ran demo and CheckboxesInRows test
Change-Id: I31a4075b3d367a63c5f72aa83adf88c325b31665
llm_reason: The commit refactors and unifies layout state management by removing redundant ComplexLayoutState and consolidating logic into LayoutNode, improving code structure and reducing complexity.

commit_message:
Make VpnFeaturesRegistryImpl calls async (#3247)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1204763839445752/f

### Description
The `VpnFeaturesRegistry` functionality is backed by a multiprocess shared preferences but all its API is synchronous and called from various different threads, including the UI thread.

This PR:
* moves its API to be async using `suspend` and offload them to `io` dispatcher
* refactors the calling side when necessary

### Steps to test this PR

Aside from CI, perform the following sanity testing

- [x] install from this branch
- [x] smoke test AppTP and VPN
- [x] NP cohort should be assigned correctly
- [x] AppTP/VPN should work correctly, on/off, off/on, on/on

commit_message:
Migrate DatabaseWriter to XPoet

This migrations allow Room to generate the database implementation in Kotlin.

Some notable changes:
* getAutoMigrations() was changed to not use Arrays.asList() and instead just add to an ArrayList. Keeps the XPoet part equivalent for both languages.
* For Java Dao impl getters emit memoization logic, but for Kotlin we'll just use Lazy, making the getter way simpler.
* This changes adds overriding abstract dao property, but it has a few flaws. Follow-up bug filled.

New XPoet API:
* Created the fallback API for XPropertySpec to allow access to the actual builder when the code generation diverges too much between languages. Used to add 'volatile' modifier in Java and for property overriding.

Bug: 127483380
Test: DatabaseKotlinCodeGenTest + existing
Change-Id: I723023639b276368e87c6abe3dc3b807bc419b42
llm_reason: The commit refactors code to use XPoet for Kotlin code generation, simplifying DAO property access by utilizing Lazy initialization and reducing boilerplate. This improves maintainability and readability.

commit_message:
Revert^2 "Adopt common Drag&Drop API for multiplatform"

This reverts commit 06ce8e5d7c6b4a1e9c7c8241c786e3be4ccefd3f.

Reason for revert: Temporary restored old API in foundation

Relnote: `Modifier.dragAndDropSource` was adopted to multiplatform. Drag gesture start detection is now performed by Compose itself. `Modifier.dragAndDropSource` does not allow customizing the start of the gesture anymore and instead exposes a lambda that is invoked with an offset when a drag session starts. The old overload has been deprecated. To use the new overload, specify a name for the lambda parameter or explicitly specify the offset parameter in the lambda. See `DragAndDropSamples.kt`.
Relnote: `DragAndDropModifierNode` is deprecated in favor to `DragAndDropSourceModifierNode` and `DragAndDropTargetModifierNode`

### Description
This PR adds the required Product Pixels for the soft release.

### Steps to test this PR

_Success rate daily_
You’ll need to run some sync operations and force some api errors. 
In `SyncDailyReportingWorkerScheduler`, comment out the enqueuing in the
WorkManager
In `RealSyncEngine`, comment out line 115
- [x] Fresh install, enable sync
- [x] Add some bookmarks and folders
Force some API errors as described in the task
- [x] Ensure errors are stored in the DB
In SyncStatsRepository, change the date filter to be today and not
yesterday
- [x] Ensure errors are stored in the DB
Remove the comment in `SyncDailyReportingWorkerScheduler` so that the
work is enqueued and install the app again
Remove the comment in `RealSyncEngine` line 115
- [x] Verify pixel is sent

_Timestamp resolution triggered_
Pixel sent when there are local changes after a sync operation has
started
- [ ] Device A -> Add a bookmark and enable sync
- [ ] Device B -> Join sync account from Device A
- [ ] Device A -> Open bookmark edit dialog
- [ ] Device B -> Rename bookmark
- [ ] Device A -> Rename bookmark
- [ ] Device A -> Verify timestamp resolution conflict triggered,
bookmark has name from Device A

commit_message:
View my pledge (#545)

* - Created a color state list for ended projects button_pledge_ended.
- Renamed the color state list for live projects button_pledge_live.
- Removed the overscroll from the rewards list.
- No rewards
  - There is a bug (IMO) with the `project.backing.rewardId`. It returns null, but the no reward `reward.id` is `0`. It returns null, but the no reward reward.id is 0. I updated BackingUtils.isBacked to handle that case (see tests).
- All rewards
  - When the project has ended, the pledge button is not visible unless the user has backed that reward. If the user backed the reward, the pledge button is black and says `View my reward` and the check is visible.
- Added `ProjectUtils.pledgeButtonColor` that returns the color resource ID of the rewards button based on project and backing status.
- Rewards are now free scrolling with `18dp` between them.
- Added the output `backedRewardPosition` to `RewardFragmentViewModel` so the backed reward is centered in the rewards list when the rewards sheet is opened on an ended project.
- Added `RewardUtils.isExpired` and `isTimeLimited` to handle logic for showing when a reward is no longer available.
- Added `RewardUtils.pledgeButtonColor` that returns the color resource ID of the rewards button based on project and if user has backed reward.
llm_reason: The commit simplifies and refactors various components, including view models and decorators, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability. For example, RewardDecoration now uses a simpler margin calculation, and multiple view model outputs are consolidated or removed.

commit_message:
Avoid encoding/decoding ComplicationData fields until needed.

This is done by backing the fields by a Map rather than a Bundle, and
writing/reading the typed values rather than parcelables.

Among other usages, this prevents ComplicationDataEvaluator from
constantly encoding/decoding protos and alike, which is expensive.

IMPORTANT:
  The new implementation prevents the Builder from changing an
  already-built WireComplicationData. This does not affect the public
  API and therefore doesn't need an API version bump, but it affects all
  direct usages of the wire format.

Bug: 339200482
Change-Id: I247ef19fb75186cb6d633aa6dd15ab728bf32485
llm_reason: The changes replace a MutableMap with a Bundle, reducing unnecessary encoding/decoding of ComplicationData fields. This optimizes for performance by avoiding expensive operations, aligning with energy efficiency.

commit_message:
Scaffold material component

Scaffold is a whole-screen component that is responsible for proper
orchestration between different screen-based components on the screen,
e.g. TopBars, Drawers, FloatingActionButton, etc.

Scaffold also provides reasonable default that people usually expect
with possiblities to override them.

To allow Scaffold to know about FAB and how it works together with
bottomAppBar, serious refactoring for BottomAppBar was needed. This
change brings orchestration responsibility back to Scaffold from BottomAppBar + unblocks important features, e.g.
SnackBar showing and bottomBar hidhing when scrolling (coming in future
CLs).

Relnote: Scaffold material component has been added. Scaffold implements
the basic material design visual layout structure.

Bug: 119399507
Test: ScaffoldTest has been added
Change-Id: I7731b4e0f0a5238176640d5a40431c5bf98185d2
llm_reason: The commit refactors BottomAppBar to improve its structure and readability, reducing code duplication by extracting common logic into reusable components. This simplifies the codebase and makes it easier to maintain.

commit_message:
Use `JvmIrCodegenFactory` in IR transform tests...

...rather than relying on internal psi2ir APIs. Using psi2ir directly
allowed the tests to go out of sync with the Kotlin compiler.

Additionally, this CL contains a number of smaller fixes and refactorings:

- Fix `irForLoop` in `AbstractComposeLowering.kt`. This code broke
  `testPrimitiveVarargParams` in recent Kotlin compiler versions, due to
  unbound symbols in `BuiltInSymbolsBase`. This went unnoticed since
  `JvmCompilation.compile` did not have this problem.
- Register an `IrMessageLogger` in tests to see backend errors and warnings.
- Remove `CompilerConfiguration` parameter from `ComposeIrGeneratorExtension`,
  since it duplicates all other configuration parameters and may not be
  available when we instantiate the extension for tests.
- Introduce `AbstractLiveLiteralTransformTests` to remove shared code from
  `LiveLiteralTransform*Test`.
- Remove `ComposeIrTransformTest`, since all subclasses of
  `AbstractIrTransformTest` were running IR transform tests using the Compose
  plugin.
- Split the extension point registration in `ComposePlugin.kt` into common
  (i.e., frontend) extension points and the IR extension point. This allows all
  tests to run the frontend checks from the plugin even if they need to
  register a different IR generation extension.
- Preserve `IrIfThenElseImpl` in `DeepCopyIrTreeWithSymbolsPreservingMetadata`,
  since this affects the printing in `IrSourcePrinter`.
- Set `correspondingPropertySymbol`s in `LiveLiteralTransformer`. This pass was
  technically producing invalid IR, but this was fixed up when running the IR
  through `DeepCopyIrTreeWithSymbols` in `ComposerParamTransformer`.

commit_message:
Always call enter and exit focus properties

We currently call enter and exit focus properties
during focus search, but not if we manually call
requestFocus or clearFocus. Calling these properties
manually has led to bugs in the past (b/b/267827550),
and this helps clean up the code too.

It should also be helpful for usecases where a parent
wants to intercept clear focus calls, and other
interop use cases.

commit_message:
Add ViewModel to JetNews.

Warning: The code in this commit does not correctly reset the ViewModel for ArticleScreen when postId changes.

Several large refactors to land this:

- Removed RefreshableUiState
- Reworked UiState to allow data, error, and loading at the same time (to support all use cases)
- Removed effects architecture and restructured using ViewModel
- Refactored Repository to expose Flow and suspend functions throughout
- Deleted JetNewsStatus global state

Composables of screens were refactored to follow hoisting best practices
- Each screen has a single stateful composable paired with a stateless composable at the top level
- Stateful composables use ViewModel to manage state
- Added slots where appropriate to avoid too many parameters

ArticleScreen:
- Moved favorites to ViewModel from JetNewsStatus
- Refactored composables to use new state

HomeScreen:
- Renames to make composables names match their function
- Added slot to LoadingContnent to extract a composable focused on initial load and swipe to refresh
- Renamed HomeScreenBodyWrapper to HomeScreenErrorAndContent to better match function
- Added empty UI state if the initial load fails ("Tap to load content")
- Hoisted snackbar state to ViewModel and made HomeScreenErrorAndContent stateless
- Renamed all composables related to displaying the post list to PostList from HomeScreenBody etc.
- Used unidirectional data flow throughout post list composables
- Renamed LoadingHomeScreen to FullScreenLoading to better match function
- Animated ErrorSnackbar on entrance and exit using AnimatedVisibility

InterestsScreen:
- Introduced TabContent object to describe a tab and it's composable content, this allows for slot usage for screen-level composables
- Stateless InterestScreen now takes a List<TabContent> which describes the tabs and their content
- Renamed InterestScreenBody to TabContent to better match function
- Moved selected topics to ViewModel, and removed global state
- Renamed *Tab composables to *List composables (e.g. TopicList) to better match function
- Removed string-key from topics, and introduced TopicSelection for TabWithSections
- Moved all previously global state into Repository (which is a singleton)
- Use unidirectional data flow throughout this screen

SwipeToRefreshLayout:
- Added TODO to document a know bug (b/164113834) that this code works around
- Disable swiping when state.value == true to avoid a bug created by duplicated state showing an infinite loading spinner
llm_reason: The commit refactors multiple components to use ViewModel and Flow, reducing code duplication and improving separation of concerns. It simplifies data management by replacing LiveData with StateFlow and removes unnecessary global state.

commit_message:
Refactor essential file migration; use CollectionManager

By performing the essential migration inside the CollectionManager
queue, we ensure that it has exclusive access to the collection files
for the duration of the call. This should hold even for legacy code, as
CollectionHelper.getCol() blocks until the queue is free.

I found the current code somewhat hard to follow, due in large part to
its Java origins, so I've also tried to tidy it up a fair bit. I think one
of the main reasons scoped storage has stalled so long is that people were
a bit intimidated by the code (I was at least!), and I hope that by making
it a bit simpler, people will feel more comfortable jumping in and helping
to get it over the line.

Notable changes:

- The validation and copying steps were intermixed and spread through
multiple files, which made it hard to follow. The source/dest folders are
now determined/validated separately, and then the actual copying
is done in a CollectionManager method. The chain starts in deckpicker.kt:migrate(),
as before.
- The typealiases and newtypes have been removed, as I felt they made
the code harder to follow. The newtype also served to validate the folder
was a scoped storage folder, but that's now done in our validation routine.
Instead, the variable names have been renamed to be clearer, eg
 destinationDirectory: ScopedAnkiDroidDirectory -> scopedDestinationDirectory: Directory
- -wal are now copied if found. Usually they would get
removed when the collection is closed, but if there is a stale -wal file,
SQLite won't clean it up until a write action is performed on the DB, so it's
safer to include it.
- The corruption check before and after migration has been removed. For one,
it was only checking some referential integrity issues, and not actually checking
if the SQLite DB was corrupt. We could change the code to fix this, but I don't
see the point - we're already confirming the bytes of the copied files exactly match
the bytes of the source files, so the extra checks seem superfluous. Doing a full
integrity check on large collections is also quite slow, and risks hanging the
UI if legacy code happens to try access the collection.
- Removed the CollectionHelper code that was opening another instance of the backend.
- Removed the LockedCollection machinery, as CollectionManager takes its place.
- Removed the RetriableException code, as it only existed to deal with concurrent
access that can not happen with CollectionManager.

Tentatively closes #13870. The first report looked like migration being
launched twice in parallel, and the second appears to have been caused by
StudyOptionsFragment refreshing the screen (perhaps in onResume),

commit_message:
[POC] Add MCP server to Maestro's CLI (#2530)

* [POC] Add MCP server using STDIO to Maestro's CLI

This proof-of-concept implements Model Context Protocol (MCP) server
capabilities for Maestro, enabling AI assistants to interact with
mobile test automation.

Motivation:
- Leverage Maestro's fantastic device automation
- Provide programmatic access to Maestro commands via MCP

Implementation:
- New MCP server module with core protocol handling
- Integration with existing Maestro CLI commands
- Basic error handling and response formatting

* Pulling in downgraded version of kotlin-sdk

* New MCP server implementation using the kotlin-sdk abstractions to simplify the code

* Added run_flow_files, run_flow, and check_flow_syntax tools

* Added inspect_view_hierarchy tool

* Added mcp tools: cheat_sheet and query_docs

* Trigger CI rebuild with updated kotlin-sdk dependency

* Trigger CI rebuild after kotlin-sdk Java compatibility fix

* Switched to lightweight YAML format for inspect_view_hieararchy tool

Instead of using JSON, the `inspect_view_hierarchy` tool now uses a l
ightweight YAML format to represent the view hierarchy. This change was made to
reduce the verbosity of the output, reducing token count and improving
"understandability" by LLMs.

It achieves this by 1) specifying an abbreviation and default values
schema at the top, and then 2) using a compact YAML format that
omits default values and uses abbreviations for common keys.

It adds functionality and refactors code structure without introducing or fixing specific issues related to energy, concurrency, storage, security, or complexity debt.

commit_message:
Wrote an implementation of R4A that uses the IR

Our old code generation strategy was to introduce extension points that allowed us to poke
at the compiler internals and effectively "trick" it into producing roughly what we wanted.  Then
we could go in after the fact and massage the bytecode until we got the desired output.  This
strategy was challenging to write/maintain and very error prone, leading to subtle bugs.

This commit is effectively a rewrite of the entire R4A compiler plugin to use the new IR backend.
This new approach will allow us to utilize the IR pipeline to perform transformations prior to
bytecode generation, which will provide us with a much better foundation for R4A's future.

This code still needs a bunch of cleanup, but I figured it's better to get it merged now
so we everyone else can start experimenting on top of it, rather than to wait until it's
perfect.  I'll go ahead and submit some cleanup diffs soon.

Change-Id: I0bb9218d0be22859c3b12d03b4d91c961c8aa9ce
llm_reason: The commit refactors the R4A compiler plugin to use the new IR backend, replacing a complex and error-prone bytecode manipulation approach with a more maintainable solution. This reduces technical debt by simplifying code generation and transformation processes.

commit_message:
Updates Material and Foundation components to expose a nullable MutableInteractionSource

Components that previously exposed an API such as:

interactionSource: MutableInteractionSource = remember { MutableInteractionSource() }

Have been updated to instead expose a nullable MutableInteractionSource that defaults to null. This allows for some components to never create and remember a MutableInteractionSource, and allows for some other components to lazily create one only when needed. Some components always need a MutableInteractionSource currently, so if null they will just create a new one internally - so for those cases there are no changes, but it allows for future optimizations.

Also deletes long deprecated experimental Card and Surface overloads.

Test: updateApi, existing tests
Bug: b/298048146
Relnote: "Material and Foundation components exposing a MutableInteractionSource in their API have been updated to now expose a nullable MutableInteractionSource that defaults to null. There are no semantic changes here: passing null means that you do not wish to hoist the MutableInteractionSource, and it will be created inside the component if needed. Changing to null allows for some components to never allocate a MutableInteractionSource, and allows for other components to only lazily create an instance when they need to, which improves performance across these components. If you are not using the MutableInteractionSource you pass to these components, it is recommended that you pass null instead. It is also recommended that you make similar changes in your own components."
Change-Id: I41c73c7809bc704da4ab6b4c2c74016d1380f001
llm_reason: The changes modify components to expose a nullable MutableInteractionSource that defaults to null, allowing for lazy creation and reducing unnecessary allocations. This optimizes performance by avoiding creating interaction sources when not needed.

commit_message:
Semantics no-op cleanups

Partly in response to lmr's broad code review, I did a pass of
superficial API/implementation cleanup.  The main changes are:

- I changed each Boolean SemanticsProperty where false is equivalent to
  not being present to take "Unit" instead.  This is conceptually
  clearer: it avoids questions like "can I cancel out a semantics from a
  merged child by setting it to false?"  Because "property = Unit" looks
  weird, I also changed the style of these to "property()".

- I moved the Semantics id generator closer to where it's used, in
  SemanticsModifierCore.  I made it internal and an AtomicInt.
  (Note that integer ids are heavily used in the Android
  AccessibilityNodeInfo APIs so I can't simply remove them entirely.)

- I deleted dead code.  Some examples include SemanticsHintOverrides,
  a public API not connected to anything, and SemanticsPropertyKey
  merge() open method which is never called.  (In both cases I have
  a different plan in mind for accessibility.)

Fixes: 145951226
Fixes: 145955412
Test: existing tests
Relnote: "Single-value semantics properties now use a calling style.
For example, 'semantics { hidden = true }' is now written as:
'semantics { hidden() }'."

Change-Id: Ic1afd12ea22c926babc9662f1804d80b33aa0cfc
llm_reason: The changes refactor the Semantics API to use Boolean properties instead of Unit, improve code clarity by changing method calls to property assignments, and remove dead code. This reduces technical debt and improves maintainability.

commit_message:
Adds auto-mirroring support to the generated icons

- Updated the vector XML parsing to read the `autoMirrored` attribute.
- Generated a new `automirrored` package that holds auto-mirrored icons
  per icon-theme. For example: Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowForward.
- Updated the ImageVectorGenerator to generate and place auto-mirrored
  icons at the new package.
- Have the generator mark any existing icons with an auto-mirrored
  version as deprecated, and suggest a replacement with the
  auto-mirrored one.
- Updated the generated test files to include an additional property
  that have the ImageVectors with the auto-mirror flag.
- Updated the tests to run against two groups of icons separately
  (mirrored and unmirrored).
- Split the API files generation to icons.txt and automirroed_icons.txt.
  Update the icons.txt to hold only icons that are not in the
  automirrored_icons.txt file.

Bug: 279989215
Test: Updated the generated test icons, and the tests themselves
Relnote: "Added support for auto-mirrored icons when rendered in
right-to-left layouts. Icons in the material-icons-core and
material-icons-extended modules are now providing additional icon sets
for supporting auto-mirroring when the icon allows it. The new sets are
prefixed with `Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled...` etc., and hold icons that
will auto-mirror on RTL layouts.
See developers.google.com/fonts/docs/material_icons for the list of
icons that can (and should) be auto-mirrored.
The previously provided icon properties for those icons are now marked
as deprecated, and provides a replacement-block suggestion to help with
the migration.
In case you don't have any special handling for mirroring icons on RTL,
we recommend migrating to the new set of icons. For example,
`Icons.Filled.ArrowBack` should be refactored to
`Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack`."

Change-Id: I4b5116207cb9c0dd1a8a72d2bd3f606f8fdd0129
llm_reason: The commit refactors code to separate auto-mirrored icons into a new package, reducing redundancy and improving maintainability. Changes include splitting API files, updating icon generation logic, and deprecating old references.

commit_message:
[Jetsurvey] Simplification of survey screens (#1058)

This eliminates the survey repository and related data models in favor
of simplifying the code base. Each of the different question types has a
generic composable such as MultipleChoiceQuestion, which takes all of
the relevant strings/resources to generate the UI. The actual survey
data is baked into a specific composable in Survey.kt, such as
FreeTimeQuestion, instead of coming from a data model.

The SurveyViewModel is now much more explicit with methods for each
question such as onFreeTimeResponse. In a production app, you'd likely
want this to be more flexible to handle server-side questions, but this
code pattern makes the sample easier to skim. Every response is
maintained in SurveyViewModel as a separate state. This increases
verbosity in favor of better readability.

Note: This does not animate between the final survey question and the
survey results. When the work to move the app to Compose Navigation is
done, the results will be a separate destination that is navigated to
and animated that way.

This fixes #1054.
llm_reason: The commit simplifies the codebase by removing the SurveyRepository and related data models, replacing them with a more straightforward approach using generic composables. This reduces complexity and improves readability.

commit_message:
Add adapters to turn UseCases into a CameraGraph

This change wires UseCase(s) together into a 'UseCaseCamera' that
CameraX can interact with through its internal interfaces. Each time a
UseCase is attached / detached the UseCaseCamera is closed and a new
instances is created. This causes the underlying CameraCaptureSession
to be recreated based on the current set of attached UseCases.

Additional changes:
* Refactored most of the direct CameraX interface implementation into
  an 'adapter' package.
* Introduced dagger for dependency management and to make testing
  easier for several scenarios. These classes live in a config package.
* Refactored the code that interacts with a single CameraGraph into a
  UseCaseCamera, and the code that tracks the currently active UseCases
  into a UseCaseManager.
* Added several mappings between CameraPipe metadata, and CameraX
  internal enums.

Test: Ran CameraX Core app using camera_pipe option.
Test: ./gradlew :camera:camera-camera2-pipe-integration:compileDebugSources
Test: ./gradlew :camera:camera-camera2-pipe-integration:testDebugUnitTest
Test: ./gradlew :camera:camera-camera2-pipe-integration:connectedDebugAndroidTest
Test: ./gradlew :camera:camera-camera2-pipe-integration:lintDebug

Change-Id: Ia14d16d3cbb5c2d96cc139d218ffcf66ba4f2d64
llm_reason: The commit refactors multiple classes into adapters, reducing code duplication and improving modularity. It introduces dependency injection with Dagger, making testing easier and improving maintainability.

commit_message:
Introduce `SavedState` to abstract the data structure used to save and restore data in native platforms when using KMP.

* An opaque `SavedState` type that typealiases to a native type based on the platform.
* A `Reader` and `Writer` abstraction to modify the `SavedState` within Kotlin's common source sets. The abstraction utilizes a `value class` and all methods are `inline fun` to guarantee no overhead when using these methods to modify an `Bundle` on Android.

RelNote: "Introduce `SavedState` to abstract the data structure used to save and restore data in native platforms when using KMP. Includes a reader and writer used to mutate the `SavedState`."

commit_message:
Consolidate all failure messaging with Fact + Step in FailureMetadata

NOTE TO API COUNCIL: Please ignore this CL, API tracking is only enabled
for Kruth to compare compatibility with Google Truth, and this is an
unpublished library with no intention to publish.

A required change to support the public API that Google Truth uses to
allow users to build custom assertions on custom subjects AND still
allow Truth to provide a way to do soft asserts, including its custom
JUnit rules which allow test assertions against custom truth assertions.

This change consolidates all the possible methods of throwing an
AssertionError into one place from FailureMetadata that is overridable
via custom FailureStrategy, with the failure message generated by a List
of Fact, messagesToPrepend (existing Kruth placeholder) and "Step"s
(internal class supporting builder api for the assertion chain). The
builders around Step allows Truth to differentiate between root subject
and the child subject in more complex assertion chains (e.g., an
IterableSubject generated from the keys of a MapSubject).

Now that we have the same error messaging architecture as Truth, we can
also fix many of the placeholder messages so that they are exactly what
Truth would throw, which is important for compatibility with users who
use the Expect / ExpectFailure API to assert the error messages of
custom subjects, which may inherit or wrap existing subjects provided by
Kruth.

One bad thing is that this introduces a lot of wrapping classes around
messages and considering that Lazy delegation for message creation was
left out, there's a lot we can improve in terms of performance (inline
value class, lazy delegate, etc), but left this for a later CL.

One more thing that was not cleaned up as part of this change is the
numerous fail methods in Subject, which essentially all call into each
other and have varying levels of visibility to support inline reified
and either converting package-private Truth APIs to internal or
protected, since package-private doesn't exist in Kotlin.

This CL also removes data class from FailureMetadata, which is not
recommended anymore for public API due to binary compat guarantees.

Note: This depends on a small part of Guava for lenient String
formatting which was ported into KMP here.

Note: Many of the error messages are still not quite exactly correct,
but fixing them all in one change will make it too difficult to review
this, so left it for a later CL. To be clear this CL does not make
things worse, just doesn't finish fixing all the cases.

Note: Additional tests for Facts + Step depend on ExpectFailure which
has been split into a dependent CL for reviewability.

Test: ANDROIDX_PROJECTS=INFRAROGUE ./gradlew kruth:kruth:allTests -Pandroidx.enabled.kmp.target.platforms=+LINUX
Bug: 317418265
Relnote: "N/A"
Change-Id: Iac9151dad10997d2435545231007f03fa4673785
llm_reason: The commit refactors multiple classes to consolidate failure messaging into FailureMetadata, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability. It simplifies error handling by centralizing assertion failures and introducing a more consistent message formatting approach.

commit_message:
Introduce KN subscriptions (#2356)

* Introduce KN subscriptions

- Introduce new API `ApolloClient.subscribe` to execute subscriptions
- Replace `GraphQLRequest.kt` / `GraphQLResponse.kt` with new `ApolloRequest.kt` / `ApolloResponse.kt`
- Refactor `NetworkTransport` to execute `ApolloRequest` and return `ApolloResponse`
- Move `ApolloHttpNetworkTransport` and everything related to own package `http`

As side effect of this refactoring is that on iOS parsing now is done on background thread.

Part of https://github.com/apollographql/apollo-android/issues/2222

* Make `ApolloWebSocketNetworkTransportTest` to be common

* Introduce auto disconnect the socket connection after timeout

* Update default timeouts

* Remove garbage

* Fix test

commit_message:
SupportSQLiteDatabase wrapper of RoomDatabase

The API RoomDatabase.getSupportWrapper() will return a SupportSQLiteDatabase wrapper from a RoomDatabase even if the database is configured with a driver.

These are APIs are meant to be used as a migration path for codebases that extensively use SupportSQLite APIs with Room (via roomDatabase.openHelper.writableDatabase) but want to migrate to using SQLiteDriver APIs but are unable to completely refactor all their codebase.

Proper splitting and theming of Material surfaces and Inks

Big refactoring for Material and Inks and renaming to be in sync the latest
New material subpackages:
1) surface
2) ripple
3) clip (should be moved out of the material module later)

For Ripple:
1) Now we have RippleSurface - the surface where the ripples will be drawn.
2) RippleEffect - the base class for ripple animations.
3) RippleEffectFactory - the factory interface to create ripple effects like DefaultRippleEffect and all further implementations we would have.
4) RippleTheme - the holder for the current styles with an Ambient for getting this and a default style based on the MaterialTheme.
5) Ripple(old InkResponse) doesn't take onTap callbacks as a parameter anymore as we don't need it with our PointerInput system. The tap will be handled by a descendant gesture detector.
6) InkWill is now BoundedRipple

For Material:
1) Surface - the main class which represents a surface in Material Design. It is quite small component which just combines all the necessary logic like:
  a) SurfaceMeasureBox - reserves the space(simple measure box which just position the children in the left top corner and takes all the available space
  b) CachingClipper - helps to cache the clipper provided clip to not recreate it on every recomposing
  c) DrawShadow - draws a shadow for the surface (didn't work now if you provide not 0 elevation as it is not implemented on a graphic side) (there is an ambient for configuring the shadow color - CurrentShadowColor)
  d) ClipPath - clips the canvas with the surface's shape
  e) DrawColor - just fills the background into a provided color(use MaterialColors.surface by default)
  f) RippleSurface - a surface on top of which we draw ripple effects
  g) DrawBorder - draws a border for the provided shape
2) TransparentSurface - a simple composable to create a Surface without a color and elevation.
3) Card - a simple composable to create a Surface with a default surface background color from MaterialColors.

Bug: 114021677
Test: Ripple activity still works and now uses colors from the MaterialTheme
Change-Id: Ida531152ca8f0093799ddd6d8165500ab2190a4d
llm_reason: The commit refactors and renames multiple classes related to Material surfaces and Inks, improving code structure by splitting into subpackages and components. This reduces complexity and aligns with best practices for maintainability.

commit_message:
New processing test APIs

This CL updates the compilation testing infra and refactors the
internals.
The biggest change is that now there is an API to assert compilation
results rather than calling `runXXXforFailed` methods.
In this new API, the handler is responsible to make a failure assertion
if the compilation should fail, otherwise, test will expect compilation
to succeed and fail the test if the compilation fails.

As we add tests in Room, I'll add APIs to also assert generated code but
right now we don't have that use case hence not adding them (also, it is
not clear yet how it will work with kotlin compile testing).

To be able to collect diagnostics before it goes to the compilation
infra, I've moved XMessager to an abstract class (from interface) and
add ability to watch messages. Unfortunately, kotlin compile testing
does not provide an API to get messages with diagnostic types which is
why we have the abstraction on our end.

llm_reason: The commit refactors and simplifies the compilation testing infrastructure by introducing a new API for asserting compilation results, reducing code duplication across test runners. This improves maintainability and readability.

commit_message:
Load local SDKs using BaseDexClassLoader.

If available space more than 100MB, then SDK DEX files will
be extracted to "getCacheDir() / RuntimeEnabledSdk / <packageName>"
and loaded using BaseDexClassLoader.
If SDK DEX files were extracted during previous loading, then they will
be used even if available space less than 100MB.
If available space less than 100MB and SDK DEX files haven't extracted
during previous loading - SDK will be loaded using InMemorySdkClassLoader.

Main components:
1. LocalSdkFolderProvider - create folder for SDK in CacheDir and remove
content after Application update.
2. CachedLocalSdkStorage - extract SDK DEX files to folder provided by
LocalSdkFolderProvider.
3. FileClassLoaderFactory - Trying to create BaseDexClassLoader using
DEX files from CachedLocalSdkStorage

Bug: 260560987
Test: SdkLoaderTest
Test: FileClassLoaderFactoryTest, CachedLocalSdkStorageTest, LocalSdkFolderProviderTest
Change-Id: I3add08cf706337b888a64bac8bf5096e3f63635d
llm_reason: The changes introduce a mechanism to extract and manage SDK DEX files based on available space, reducing unnecessary storage usage by only extracting when sufficient space is available.

commit_message:
Adds viewport listener and coordinate translation APIs

Optimizes PageLayoutManager to compute certain data that's
derived from the current viewport, namely the visible regions
of visible pages and each visible page's location in view,
only once each time the viewport changes.

Makes visible pages / fully visible pages normal properties
in lieu of observable flows. There's no use-case for observing
these values, only reading their current value. Computing
visible pages and page locations synchronously and directly
when the viewport (scroll, zoom, size) changes enables real-
time dispatching of pagination information to listeners.

Shores up test coverage of PageLayoutManager.

Adds a few clarifying comments related to "view" vs "content"
coordinates and what each means throughout PdfView.

This not only maintains the fast scroll performance
optimizations from aosp/3511737 but improves upon them.

Bug: 389788074
Bug: 389788285
Test: manual, ./gradlew :pdf:pdf-viewer:(test|cAT)
Change-Id: If31ad7a77f3dad98574537e6c3866dc2891ca22f
llm_reason: The changes optimize PageLayoutManager to compute visible regions and page locations synchronously, reducing redundant calculations and improving real-time performance. This addresses potential inefficiencies in previous asynchronous operations.

Calculate the maximum supported frame rate during the StreamSpec calculation and introduce simulateAddUseCase()

 Calculate the maximum supported fps during the UseCase binding flow ensures consistent calculation within the existing complex process, including the consideration for StreamSharing.

commit_message:
Add support for scam protection (#5853)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/0/1209879813659870 

### Description

### Steps to test this PR

_Feature 1_
- [ ] Fresh install the app
- [ ] Using the app inspector, check that only phishing and malware
datasets are present. Data needs to be downloaded, so it might take a
few seconds to complete. The easiest way is to check the `revisions`
table in `malicious_sites` db
- [ ] Open
https://privacy-test-pages.site/security/badware/phishing.html and check
it's blocked
- [ ] Open https://privacy-test-pages.site/security/badware/malware.html
and check it's blocked
- [ ] Open https://privacy-test-pages.site/security/badware/scam.html
and check it's not blocked

_Feature 2_
- [ ] Set `@Toggle.DefaultValue(true)` for `scamProtection`
- [ ] Fresh install the app
- [ ] Using the app inspector, check that phishing, malware and scam
datasets are present. Data needs to be downloaded, so it might take a
few seconds to complete. The easiest way is to check the `revisions`
table in `malicious_sites` db
- [ ] Open
https://privacy-test-pages.site/security/badware/phishing.html and check
it's blocked
- [ ] Open https://privacy-test-pages.site/security/badware/malware.html
and check it's blocked
- [ ] Open https://privacy-test-pages.site/security/badware/scam.html
and check it's blocked
- [ ] Check `m_malicious-site-protection_error-page-shown` is fired with
`category=scam`
- [ ] Accept risk and visit site
- [ ] Check `m_malicious-site-protection_visit-site` is fired with
`category=scam`
- [ ] Click the omnibar icon
- [ ] Check privacy dashboard is shown normally
- [ ] Disable `scamProtection` under Feature Flag Inventory and restart
the app
- [ ] Open https://privacy-test-pages.site/security/badware/scam.html
and check it's not blocked

commit_message:
ListenableFuture API Overhaul

Create new LisenableFuture<> based API underlying callback loading APIs.

 - Simplifies async initialization - PagedLists with async initial
   loads no longer start empty

 - Lays groundwork for cancellation

 - Enables other async APIs in the future

 - No longer need to disable placeholders to disable
   PositionalDataSource tiling, due to late PagedList creation.

 - DataSource#getExecutor is pretty gross, but lets us build callback
   impls on top of Listenable*DataSource APIs without adding them to
   Params. Problem is you can't call ListenableFuture based load
   methods on callback when a PagedList isn't used without crashing
   (e.g. in tests).

 - Refresh in LivePagedListBuilder currently uses private APIs

 - Can't yet use 'Result' types with callbacks - needed once we add
   more functionality.

 - Tiled (non-contiguous) loading was removed, will be added back in a
   follow-up

 - RxPagedListBuilder support for new refresh system will be in a
   follow-up

Test: ./gradlew :paging:paging-common:test :paging:paging-common-ktx:test :paging:paging-runtime:cC :paging:paging-runtime-ktx:cC :paging:
paging-rxjava:test :paging:paging-rxjava-ktx:test
Fixes: 110843692

Change-Id: Ied0f1da732a1527f9fef524e37889146084acb7b
llm_reason: The commit refactors the code to use ListenableFuture-based APIs, simplifying async initialization and enabling future extensibility. It removes deprecated methods and consolidates data source implementations, reducing technical debt.

commit_message:
Supports switching between date picker and input

- Removed the public `DateInput` and consolidated its content to apper
  as a mode for `DatePicker`. A developer can still start the
  `DatePicker` in an input mode by setting an initial mode state on the
  `DatePickerState`.
- Added a `DisplayMode` to the state for representing the current
  input/picker mode that the UI is displaying.
- Updates to the API with a `showModeToggle` to allow switching from
  DateInput to DatePicker. By default, this is set to `true`.
- Internal changes to support the picker/input content switch motion.
  Refactor the composables to have a wrapping `DateEntryContainer` that
  holds the `title` and `headline`, and wraps the date input/picker
  bottom content.
- Rename the `datePickerState` parameters to just `state`.
- Add content description to announce "Today" when selecting the current
  day at the DatePicker.

Bug: 245821760
Bug: 245822134
Fixes: 266614325
Test: DatePickerTest, DateInputTest and screenshot tests
RelNote: "Add support for switching between date picker and date input
modes. A date input is now a display mode for the DatePicker."

Change-Id: Ieeff707da21566b0b8d4fe365662ce6ae2a6acd1
llm_reason: The changes refactor the DatePicker and DateInput components to reduce code duplication by consolidating their logic into a shared state model. This improves maintainability and simplifies future enhancements.

commit_message:
Refactor BringIntoViewResponder to internalize request propagation.

- BringIntoViewParent interface is introduced to abstract parent
  responder modifiers and platform-specific integrations.
- BringRectangleOnScreen is removed, replaced with a single composable
  function to return the default BringIntoViewParent.
- ModifierLocal used to specify responders is made internal.
- BringIntoViewResponder interface is now a single-method fun interface
  that is only responsible for scrolling the current node, not
  propagating the request to the next parent.
- New bringIntoViewResponder modifier function added to add a
  BringIntoViewResponder into the modifier context and automatically
  wire up request propagation.
- Added more non-screenshot test cases.
- Added demo for using inside nested scrollables.

Test: ./gradlew :compose:f:f:cDAT
Relnote: "BringIntoViewResponders are no longer required to manually
    pass requests up to parent responders, and are instead required to
    immediately return the rectangle they want their parent to bring
    into view."
Change-Id: I8e66acfde483dd1a3b67dbf37cc815967eae746a
llm_reason: The commit refactors BringIntoViewResponder to internalize request propagation, reducing code complexity by introducing an interface and simplifying method responsibilities. This improves maintainability and adheres to single responsibility principles.

commit_message:
Make terminal separator behavior configurable

Adds an enum allowing a choice between waiting for both PagingSource and
RemoteMediator to resolve, or to simply await for PagingSource to
resolve allowing the use-case where initial load can immediately prepend
a header without requiring the user to scroll to see it even when using
RemoteMediator.

Relnote: "Allow configuration of terminal separator (header / footer)
  behavior with an additional optional SeparatorType parameter. The two
  options are:
  * FULLY_COMPLETE - existing behavior; wait for both PagingSource and
  RemoteMediator to mark endOfPaginationReached before adding terminal
  separators. If RemoteMediator is not used, remote loadState is
  ignored. This is primarily useful if you only want to show section
  separators when the section is fully loaded, including fetching from
  remote source e.g., network.
  * SOURCE_COMPLETE - only wait for PagingSource to mark
  endOfPaginationReached even if RemoteMediator is used. This allows
  headers and footers to be presented synchronously with the initial
  load, which prevents users from needing to scroll to see terminal
  separators."
Test: ./gradlew paging:paging-common:test
Fixes: 174700218
Change-Id: Ibe9938d069e66d0deae806dc44684f3a05e651a0
llm_reason: The changes refactor the code to make terminal separator behavior configurable by introducing an enum and modifying functions to accept this parameter. This reduces complexity by allowing different behaviors without duplicating code.

commit_message:
Defer dispatching coroutines resumed during animation callbacks in UI tests.

This is a workaround for the fact that we use an unconfined dispatcher
in UI tests (b/254115946), which prevents us from being able to perform
layout passes for frames in some cases (b/222093277).

TestMonotonicFrameClock gets a continuation interceptor which wraps continuations with
behavior that will usually no-op, but if the continuation is resumed
while frame callbacks are running, it will defer resuming the
underlying continuation until all the frame callbacks have finished.
This matches how continuations are dispatched in non-test code, since
that code does not use an unconfined dispatcher.

In order to preserve test delay skipping, the interceptor implements
Delay and delegates it to the underlying dispatcher, just like
ApplyingContinuationInterceptor is already doing.

This CL also adds a callback that runs after each frame in
TestMonotonicFrameClock. This change was originally in aosp/2121435.

This allows us to perform measure and layout after each frame if
necessary, which aligns the measure/layout timing with production.

Bug: b/254115946
Bug: b/222093277
Bug: b/255802670
Test: PhaseOrderingTest, TestMonotonicFrameClockTest
Relnote: "In UI tests using a Compose rule, continuations resumed during
    `withFrameNanos` callbacks will not be dispatched until after all
    frame callbacks have finished running. This matches the behavior of
    compose when running normally. However, tests that rely on the old
    behavior may fail. This should only affect code that calls
    `withFrameNanos` or `withFrameMillis` directly, and has logic
    outside of callback passed to those functions that may need to be
    moved inside the callbacks. See the animation test changes in this
    CL for examples."
Relnote: "Added optional `onPerformTraversals: (Long) -> Unit` parameter to
    `TestMonotonicFrameClock` constructor and factory function to run
    code after `withFrameNanos` callbacks but before resuming callers'
    coroutines."
Change-Id: Idb41309445a030c91e8e4ae05daa9642b450505c
llm_reason: The changes address issues with coroutine dispatching during animation callbacks in UI tests, ensuring that continuations are deferred until after frame processing. This prevents race conditions and ensures proper execution order.

commit_message:
Make XType extend XAnnotated to support type annotations

This is to make XProcessing support type/type-use annotations, like
`@A` here:

```
class Subject : @A Base
```

On JavaAP with Kotlin sources/classes, we're relying on Kotlin Metadata and kotlinx-metadata-jvm since KAPT doesn't emit type annotations. Two new XProcessing types, `JavacKmAnnotation` and `JavacKmAnnotationValue`, are created to wrap `KmAnnotation` and `KmAnnotationArgument` to
support this.

There're some limitations in this CL. First, we're not able to find
type annotations from precompiled Java classes[1]. Second, on KSP
side we support type annotations on supertypes only[2]. We'll follow up to make type annotations support more complete.

[1] https://github.com/google/ksp/issues/1296
[2] https://github.com/google/ksp/issues/1325

Test: XAnnotationTest.kt, XAnnotationValueTest.kt, XTypeElementTest.kt,
XTypeTest.kt

Change-Id: Ie0896beb3f14912289b6fd40fcf7ea6e8dd0c020
llm_reason: The changes primarily involve refactoring to support type annotations by extending XType and modifying related classes. There's no indication of introducing or fixing code smells like energy, concurrency, storage bloat, security vulnerabilities, or high complexity debt.

### Description

Adds a new tracker animation tile to the tab switcher behind a feature
flag that displays the number of trackers blocked in the last 7 days.
The tile appears at the top of the tab list and includes an animated
shield icon with a counter that animates up to show the total number of
trackers blocked.

The tracker count is currently a random number. A future PR will add the

- [x] Verify shield animation plays correctly

commit_message:
Adopt common Drag&Drop API for multiplatform

This change is targeting to unblock implementations of Drag&Drop APIs on platforms where transfer session might be initiated only by the system itself (iOS, Web).

To do that `dragAndDropSource` now implements default start detector that not only allows delegating it to the system, but also simplifies the usage of it. Specifying custom behavior is still possible by passing a new `detectDragStart` parameter (not available on some platforms). Inside the scope of this detected there is an ability to request starting the transfer.

In addition of entry point changes in `compose:foundation`, there are important `compose:ui` API changes:
- Requesting of start transaction now limited by callback scope.
- Splitting `DragAndDropModifierNode` into `DragAndDropSourceModifierNode` and `DragAndDropTargetModifierNode`
- Removing from `DragAndDrop*ModifierNode` APIs that shouldn't be exposed outside of ui module

Also these changes fix a few scenarios (even on Android):
- Cleanup `interestedTargets` (b/326945380)
- `DragAndDropNode.contains` check used incorrect size in case of applied padding modifier

Relnote: `Modifier.dragAndDropSource` was adopted to multiplatform - start detection is performed by Compose itself (or delegated to system in case of Web or iOS), so callback is only used for obtain a transfer data
Relnote: `DragAndDropModifierNode` is deprecated in favor to `DragAndDropSourceModifierNode` and `DragAndDropTargetModifierNode`
Fixes: 326945380
Test: existing tests + manually
Change-Id: I0389dd1406de633206650eec717d1ae778734911
llm_reason: The changes refactor and simplify drag-and-drop APIs by splitting nodes into source and target modifiers, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability.

commit_message:
ScatterSet, a fast, allocation-free hash set

Relnote: "ScatterSet and MutableScatterSet have
Set-like and MutableSet-like APIs, repectively,
and offer reduced allocations."

MutableScatterSet copies ScatterMap's implementation,
but without values. MutableScatterSet is a container
with a MutableSet-like interface based on a flat
hash table implementation. The underlying
implementation is designed to avoid all allocations
on insertion, removal, retrieval, and iteration.
Allocations may still happen on insertion when
the underlying storage needs to grow to accommodate
newly added elements to the set.

Benchmarks running on a Pixel Fold:
MutableScatterSet
16,812   ns           3 allocs insert[size=1,000]
 7,283   ns           0 allocs remove[size=1,000]
 5,511   ns           0 allocs forEach[size=1,000]

HashSet
32,384   ns        1004 allocs insert[size=1,000]
 7,298   ns           0 allocs remove[size=1,000]
12,485   ns           1 allocs forEach[size=1,000]

LinkedHashSet (implementation for mutableSetOf)
49,714   ns        1003 allocs insert[size=1,000]
 8,784   ns           0 allocs remove[size=1,000]
 5,662   ns           1 allocs forEach[size=1,000]

Test: new test and benchmark
Change-Id: Ieea4ec844346a7f595c90f9237f214a8d7af3888
llm_reason: The commit introduces a new data structure, ScatterSet, which is optimized for allocation-free operations. The benchmarks show significant reductions in allocations and improved performance compared to existing implementations like HashSet and LinkedHashSet. This directly impacts CPU efficiency by minimizing garbage collection overhead.

commit_message:
Path API improvements

This change is a series of improvement to path APIs, in large part as
foundational work for upcoming changes to Cubic/PathEasing:

- Add the ability to set the winding direction when adding shapes to
  a Path (addOval(), addRect() and addRoundRect()). The winding
  direction is necessary to create complex shapes with holes. For
  instance a donut is made of two concentric circles each with a
  different winding.

- Deprecate quadraticBezierTo() and relativeQuadraticBezierTo() in
  favor of quadraticTo() and relativeQuadraticTo(). In addition to
  being shorter names, these APIs are also more consistent with
  cubicTo() and relativeCubicTo().

- Add easier APIs for Path.op:
  - Union: p1 + p2
  - Union: p1 or p2
  - Intersection: p1 and p2
  - Difference: p1 - p2
  - Xor: p1 xor p2

- Add APIs to iterate over the segments of a Path using Path.iterate().
  The API is modeled after androidx-graphics:graphics-path. A key
  component of the API is the ability, turned on by default, to convert
  conic path segments into quadratic path segments.
  The new PathIterator API provides the ability to iterate over the
  segments in two distinct ways:
  - The "OOP" way with allocations, by returning a new PathSegment
    instance for every segment.
  - The "graphics" way by passing a float array that gets filled with
    the data required to describe the path segment.
  The first API is useful for readability/ease of use, while the second
  one is necessary for performance/to avoid allocations.
  To make this feature possible across all supported API levels, the
  compose-ui-graphics module now depends on graphics-path.

Note: this CL provides a Skiko implementation of path iteration albeit
with a limitation. The conics to quadratics part of the implementation
is missing because Skiko currently contains a bug preventing this
feature from being implemented properly. See the issue on GitHub for
more details: https://github.com/JetBrains/skiko/issues/820

This CL also does a bit of cleanup throughout related code.

Test: PathIteratorTest
RelNote: Added Path.iterate() to iterate over Path segments
RelNote: Use Path.quadraticTo() instead of Path.quadraticBezierTo()
RelNote: You can now set the winding direction when calling Path.addOval(), addRect(), and addRoundRect()
Change-Id: If19bc174d2649ef9024943517395ccb0ef333f61
llm_reason: The commit refactors and deprecates several methods to improve API consistency, such as replacing quadraticBezierTo with quadraticTo for better alignment with cubicTo. It also introduces new Path.iterate() APIs for iterating over path segments, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability.

commit_message:
Create a passthrough connection pool for drivers with internal connection pool.

Instead of special handling the AndroidSQLiteDriver and the SupportSQLiteDriver consolidate these two and any other driver that has an internal connection pool reported via the new API SQLiteDriver.hasConnectionPool to use a passthrough connection pool that does not do any connection management.

This also enables driver wrappers of AndroidSQLiteDriver as otherwise the instance check would fail leading Room to use the incorrect pool.

RoomSupportSQLite wrapper was also updated to provide compatibility for Room databases with drivers that have an internal pool, such as the AndroidSQLiteDriver.

Note that AndroidSQLiteConnection must be kept for backwards compatibility but was refactored to prepare statements of type SupportSQLiteStatement, while AndroidSQLiteStatement was merged with SupportSQLiteStatement and avoid repeated code. Moreover. in order to properly support the passthrough pool the driver handles transaction SQL statements directly.

Bug: 408010324
Test: Existing
Relnote: "Add an API to SQLiteDriver to report if internally it has a connection pool or not."
Change-Id: I52a5148dfeab4e576b613bb9d25f725d0c641a8e
llm_reason: The changes consolidate and simplify connection pool management by introducing a passthrough pool for drivers with internal pools, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability.

commit_message:
New AppBuildConfig instead of BuildConfig statics (#1643)


Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/414730916066338/1201468074269629/f

### Description
* The autogenerated `BuildConfig` file contains information about the build that we often use in our logic
* but only the `BuildConfig` in the `:app` module contains ALL information
* `BildConfig` generated in library gradle modules contain a small subset of all information
  * eg. there is no `VERSION_NAME`, `FLAVOR` etc
* As we have more a more gradle modules, more often we need to get access to this information from them
* And we're forced to create classes in the `:app` module just to get that information, eg. `AppVersionCollector`

This PR:
* creates a new `:appbuildconfig-api` that contains the `AppBuildConfig` interface with all relevant information about the build
* the interface will be implemented in the `:app` module by the `RealAppBuildConfig`
* all other gradle modules can then just depend on the `:appbuildconfig-api` and request the `AppBuildConfig` whenever they want to access to build information

### Steps to test this PR
* smoke tests for browser and AppTP
* all tests shall pass
llm_reason: The commit replaces references to a custom AppBuildConfig with BuildConfig, reducing code duplication and centralizing build information management. This refactoring simplifies dependencies and improves maintainability.

commit_message:
Do not track remote states locally within PageFetcher for PageEvents

Previously from fetcher side, we locally tracked remote load states
per-generation to inject them into the page event stream, however this
leads to issues between generations, where the local state is incorrect
before it receives an event from either the source or remote stream.

This CL changes .injectRemoteEvents into a custom .combine operator so
that we always wait for a real event from both remote and source flows,
so that we are guaranteed to have valid states for both. We also now
immediately send REFRESH=Loading from source so that it is less likely
to drop remote events due to waiting on source flow from .combine.

The reason we use a custom .combine operator is to remove the "batching"
logic in .combine, which would normally output the latest values from
each Flow and can miss emissiosn when we get multiple emissions on the
same Flow, as opposed to alternating emissions.

Since it also affected some tests, this CL also fixes a longstanding
issue with cancellation on invalidation. Previously, a collector
would continue to receive remote events after invalidation, which
can make it difficult for Java users or those not using collectLatest to
switch to the next generation, since submitData would never return until
cancelled. We now eagerly cancel collection on the remote stream (within
a generation) to close the Flow<PageEvent> from a PagingData as soon as
we receive a new generation.

RelNote: "LoadStates from Paging now await valid values from both
  PagingSource and RemoteMediator before emitting downstream between
  generations. This prevents new generations of PagingData from sending
  NotLoading in CombinedLoadStates.source.refresh if it was already
  Loading; new generations of PagingData will now always correctly begin
  with Loading for refresh state instead of first resetting to NotLoading
  incorrectly in some cases.

  Cancellation on past generations now happen eagerly on invalidation /
  new generations. It should no longer be required to use .collectLatest
  on Flow<PagingData>, although it is still highly recommended to do so."

Fixes: 177351336
Fixes: 195028524
Test: ./gradlew paging:paging-common:test
Change-Id: I0b2b5e0120e2e3a677f65d40d3955a9bbbe2e1a9
llm_reason: The changes address issues with concurrent state management and event emission between generations, ensuring that load states are correctly tracked without race conditions. The removal of batching logic and custom combine operators helps prevent missed emissions and ensures valid states from both source and remote flows.

commit_message:
Define ChangeList to manage pending changes

This CL is the first in a multi-step refactor of how operations are
tracked by the Composer. Currently, we allocate a lambda for each
action we want to execute when running the changes, which can lead to
alloc/GC churn. This change introduces an `OpStack` type, which can
track a bespoke set of `Operation`s and their corresponding arguments.

We utilize three arrays here (one for the operations, one for `int`
arguments, and one for all other arguments), which helps us reduce
allocations and prevent autoboxing.

As an added bonus, the ChangeList can be formatted as a string to
reveal what operations are pending, which can be a big help for
debugging.

This CL just attempts to introduce the data structure changes. As an
intermediate step, we're using a `BackwardsCompatOp` that takes in a
lambda and behaves exactly the same as the previous behavior of
allocating lambdas. This operation will be removed by the end of the
refactor sequence.

Test: OpStackTest, ./gradlew :compose:runtime:runtime:tDUT
Change-Id: I70eabbddb874c24c926a05ea624f566ff878922a
llm_reason: The changes replace a lambda-based approach with an OpStack using arrays to reduce allocations and prevent autoboxing, which should lower GC overhead and improve performance.

commit_message:
Safe by default shell commands

Fixes: 255402908
Bug: 253094958
Test: Tests in benchmark-common, benchmark-macro, benchmark-junit4 # API 23 26 31 (emulators) 33 (device)

Require caller to specify if they want stdout or stderr, and validate
empty if they are not used.

This will incur a performance penalty for every command changed in
this way, but gives us much greater safety in avoiding ignored shell
commands leaving the test/device in an undefined state. By default,
this was a significant regression in shell execution, so selectively
used unsafe variant, and optimized the script to avoid a chmod and
multiple kotlin-side file deletes.

This also fixes the script command to capture all stderr, instead of
that from just the last command.

For simplicity, removed argument support from scripts, and hard code
perfetto port. This could be fixed by adding back arg support if
needed.

Relnote: "Improved safety of all internal shell commands by validating
all output/errors."

commit_message:
Introduce onFirstVisible and onVisbilityChanged modifiers

Relnote:"""
Modifier.onFirstVisible and Modifier.onVisibilityChanged modifiers were introduced, which are high level modifiers built on top of Modifier.onLayoutRectChanged. These modifiers are built specifically to handle a lot of common application requirements such as logging impressions, auto playing videos, etc. These have been built with performance in mind so that they can be used in critical list-based scenarios without risk of sacrificing scroll performance.

In addition to these modifier APIs, additional APIs to RelativeLayoutBounds have been added in order to support these use cases, as well as make it easier for developers to easily create similar custom modifiers that suit their use case exactly.
"""

Change-Id: I759b8256fd7797ad9a10a955368231b70470dd7c
llm_reason: The changes introduce new modifiers onFirstVisible and onVisibilityChanged, which are optimized for performance in list-based scenarios. They use throttling and debouncing to prevent excessive calculations, reducing CPU usage during scrolling.

commit_message:
Refactoring focus search and fixing some bugs

This cleanup is in preparation to implement a focus cluster.
Focus Cluster will be added as a new foundation API in a separate CL.

When we move focus among children of a deactivated node, we treat the
nodes as if they are children of the first non-deactivated ancestor.
Instead of doing that, we should treat children of a deactivated node
as siblings and move focus among them before visiting other children
of the non-deactivated ancestor.

I also found a bug in tests that use containsExactly(). The assertion
only checks if all the values are present, but does not check if the
values occur in the specified order. We need to use
containsExactly().inOrder() to assert that the items occur in the
expected order. I fixed the incorrect tests and added a test util
function to prevent this kind of error from happening in the future.

Bug: 213508274
Test: /gradlew compose:ui:ui:connectedCheck -P android.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=androidx.compose.ui.focus.OneDimensionalFocusSearchNextTest
Test: /gradlew compose:ui:ui:connectedCheck -P android.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=androidx.compose.ui.focus.OneDimensionalFocusSearchPreviousTest
Test: /gradlew compose:ui:ui:connectedCheck -P android.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=androidx.compose.ui.focus.TwoDimensionalFocusTraversalTest
Change-Id: I68f1d0d78d00567b9719e752fa4b9c1c4ec26386

commit_message:
Add FakeImageSources and simulation functions for testing

This change introduces a dedicated provider interface for creating
ImageSource(s) in CameraPipe, which allows for CameraGraphs to:

- Simulate fake images during tests
- Simulate fake images for all requests during tests
- Check that all simulated images have been closed
- Check that all fake ImageReaders and ImageSources have been closed
- Inspect all CameraGraphs that have been created via a
  CameraPipeSimulator interface.

This change also causes CameraGraphSimulator and CameraPipeSimulator
to extend their respective interfaces by delegation, which ensures
that the underlying behavior remains in sync with the real CameraPipe
and CameraGraph implementations.

Test: ./gradlew\
      :camera:camera-camera2-pipe:testDebugUnitTest\
      :camera:camera-camera2-pipe-testing:testDebugUnitTest\
      :camera:camera-camera2-pipe-integration:testDebugUnitTest

llm_reason: The changes refactor the CameraGraphSimulator and related classes to improve testability and reduce code duplication. commit_message:
load privacy plugins data into memory just for main process (#4189)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1206608309382832/f

### Description
Many privacy plugins data is loaded into memory for both `:app` and `:vpn` processes.
Even when the plugins are only used from the app process, because privacy config is only downloaded from the `main` process, the loading mistakenly happens in the `vpn` process as well because
all plugins have a `loadToMemory` method that is executed in the `init` block (ie. when the instance is created) and the `PluginPoint<MainProcessLifecycleObserver>` is a dependency to the `Application` instance,
which is created for both `vpn` and `app` processes.

However loading into data for the `vpn` process should not be required/done as we don't use that data from the `vpn` process.

This task is a stop gap to avoid that in-memory data loading to happen. 

In sub-sequent PRs we'll want to have a better nad more standardize mechanism to perform in-memory Cache backed by disk storage.


### Steps to test this PR
Code review and smoke test the app
Make sure privacy config downloads and sets correctly.
llm_reason: The changes remove conditional checks for `isMainProcess`, ensuring that data loading into memory happens only once per process, thus optimizing resource usage and reducing unnecessary initializations.

commit_message:
Change how compiler features are rolled out

This change introduces a new mechnism to roll out compiler
features that are intended to be made the eventual default
behavior. Instead of introducing a flag that can be confused
with configuration option will always be options, all such
features are now enabled or disabled through "featureFlag".

Relnote: """Changed how features being rolled out are
enabled and disabled. Features, such as strong skipping and
non-skipping group optimizations are now enabled through the
"featureFlag" option instead of their own option.

A feature with a feature flag start off initially disabled
and will eventually become enabled by default.

For example, to enable strong skipping use:

  -P plugin:androidx.compose.compiler.plugins.kotlin:featureFlag=StrongSkipping

To enable the non-skipping groups optimization use:

  -P plugin:androidx.compose.compiler.plugins.kotlin:featureFlag=OptimizeNonSkippingGroups

the options strongSkipping, experimentialStrongSkipping,
and nonSkippingGroupOptimization are now all depricated
and will report an warning if used but are still honored.

A feature flag can be removed if its name is prefixed
with a "-". For example:

  -P plugin:androidx.compose.compiler.plugins.kotlin:featureFlag=-StrongSkipping

will disable strong skipping.

Using a feature that is unknown to the plugin will
report a warning.

If a feature is enabled that is enabled by default or
disabled and is disabled by default, a warning will be
issued.
"""

Test: ./gradlew :compose:c:c-h:i-t:check
Change-Id: Iab20b3f0e5e818a98f7952bbe1e1ebbd2b8251c7
llm_reason: The changes refactor feature flags handling by replacing individual options with a unified 'featureFlag' option, reducing code complexity and improving maintainability.

commit_message:
Introduce placeable.placeWithLayer

We decided to integrate functionality currently available as DrawLayerModifier right inside the Layout system.
Why to do so:
1) To allow custom layouts to insert the layers for the children if they need to. For example It is needed in LazyColumn as an optimization (b/170296989)
2) Allows to simplify the system by having less concepts. Now DrawLayerModifier is a whole separate concept. With integrating layers right inside the layout system Modifier.drawLayer() will be implemented just as a simple LayoutModifier with DrawLayerModifier class being removed. We already did the same thing with ZIndexModifier (b/171493718)

Relnote: New methods placeable.placeWithLayer() and placeable.placeRelativeWithLayer() were added which allows custom layouts and layout modifiers to place a child with introducing a graphics layer for their drawing. Having that we can first optimize redrawings, so when we need to move a child we don't have to redraw its content, second we can apply draw transformations for a child
Bug: 173030831
Test: New tests, existing tests are passing
Change-Id: Ibd8f6bb03f391c3f1bbf13f659d79e3b57019a46
llm_reason: The changes refactor the code to integrate DrawLayerModifier into the Layout system, reducing the number of concepts and simplifying the architecture. This eliminates the need for separate modifiers and introduces new methods placeWithLayer and placeRelativeWithLayer, making the code more maintainable and easier to understand.

commit_message:
Room KMP Plugin Support

Add support for multiplatform KSP tasks to be configured by the Room Gradle Plugin for exporting schemas.

The code is refactored for the plugin to use 'integrations' that allow the plugin to configure various combinations of supported porjects, such as Android + Java only, Android + Kotlin, Kotlin Multiplatform (with or without Android targets), etc. This is done by using plugin ids to perform configuration and only accessing plugin classes (AGP or KGP) once the plugins have been found, an indicator that the plugin classes are in the plugin classpath.

For KMP projects, the Android target will still be configured through AGP's variant APIs as those better reflect the name of the source sets. For other Kotlin targets, then the KSP task is found with the same method as KSP's own Gradle Plugin, by inspecting the configured Kotlin targets, their compilation tasks and KSP side task.

In terms of APIs, a Kotlin target is now an acceptable 'match name' for configuring schema locations, i.e. `schemaLocation("native", ...)`. To improve the experience on KMP projects, per-variant schema locations will accept an 'android' prefix to align with the target name usage, i.e. `schemaLocation("androidDemoDebug", ...)`.

Lastly, the Room extension will now contain `generateKotlin` to enable / disable Kotlin code generation through the plugin.

Bug: 310920612
Bug: 317909626
Test: RoomKmpGradlePluginTest
Change-Id: Idcbcc5d47d77a9badc8d636d8e1fcbfe0cec7d2e
llm_reason: The commit refactors code to support multiplatform projects by introducing integrations, reducing duplication and improving modularity. It simplifies variant matching logic and enhances maintainability.

commit_message:
First Version of the Wear Material Scaffold component.

The Scaffold provides automatic handling of PositionIndicator (such as scroll position or volume setting) which is based on state and styling provided through the ScaffoldState. This allows the developer to tell the scaffold which type of indicator to show for the main application content. The PositionIndicator is located at the right center of the screen and is either curved or straight depending on the watch type. The Scaffold also overlays a Vignette to allow for blurring of the screen edges when there is scrollable main content.

The Scaffold also provides a slot for the developer to provide a TimeText (component will follow in another patch) that allows for displaying the time and application status in an area at the top of the watch. The TimeText component will be watch shape aware.

In order to be multi-platform capable an indirection via a new Resources.kt file is provided to enable the Vignette images to be loaded. Thanks to Louis (lpf@) and Jim (jsproch@) for advice on how to achieve this.

Thanks to ssancho@ for the original version of the PositionIndicator.kt code

Test: androidx.wear.compose.material.ScaffoldTest,androidx.wear.compose.material.PositionIndicatorTest
Bug: 196387222
RelNote: "We have added Scaffold a top level application composable that provides a structure for handling PositionIndicators (like Scroll or Volume) position, an area at the top of the screen for displaying the time and application status, and also supports a Vignette to blur the screen top and bottom edge for scrollable content. The main area of the Scaffold is where the application contents are placed."
Change-Id: I5e0bf5d3b7fe6ebc247a8b9ba255b00167fe35c4
llm_reason: The commit introduces a new feature, the Wear Material Scaffold component, which provides structure for handling PositionIndicators and Vignettes. The changes include adding new classes, tests, and resources without introducing any code smells.
