commit_message:
Create the feature flag code generator (#2818)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1203898052213031/f

### Description
See [asana task](https://app.asana.com/0/1202552961248957/1203898052213029/f) for full background.

The PR adds a code generator for (remote) feature flags. 

For instance, the following remote feature:

```json
{
  "myFeatureName": {
    "exceptions": [...],
    "state": "enabled",
    "settings": {...},
    "features": {
      "remoteFeatureName": {
        "state": "disabled"
      }
    }
    "hash": "..."
  }
}
```

can be represented with the following code:

```kotlin
@ContributesRemoteFeature(
    scope = AppScope::class,
    featureName = "name",
//    settingsStore = MyFeatureFeatureSettingStore::class,
//    exceptionsStore = MyFeatureFeatureExceptionStore::class,
)
class MyRemoteFeature {
  @Toggle.DefaultValue(false)
  fun self(): Toggle

  @Toggle.DefaultValue(false)
  fun remoteFeatureName(): Toggle
}

class MyFeatureFeatureToggleStore : Toggle.Store {...}

class MyFeatureFeatureSettingStore : RemoteFeatureSettingsStore {...}

class MyFeatureFeatureExceptionStore : RemoteFeatureExceptionStore {...}

```

Where:
* the `MyRemoteFeature` will be the class used to check the state of the flags
* the `MyRemoteFeature` shall be annotated with `FeatureName` that will specify the name of the global feature
* the `MyRemoteFeature` shall be annotated with `ContributesRemoteFeature` for the code to be generated and shall specify
  * Dagger scope
  * `settingsStore` class that shall implement `RemoteFeatureSettingsStore` interface (Optional)
  * `exceptionStore` class that shall implement `RemoteFeatureExceptionStore` interface (Optional)
  * _NOTE: settings and exceptions store are optional. If not provided, neither will be stored_
* all methods in `MyRemoteFeature` shall return `Toggle`
* the `MyRemoteFeature` class shall contain one method called `self()`
  * this method will return the state of the global `featureName`
* the `MyRemoteFeature` may content other methods, dubbed after the sub-feature name that will also return `Toggle`


The codegen adds validation checks to ensure the correctness of the feature interfaces

After, to use the feature one can do the following:

```kotlin
private val feature: MyRemoteFeature

feature.self().isEnabled()
feature.self().setEnabled(state)

feature.remoteFeatureName().isEnabled()
feature.remoteFeatureName().setEnabled(state)
```

### How does this work?
The magic is done in two steps:
* `ContributesRemoteFeatureCodeGenerator` class is triggered by interfaces annotated with `ContributesRemoteFeature` that represent the feature. 
  * generates and contributes a `PrivacyFeaturePlugin` that will do the proper parsing for the feature
  * generates a dynamic proxy for the feature flag interface. This proxy intercepts all interface function calls and then checks the state of the toggles in the `Toggle.Store`
* finally, the proxy implementation leaves in `FeatureToggles` class

### Steps to test this PR

_Test App/Autofill_
- [x] smoke test the app and autofill to ensure nothing changed
llm_reason: The commit introduces a new code generator for feature flags, which simplifies the creation of feature toggle interfaces by automatically generating boilerplate code. This reduces redundancy and improves maintainability.

commit_message:
Animations optimizations

- In VectorizedAnimationSpec, use fastCoerceIn() to save 66
  instructions and 7 branches (100->34)
- In ArcSpline:
  - ArcSpline.init(), save 11 instructions and 3 branches
  - getPos(): go from 376 instructions and 66 branches down
    to 258 and 34
  - getSlope(): go from 179 instr. and 30 branches down to
    134 and 19
  - calcDX/DY(): go from 33 instructions and 2 branches
    down to 28 instructions and no branches
  - Arc.init(): go from 472 instructions and 59 branches down
    to 134 and 13. A good chunk of the savings comes from
    not inlining buildTable()
  - buildTable(): go from 262/44 down to 155/22. Even if
    inlined in init(), that's still substantial savings
- Get rid of unnecessary companion objects to save extra code
  generated for static initialization (including at call
  sites)
- Move binarySearch() to the collection library and optimize
  it by removing throws and branches
- Inline cheap operators and functiosn in [Primitive]List
  to reduce instructions at call site and remove jumps

commit_message:
Update Settings: Update VPN settings item (#5394)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/0/1208966262488271/f 

### Description

Updates the VPN settings item to match new designs. As the new designs
require us to know more about the subscription state we need to expose
more Subscription Statuses so we can reflect that in the settings screen
like the "Waiting" state that is equivalent to our new "Activating"
state.

The biggest thing to note is that now it's not like it used to be where
we had just 2 states, locked and unlocked, we now more readily show the
user the VPN item and what it's subscription state is.

I'm still waiting on answers regarding the [onboarded
state](https://app.asana.com/0/0/1208847942321390/1208973040958179/f)

And [connecting state
](https://app.asana.com/0/0/1208847942321390/1208973157895050/f) but I
can always circle back later and address these as necessary.


[Designs](https://www.figma.com/design/CjH849hL53lhsPlf6Ufeo4/%E2%9A%99%EF%B8%8F-Browser-Settings-Documentation-(All-Platforms)?node-id=7605-431390&node-type=instance&t=ZKIMXkdZqzh5gEjU-11)

Prerequisite: Enable `newSettings` feature toggle

Prerequisite: Apply the patch in the [Asana
task](https://app.asana.com/0/1207908166761516/1208966262488271/f)

Note: Only the VPN item and possibly the Settings item will be seen,
depending on the different states. PIR, ITR are not included as part of
this PR and so are not visible.

- [x] Verify VPN item is visible with colored icon and status is off

_Unsubscribed_

and status is off
- [x] Click VPN item
- [x] Nothing should happen

_PPro Activating_

SubscriptionStatus.WAITING`
- [x] Open New Settings
- [x] Verify VPN item is visible with grey icon and status is off
- [x] Click VPN item

- [x] In `SubscriptionManager` change ln 442 to `return

- [x] In `RealSubscriptions` change ln 77 to `return flowOf(listOf())` 
- [x] Open New Settings
- [x] Verify VPN item is not visible

_Legacy Support_

- [x] Verify old VPN settings works by repeating steps above

commit_message:
Add Expect APIs to Kruth

Note to API council - this is an unpublished library, so please ignore
this for API review. API tracking is only enabled to check against
compatibility with the public API of Truth.

Expect adds a way to do soft asserts in Kruth. These APIs are kept
JVM-only for now, as they have an unfortunate dependency on JUnit
TestRule, but it should be possible to commonize the logic and keep
binary compat by splitting the implementation in a future change.

This change also more closely aligns with the way Truth is
implemented. These are JVM-only because they depend on JUnit TestRule,
but presumably we could figure out a way to implement these on top of
kotlin.test or other KMP-compatible test engines to bring the same
functionality. Since it is strictly additive, I wanted to start by
matching the API in just JVM.

In order to get the original Truth tests passing it was required to fix
b/296945032 to align the way stacktraces are cleaned up / passed around
in the resulting exceptions. It was also necessary to fix the return
type of FailureStrategy#fail so that it does not return Nothing (forced
to throw), as soft assert depends on the ability to defer this.

NOTE: Truth has logic to clean stack traces via StackTraceCleaner, but
there are only JVM-apis to modify stack traces of thrown exceptions,
since they work differently on different platforms. Also, the primary
way of constructing AssertionErrorWithFacts, which instantiates it with
an empty stack trace has been deferred to a future CL to keep this one
smaller for review.

Fixes: 296945032
Relnote: "N/A"
Test: ANDROIDX_PROJECTS=INFRAROGUE ./gradlew kruth:kruth:allTests -Pandroidx.enabled.kmp.target.platforms="+LINUX,+MAC" --rerun-tasks
Change-Id: I084ce7c5373dd0e0c9c58f10c4c85599f65ea015

commit_message:
Move source information to a side table

Prior to this change, source information collected for tooling
was as a slot in the group associated with a call. This had two
down-sides, 1) it grew the size of the slot table unnecessarily
and 2) it introduced groups just to record the source information
for functions that otherwise would not need a group.

With this change, the collection of source information is
1) now disabled by default and only enabled when the tooling
API requests it, and 2) moved to a side table instead of being
recorded in the main table.

As source information collection was on by default and is now
off, the size of the slot table for builds that do not use R8,
ProGuard or similar tools are smaller.

Test: ./gradlew :composer:r:r:tDUT
Fixes: 254480106

Change-Id: Ic8b75b1dab148d7cb0313131bd5df5b8a209cc6e
llm_reason: The changes refactor the code to move source information into a side table, reducing the main slot table's size and improving code structure by eliminating unnecessary groups. This simplifies data management and improves efficiency.

commit_message:
API Changes to KeyEvent

This CL does a few things:

1. Prevents false positives:

The current API can lead to false positives. The Compose
 "KeyEvent" has a "Key" property. If we don't have a
 matching "Key" value, (i.e. the platform sends us an
 unknown key), we currently use Key.Unknown to represent
 it. This has the drawback that two different unknown
 keys map to the same value. For example, if a platform
 does not have Key.Home, then the condition
      if(keyEvent.key == Key.Home) { ... }
 will pass for any unknown key. The root cause is that
 "Key" is a property, and it has to be set even if it
 is not supported on a particular platform. This CL
 changes this API to:
    if(keyEvent.isKey(Key.A))  { ... }
 Now the different platforms are not forced to populate
 the property with some default value but just have to
 return false when the key is unknown.

2. Ability to distinguish between left and right Alt keys

We Currently use an integer "keycode" to distinguish between
 different keys. This works fine for Android because every
 key has a unique keycode. The desktop however, uses the same
 keycode for left and right "Alt" keys and uses an additional
 property called "Location" to distinguish between the left
 and right keys. The result is that the left and right Alt
 Keys are identical in Compose. Thr root cause is that the
 Key class is an inline class (Integer) that forces the
 desktop to use an integer to represent each key. Now that
 we changed the api to keyEvent.isKey(), the desktop can
 provide an implementation that is backed by multiple
 properties of its native keyCode.

3. Exposes Native Android and Desktop Key Events

When the API provided with the Compose KeyEvent is not
 sufficient, the user should be allowed to access the native
 KeyEvent (as an escape hatch). This CL enables that by
 adding a property called "nativeKey" which exposes the native
 key event.

Bug: 173086397
Bug: 173080474
Test: ./gradlew compose:ui:ui:connectedCheck -P android.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=androidx.compose.ui.input.key.KeyTest
Test: ./gradlew compose:ui:ui:connectedCheck -P android.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=androidx.compose.ui.input.key.AndroidProcessKeyInputTest
Test: ./gradlew compose:ui:ui:connectedCheck -P android.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=androidx.compose.ui.input.key.MetaKeyTest
Test: ./gradlew compose:ui:ui:connectedCheck -P android.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=androidx.compose.ui.input.key.ProcessKeyInputTest
Relnote: "The native keyEvent can now be accessed through keyEvent.nativeKeyEvent"
Change-Id: I87c57d68b76441fe92d2b91f58385832fc40ec8d
llm_reason: The changes refactor the Key class to use Int instead of Long, simplify the API by removing redundant properties and methods, and improve code structure by eliminating duplicate code across platforms. This reduces technical debt and improves maintainability.

commit_message:
Fix Camera2ExtetsionsActivityTest failures

Root cause: newly added checkPreviewUpdated_afterSwitchCamera test may fail if the
opposite side camera doesn't support the same extension mode.

Refactor the camera capture session creation flow. The new flow will act
according to current state. When requesting to start preview:

- STATE_CAMERA_CLOSED -> Open camera first. Open the capture session
  when camera is opened.
- STATE_CAPTURE_SESSION_CONFIGURED -> Close the capture session and
  camera first. If the target camera is the same, directly open the
  capture session with the new target extension mode. Otherwise, reopen
  the camera and then switch to the new extension mode
- Others -> Only update the target camera and extension mode info. When
  receiving camera onOpened or capture session onConfigured events,
  reopen to the new target camera and extension mode if it is mismatched.

Update the flow to limit the fields access in specific threads to avoid
race conditions

- Main thread: camera id and extension mode related info
- Camera thread: camera pipe line state, camera device or capture session
related info

Rotary injection scopes. Adds support for keyboard input injection

commit_message:
Introduce CollectionManager for preventing concurrent access

Currently there are many instances in the AnkiDroid codebase where
the collection is accessed a) on the UI thread, blocking the UI, and
b) in unsafe ways (eg by checking to see if the collection is open,
and then assuming it will remain open for the rest of a method call.
"Fix full download crashing in new schema case" (159a108dcb) demonstrates
a few of these cases.

This PR is an attempt at addressing those issues. It introduces
a `withCol` function that is intended to be used instead of
CollectionHelper.getCol(). For example, code that previously looked
like:

        val col = CollectionHelper.getInstance().getCol(this)
        val count = col.decks.count()

Can now be used like this:

        val count = withCol { decks.count() }

The block is run on a background thread, and other withCol calls made
in parallel will be queued up and executed sequentially. Because of
the exclusive access, routines can safely close and reopen the
collection inside the block without fear of affecting other callers.

It's not practical to update all the legacy code to use withCol
immediately - too much work is required, and coroutines are not
accessible from Java code. The intention here is that this new path is
gradually bought into. Legacy code can continue calling CollectionHelper.
getCol(), which internally delegates to CollectionManager.

Two caveats to be aware of:
- Legacy callers will wait for other pending operations to complete
before they receive the collection handle, but because they retain it,
subsequent access is not guaranteed to be exclusive.
- Because getCol() and colIsOpen() are often used on the main thread,
they will block the UI if a background operation is already running.
Logging has been added to help diagnose this, eg messages like:

E/CollectionManager: blocked main thread for 2626ms:
    com.ichi2.anki.DeckPicker.onCreateOptionsMenu(DeckPicker.kt:624)

Other changes:

- simplified CoroutineHelpers
- added TR function for accessing translations without a col reference
- onCreateOptionsMenu() needed refactoring to avoid blocking the UI.
- The blocking colIsOpen() call in onPrepareOptionsMenu() had to be
removed. I can not reproduce the issue it reports, and the code checks
for col in onCreateOptionsMenu().
- The subscribers in ChangeManager need to be cleared out at the start
of each test, or two tests are flaky when run with the new schema.
llm_reason: The commit introduces a `withCol` function to serialize access to the collection, preventing concurrent modifications and ensuring thread safety. This addresses potential race conditions and unsafe accesses, improving concurrency management.

Introduce a GraphLoop that will accept and submit requests.

This class is built to asyncronously enqueue and process commands
that will be sent to an active camera. This replicates a lot of
the core logic that currently exists in GraphProcessor while also
addressing several race conditions and bugs that can show up with
the existing implementation when requests are aborted or during
fast sequences of events and updates.

The new system is more testable and deterministic, and will likely
reduce the need for additional synchronization in downstream
classes. This will reduce CPU load and overhead when submitting
requests.

commit_message:
One composition measurement and layout

The CL adds ComplexLayout, a type of measure box which composes its
children during its own composition. Using this measure box, the entire
tree of component nodes will be built as a result of AndroidCraneView
being emitted, without waiting for subsequent collect calls. Avoiding
starting one subcomposition per measure box should be a performance
improvement. ComplexLayout does not provide collect, but expects
children to be passed when creating/emitting it, which means, among
others, that:
- it is no longer possible to collect a composable that depends on
  measurement constraints
- it is no longer possible to collect multiple composables
The above cases should be supported, but this will come in a further CL.
Therefore, the current CL leaves in place (but deprecates) the already
existing ComplexMeasureBox (and related classes). The implementation
supports combined hierarchies of ComplexMeasureBox and ComplexLayout.

The CL adds Layout, which is a ComplexLayout unaware of intrinsic
measurements.

Example usage for a Row widget:
    <ComplexLayout
        layoutBlock={ measurables, constraints ->
            val placeables = mutableListOf<Placeable>()
            val width = measurables.map { measurable ->
                measurable.maxIntrinsicWidth(constraints.maxHeight).also {
                    placeables.add(measurable.measure(Constraints(
                        it, it, 0.ipx, constraints.maxHeight
                    )))
                }
            }.sum()
            val height = placeables.map { it.height }.max()

            layoutResult(width, height) {
                var left = 0.ipx
                placeables.forEach { placeable ->
                    placeable.place(left, 0.ipx)
                    left += placeable.width
                }
            }
        }
        minIntrinsicWidthBlock={ measurables, h ->
            measurables.map { it.minIntrinsicWidth(h) }.sum()
        }
        maxIntrinsicWidthBlock={ measurables, h ->
            measurables.map { it.maxIntrinsicWidth(h) }.sum()
        }
        minIntrinsicHeightBlock={ measurables, w ->
            measurables.map { it.minIntrinsicHeight(w) }.max()
        }
        maxIntrinsicHeightBlock={ measurables, w ->
            measurables.map { it.maxIntrinsicHeight(w) }.max()
        }
        children />

and for a Padding widget:
    <Layout layoutBlock = { measurables, constraints ->
        val measurable = measurables.firstOrNull()
        if (measurable == null) {
            layout(constraints.minWidth, constraints.minHeight) {}
        } else {
            val paddingLeft = padding.left.toIntPx()
            val paddingTop = padding.top.toIntPx()
            val paddingRight = padding.right.toIntPx()
            val paddingBottom = padding.bottom.toIntPx()
            val horizontalPadding = (paddingLeft + paddingRight)
            val verticalPadding = (paddingTop + paddingBottom)

            val newConstraints = Constraints(
                minWidth = (constraints.minWidth - horizontalPadding).coerceAtLeast(IntPx.Zero),
                maxWidth = (constraints.maxWidth - horizontalPadding).coerceAtLeast(IntPx.Zero),
                minHeight = (constraints.minHeight - verticalPadding).coerceAtLeast(IntPx.Zero),
                maxHeight = (constraints.maxHeight - verticalPadding).coerceAtLeast(IntPx.Zero)
            )
            val placeable = measurable.measure(newConstraints)
            val width =
                min(placeable.width + horizontalPadding, constraints.maxWidth)
            val height =
                min(placeable.height + verticalPadding, constraints.maxHeight)

            layout(width, height) {
                placeable.place(paddingLeft, paddingTop)
            }
        }
    } children />

Bug: 129273059
Test: ran SingleCompositionLayoutTest and layout tests
Change-Id: I4fff44ab4d3f87b54273d8484554941064ac356b
llm_reason: The commit introduces ComplexLayout, which replaces ComplexMeasureBox with a more efficient composition model. This reduces code complexity by eliminating deprecated classes and simplifying the layout hierarchy.

commit_message:
Code size optimizations in UI modules

The main change this CL introduces is replacing calls to check() by calls to
checkPrecondition(), which produces smaller code by avoiding an unnecessary
toString() call and by not throwing an exception directly at the check site.

In LayoutCoordinates, this CL does a couple of things:
- Invoke properties only once to avoid the extra checks they do
- Use fastMin/MaxOf(), variants of min/maxOf() that accept 4 parameters
  without having to create an array for the varargs. The generated code gets
  inlined down to 3 fmin/fmax on aarch64

Test: compose:ui:androidInstrumentedTests/androidUnitTest, same in ui-unit
RelNote: N/A
Change-Id: I2ba2708481fdb73e4181127f9a050da607fc0881
llm_reason: The commit replaces calls to check() with checkPrecondition(), which reduces code size by avoiding unnecessary toString() calls and direct exceptions. This optimization decreases CPU usage and improves performance.

commit_message:
Support changes in the tracker entities (#3115)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1204479514332481/f

### Description
See the [asana task](https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1204479514332481/f) for more context but in a nutshel:
* This PR adds support for changes in tracker entities
* It moves the `AppTrackerBlockingStatsRepository` repository to the `store` module and makes the vpn tracker DAO private so that all consumers have to go through the repository
  * This way we sealed the UI from any meaningful change

### Steps to test this PR

_Test entity changes_
- [x] install from this branch
- [x] launch app and enable AppTP
- [x] launch some apps that contain trackers (speed test for instance)
- [x] In the `vpn_app_tracker_entities` db table, modify the `trackerCompanyId` for one of the blocked trackers to be eg. `-1`
- [x] go to AppTP activity screen
- [x] verify the associated trackers no longer appear in any of the counts/UI that show trackers blocked
- [x] In the `vpn_app_tracker_blocking_list_metadata` db table, change the eTag to be eg. `1`
- [x] Go to in-app settings -> AppTP dev settings and force update the blocklist
- [x] Go to AppTP activity screen
- [x] verify previously gone trackers now appear as normal
llm_reason: The changes refactor the code to reduce dependencies and improve encapsulation by moving AppTrackerBlockingStatsRepository to the store module, making the DAO private. This simplifies the architecture and reduces coupling between components.

commit_message:
Address ActivityEmbedding APIs feedback

See below Relnote
RelNote: "
1. Add RuleController#setRules to set a bunch of rules
2. Extract rule related APIs from SplitController to singleton RuleController. They are:
  - addRule
  - removeRule
  - getRules
  - setRules
  - parseRules
  - clearRules
3. Extract #isActivityEmbedded from SplitController to singleton
   ActivityEmbeddingController, which is:
  - isActivityEmbedded
4. Remove SplitController#initialize. To set rules from XML file, please
   use RuleController#parseRules and #setRules.
   Before this change:
     SplitController.initialize(context, R.xml.static_rules)
   After this change:
     val ruleController = RuleController.getInstance(context)
     val rules = ruleController.parseRules(R.xml.static_rules)
     ruleController.setRules(rules)
5. We don't distinguish static rules with runtime rules anymore. That said,
   calling #clearRules results to clear all rules no matter they are
   registered with static XML rule definitions or at runtime. To have
   the legacy behavior of `SplitController#clearRegisteredRules`, please
   call `RuleController#parseRules` with the XML resources id and call
   `RuleController#setRules` to set back the rules again.
   Before this change:
     SplitController.getInstance(context).clearRegisteredRules()
   After this change:
     val ruleController = RuleController.getInstance(context)
     val rules = ruleController.parseRules(R.xml.static_rules)
     ruleController.setRules(rules)
"
Test: presubmit
Bug: 255952916

Change-Id: I322b75a0f7f34fa1cb74443c02c2ce8bacc1d5d7
llm_reason: The commit refactors code to consolidate rule management into SplitController, reducing redundancy and improving maintainability by eliminating separate RuleController.

Add Experimental TraceMetric for fully custom metrics from traces

Fixes: 219851406
Test: ./gradlew bench:b-m:cC
Relnote: "Added new experimental TraceMetric API for defining fully
custom metrics based on content of a Perfetto trace."

Along with opening up the metric, migrates Metric to more extensible
output types. This allows us to eventually add further per-submetric
customization (such as per-submetric strictness on presence in each
iteration, custom display in studio, etc.

Also now correctly propogates TraceProcessor errors (e.g. query syntax
errors) to Kotlin callers.

This change also removes the concept of timelineRange in metric
results / trace UiState, as it's no longer necessary after
optimizations to remove sleep()s at the beginning/end of trace
capture.

commit_message:
Extract configuration related logic out from BrowserAutofill (#2259)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1202922320754385/f

Description

Prior to this change, InlineBrowserAutofill does 2 things: 1.) configures the webview for the specific site being loaded and 2.) handles injection of credentials. Because of this 2 purposes, BrowserAutofill is injected in 2 places creating 2 instances for every tab. The problem with this is that AutofillStoredBackJavascriptInterface together with majority of its dependencies are duplicated when we only need them for purpose 2.

To solve it in this PR, we separate the 2 purposes into 2 separate group of classes. Thus we only inject exactly what we need in the BrowserWebViewClient.

Steps to test this PR

  Smoke test Autofill
  Smoke test Email
llm_reason: The commit refactors code to separate configuration logic from autofill functionality, reducing dependencies and improving modularity. This simplifies the class structure by eliminating duplicate code and moving responsibilities to appropriate scopes.

commit_message:
Show import promo in autofill management (#6266)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/1/137249556945/project/72649045549333/task/1210598549117736?focus=true 

### Description
Includes:
- logic to show or not import promo in autofill management list
- store boolean if user has ever imported passwords (to avoid other prompts)
- heuristics around messaging
- remote FF
- support animation in message cta

### Steps to test this PR

_Showing the promo_
- [ ] fresh install
- [ ] Go to passwords list
- [ ] You should not see any promo
- [ ] Add one credential
- [ ] You should see a promo now (with the animated logo)
- [ ] you should see `autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_shown` with `source = passwords_management_promo`
- [ ] Click Open Passwords
- [ ] you should see pixel `autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_tapped_android_phone` with `source = passwords_management_promo`
- [ ] Enter the flow but leave it without completion
- [ ] Go back to the list
- [ ] Ensure promo is there

_Dismiss_
- [ ] Dismiss the promo
- [ ] Ensure promo goes away
- [ ] Ensure next promo shows up
- [ ] Leave enter the screen
- [ ] No more import promos

_Complete import_
(To test this better go to `RealImportInPasswordsVisibility` change MAX_PASSWORDS_TO_SHOW_PROMO to 5k , just a random big number. This is so we make sure we hide the promo because import happened, and not because number of passwords is >25)
- [ ] fresh install
- [ ] Go to passwords list
- [ ] You should not see any promo
- [ ] Add one credential
- [ ] You should see a promo now
- [ ] Import your passwords
- [ ] When it completes, promo is gone

_Complete import (different entry point)_
(Keep the hardcoded value)
- [ ] fresh install
- [ ] Go to passwords list
- [ ] You should not see any promo
- [ ] Add one credential
- [ ] You should see a promo now
- [ ] From the overflow menu, import your passwords from google
- [ ] Complete the import
- [ ] When it completes, promo should be gone

_max 25 passwords_
(Remove any hardcoded values)
- [ ] fresh install
- [ ] add more than 25 passwords
- [ ] go to passwords list
- [ ] you should not see the import promo

_FF disabled_
(Remove any hardcoded values)
- [ ] fresh install
- [ ] go to FF inventory and disable `canPromoteImportPasswords`
- [ ] go to passwords list
- [ ] add 1 password
- [ ] you should not see the import promo

commit_message:
remove Kotlin's "Context Receiver" feature

Context Receivers are an experimental Kotlin feature
. They will be removed and replaced with Context Parameters.
It is unlikely that there will be any overlap period, so we need
to remove our Context Receiver code, with the intention of replacing it
at a later date

> * **2.0.20**: A warning is introduced for declarations that use
> context receivers.
> The warning should mention that the design of context receivers
> is about to change, according to the new design (add a link to the KEEP/YT).
> The safest workaround is to migrate your code to extensions.
>  * **2.1.20**: The implementation of context receivers is removed from the compiler.
> Effectively, this warning is turned into an error.
> Note that this part is most likely inevitable as the implementation
> of context parameters requires  quite extensive refactoring in the compiler,
> and there are significant chances that we won't have either
> context receivers or context parameters at that time.
>  [There is a slight chance that we'll have an early prototype of context parameters,
> but we are not counting on that.]
> * **2.2.0**: Context parameters are introduced under a new compiler flag.

https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-67119/Migration-warning-from-context-receivers-to-context-parameters

> **Phased removal of context receivers feature**
> In Kotlin 1.6.20, we introduced context receivers as an
> Experimental feature. After listening to community
> feedback, we've decided not to continue with it. Instead,
> we plan to introduce a replacement in future Kotlin
> releases: context parameters. You can find the proposal
> for context parameters in the KEEP.

https://kotlinlang.org/docs/whatsnew-eap.html#phased-removal-of-context-receivers-feature

Deprecated:
https://redirect.github.com/Kotlin/KEEP/blob/context-parameters/proposals/context-receivers.md
* https://redirect.github.com/Kotlin/KEEP/issues/259

To be replaced by:
https://redirect.github.com/Kotlin/KEEP/blob/context-parameters/proposals/context-parameters.md
* https://redirect.github.com/Kotlin/KEEP/issues/367

Fixes 16863

Reverts: 672b075a546e806e93f8e2c158749231c065a021
Reverts: 1c01e4cb6f06a464f31fcc80a1907d58d1952c63
llm_reason: The commit removes context receivers and replaces them with context parameters, which simplifies the code by eliminating experimental features and aligns with Kotlin's updated language standards. This reduces technical debt by making the codebase more maintainable and future-proof.

commit_message:
Migrate auto-close to Coroutines

This change moves AutoCloser and relevant classes to Coroutines, aligning with the rest of the Coroutine-based infra refactor for KMP. It also reduces the lock contention by using atomics and avoiding the usage of the lock object along with improving the auto-boxing of binding arguments in the AutoClosingSQLiteStatement.

This CL also adds an internal 'Watch' interface to get the uptime in millis for time tracking, enabling time movement using a fake watch for tests. By using a test watch along with a TestCoroutineDispatcher, this CL fixes the flaky tests that have plague auto-close for a while and removes the usages of Thread.sleep(), thus creating deterministic tests.

commit_message:
Don't return collapsedSemantics on unattached or deactivated nodes

This patch ensures that if a node is deactivated but not attached then `collapsedSemantics` are not returned (and `applySemantics` is not called). This addresses the exception thrown when `applySemantics` is called on an unattached node.

In returning null for deactivated nodes, the SemanticsNodeInteraction.assertIsDeactivated assert needs to be refactored. We need to surface deactivated nodes but cannot do so with testTags because testTags come from semantics config, which comes from modifier nodes. This patch rewrites all foundation tests using the `assertIsDeactivated` assert by instead saving the semantics ids of relevant nodes, then verifying that those nodes all are deactivated by checking `layoutInfo.isDeactivated`.

Test: manually verified Material catalog does not crash
Fixes: 353595612
Fixes: 353538515
RelNote: "New `semanticsId()` test API introduced."
Change-Id: Iee26db6343fbaa3689546256eac2bb0d9725adea
llm_reason: The changes refactor test code to use a more direct assertion method, replacing manual ID tracking and node traversal with `assertIsDeactivated()`, which simplifies the tests and reduces technical debt.

commit_message:
Customisable dimensions in ConstraintLayout

The CL adds support for customizing ConstraintLayout children dimensions
in the ConstraintSet DSL. The width and height of children can now be
wrap, wrapFixed, fixed, percent, etc.
ConstraintLayout is temporarily allowed to perform multiple children
measurements, until this logic is replaced with intrinsic measurements.

Relnote: Added support for customizing dimensions of children of ConstraintLayout
Bug: 144953320

llm_reason: The commit refactors and simplifies the ConstraintLayout implementation by removing deprecated code, reducing the number of lines, and improving the overall structure. The changes include removing MultiMeasureLayout, simplifying dimension handling, and streamlining the measurement logic.

commit_message:
Use declared annotation targets for site usage filtering

This fixes an issue with unexpected annotations on properties due to a bug, KSP incorrectly copies annotations from a constructor
parameter (`KSValueParameter`) to its associated property (`KSPropertyDeclaration`). See https://github.com/google/ksp/issues/1198.

This CL also parameterizes XTypeElementTest so that all tests are run as both source and precompiled classes.

Test: XAnnotatedTest
Change-Id: I20a56b8b1ee8510538f105f908de2e9446f042c8
llm_reason: The changes primarily address a bug related to incorrect annotation copying from constructor parameters to properties, which is not directly tied to any of the specified code smells. The modifications focus on improving how annotations are filtered and handled, ensuring correct use site targets without introducing inefficiencies or security issues.

commit_message:
Add loading bar performance experiment (#4978)

Task/Issue URL:
https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1208210291365732/f

### Description

- Adds a custom experiment to measure the perceived performance of the
loading bar on existing users.
- The experiment randomly assigns a variant on-update, with percentage
roll-out controlled by the loadingBarExp feature toggle.

### Steps to test this PR
_Point at the config linked in the task_

-------
_In `LoadingBarExperimentVariantInitializer` set `val probabilities =
doubleArrayOf(0.0, 1.0)`_

_Enrolment_
- [x] Fresh install from this branch
- [x] Verify that `m_loading_bar_exp_enrollment_test` is sent

_Daily_
- [x] Verify that `m_browser_feature_daily_active_user_d` is sent with
param `loading_bar_exp=1`

_URI loaded_
- [x] Load a site (eg. example.com)
- [x] Verify that `m_uri_loaded` is sent with param `loading_bar_exp=1`

_Pull to refresh_
- [x] Pull to refresh the page
- [x] Verify that `m_browser_pull_to_refresh` is sent with param
`loading_bar_exp=1`
 
_Refresh_
- [x] Refresh the page from the menu
- [x] Verify that `m_nav_r_p` is sent with param `loading_bar_exp=1`

_Refresh action daily_
- [x] Verify that only one `m_refresh_action_daily` is sent with param
`loading_bar_exp=1`

_Loading bar_
- [x] Search for something on SERP
- [x] Scroll to hide the loading bar
- [x] Click a link
- [x] Verify that the loading bar shows immediately

_Feedback_
- [x] Go to “Settings” > “About” > “Share Feedback"
- [x] Share positive feedback
- [x] Verify that `mfbs_positive_submit` is sent with param
`loading_bar_exp=1`
- [x] Share negative feedback
- [x] Verify that `mfbs_negative_submit` is sent with param
`loading_bar_exp=1`

-------
_In `LoadingBarExperimentVariantInitializer` set `val probabilities =
doubleArrayOf(1.0, 0.0)`_

_Enrolment_
- [x] Fresh install from this branch
- [x] Verify that `m_loading_bar_exp_enrollment_control` is sent

_Daily_
- [x] Verify that `m_browser_feature_daily_active_user_d` is sent with
param `loading_bar_exp=0`

_URI loaded_
- [x] Load a site (eg. example.com)
- [x] Verify that `m_uri_loaded` is sent with param `loading_bar_exp=0`

_Pull to refresh_
- [x] Pull to refresh the page
- [x] Verify that `m_browser_pull_to_refresh` is sent with param
`loading_bar_exp=0`
 
_Refresh_
- [x] Refresh the page from the menu
- [x] Verify that `m_nav_r_p` is sent with param `loading_bar_exp=0`

_Refresh action daily_
- [x] Verify that only one `m_refresh_action_daily` is sent with param
`loading_bar_exp=0`

_Loading bar_
- [x] Search for something on SERP
- [x] Scroll to hide the loading bar
- [x] Click a link
- [x] Verify that there is a delay when showing the loading bar

_Feedback_
- [x] Go to “Settings” > “About” > “Share Feedback"
- [x] Share positive feedback
- [x] Verify that `mfbs_positive_submit` is sent with param
`loading_bar_exp=0`
- [x] Share negative feedback
- [x] Verify that `mfbs_negative_submit` is sent with param
`loading_bar_exp=0`

-------

commit_message:
Allow removing groups that are no longer required

Added an option, "nonSkippingGroupOptimization" (disabled by default),
that allows removing groups around non-skipping composable functions
(including intrnsic remember) when it is supported by the runtime

The runtime was modified to allow calling `cached` after a child
group was added to the slot table. This means that static calls
that do not skip are no longer required to have a group. This
change modifies the plugin to not generate the groups that are
no longer necessary after the runtime change.

Relnote: """
Added an experimental optimization that will remove the groups
around functions that are not skippable such as explicitly marked
as `@NonSkippableComposable` and functions that are implicitly
not skippable such inline functions and functions that return a
non-Unit value such as `remember`.

This optimization can be enabled by passing plugin option,

  -P plugin:androidx.compose.compiler.plugins.kotlin:nonSkippingGroupOptimization=true

to the Kotlin compiler.
"""

Bug: 254480106

Change-Id: I1688f5dbf178696231c9c375c1299cd4a45a9fcd

commit_message:
Refactored API to clean up singleton usage

Various changes according to ongoing API reviews. The major
change is a refactor which splits out the state logic of JankStats
into the new class ApplicationState.

PerformanceMetricsState is a per-window singleton (as JankStats
was previously) which
allows storage and retrieval of app state in a single object within
the hierarchy. Meanwhile, JankStats is now a regular object (not a
singleton), created via constructor, that is wholly managed by the
application. This division should make it clearer and cleaner such
that logic outside of the app code (e.g., RecyclerView) can retrieve
the PerformanceMetricsState and store information there without needing to
deal with (and potentially interfere with) JankStats.

Note that PerformanceMetricsState could even be used by other non-JankStats
libraries eventually; it is not specific to JankStats (though it still
contains frame timing logic, so it is best used by performance clients).

Also cleaned up other APIs and functionality:

Removed clearStats().
Documented example usage of jankHeuristicMultipler.
FrameMetrics usaged protected against doubled reports.
JankStats now takes a Window directly. PerformanceMetricsState takes a view
in the hierarchy, similar to the previous JankStats singleton.
PerformanceMetricsState is cached inside a MetricsStateHolder object.
Clients should request a holder and can then query it quickly to
get the state, rather than having to call the instance getter each time,
which walks the view hierarchy up to the root.

Bug: 205904685
Bug: 205806861
Test: Manual (integration tests and JankStats runs across devices)
Relnote: Removed clearStates() based on API review
Change-Id: I489696a1383a09be1222d5bb744a3615ba176c56
llm_reason: The commit refactors JankStats to split state logic into a new class, improving code structure and reducing complexity. This makes the API cleaner by separating concerns.

commit_message:
Introduce BasicSecureTextField in text2

Add a new Experimental text input composable to foundation that's specifically designed to be used as a password field.

`BasicSecureTextField` accepts 3 methods of obfuscation of its content; `Visible`, `RevealLastTyped`, and `Hidden`.

This CL also adds another Experimental API to `BasicTextField2` called `CodepointTransformation`. This new interface is targeted to be a replacement of `VisualTransformation` in new text fields.

`OffsetMapping` has been a huge source of pain for both developers and maintainers. Allowing text length to change arbitrarily has been proven to be difficult for many reasons. Writing a correct function that calculated OffsetMapping has been a very difficult task. Also, selection and text offsets do not directly translate to each other which makes selection act wonky even when `TransformedText` is calculated correctly.

`CodepointTransformation` requires a 1-to-1 mapping of each codepoint in original text, which guarantees that the length of the current input and rendered text are always the same. This makes sure that selection does not run into any weird offset misalignment.

Test: :compose:foundation:foundation:cAT
Relnote: N/A
Change-Id: I4fbec661baa9cf8ba22e798f221acb24757e1e3e
llm_reason: The commit introduces a new `BasicSecureTextField` component, which refactors and simplifies secure text input handling by replacing `VisualTransformation` with `CodepointTransformation`. This reduces code complexity and potential bugs related to offset mapping issues.

commit_message:
Centralize thread confinement to UseCaseCameraRequestControl

1. Removed coroutine usage from CameraScope classes that was mainly used for thread confinement strategy
  - This ensures that code flows run on caller thread longer and switch to background thread later in common functions when required.
2. Added thread confinement in UseCaseCameraRequestControl which serves as the entry point for capture requests to UseCaseCameraScope
  - This removes the need of complicated synchronization lock handlings and thus less error-prone and reduces synchronization issues in UseCaseCameraScope layer.
3. Convenience functions are added in UseCaseThreads to better convey the purpose of the coroutine scope usages in thread confinement sites
  - The primary objective in these places is thread confinement, not async operation. In future, this may be further refactored to provide thread confinement in a different way (may not even require coroutine scopes).

  It is important that the synchronization points (e.g. threads.sequentialScope.launch calls) are removed from all UseCaseCameraControl classes at the same time as adding in RequestControl, so that additional sync points are not introduced in a code flow which can create hard-to-debug race conditions. TorchControl is an exception and thus carefully handled until blocking bug is resolved.

### Description
This PR adds the necessary pixels for Sync M2

### Steps to test this PR

_DAUs_
Modify FeatureRetentionPixelSender so it sends pixels even if they same
day was already sent.
Remove the if statement in line 63

- [ ] Open app and enable sync
- [ ] Force close the app and open it again
- [ ] Verify m_browser_feature_daily_active_user_d is sent with
parameter sync = 1

_Daily Success Rate_
Modify SyncDailyReportingWorkerScheduler so it schedules the Worker
every minute instead of every 24 hours
Modify line 45 in SyncStatsRepository so it doesn’t filter by sync
operations of the day before

 - [ ]Open app and enable sync
- [ ] Do some changes in syncable objects so sync operations are
triggered
 - [ ]Force close the app and open it again
- [ ] Verify m_sync_daily_success_rate is sent
- [ ] Verify m_sync_daily_attempts is sent

commit_message:
Optimize various things:

Optimize array management:
- Use @NeverInline in the cold code paths where we reallocate arrays
  to avoid putting a large amount of instructions on the hot path
  (caused by ART inlining).
- Introduce fastCopyOfRange() to reduce the amount of generated code
  (exception/stack trace + string builder) by skipping parameters
  validation we don't need.
- Introduce fastCopyInto() to eliminate unnecessary checks and
  exception throwing code introduced by the compiler after inlining.
- Don't use copyOf() in the color space code, just create the array
  we need with the known values we need. It produces a lot less
  code.
- Remove extra branches and instructions from IntStack.

Other optimizations:
- Branchless calls in ScrollNode
- Skip static initialization checks in OpArray by removing the
  companion object we don't need.
- Revert Constraints.bitsNeed...() and .maxAllowedForSize() to
  the original implementation. The dependent add operation we
  had to add is actually slightly worse on modern devices.
  It's better to keep the code simpler.
- Unify padding checks in Padding.kt. Removes 60% of the
  branches on initialization.
  Optimize for the common case by switching to and instead
  of && in the test to go branchless. Factor method calls.
- Use "fast" coerce implementations where possible.
- Don't compare dps to Dp.Unspecified, use Dp.isSpecified
  instead (or Dp.isUnspecified). Same result, but much
  cheaper since we know Dp.Unspecified is NaN.
- Cleanup: address various warnings.

Test: ui tests, foundation tests, graphics tests
Relnote: N/A
Change-Id: I8402e8cfabf9726362273b2e7f5647854e602d63
llm_reason: The commit includes various optimizations such as using @NeverInline to prevent excessive inlining, introducing fastCopyOfRange and fastCopyInto methods to reduce generated code, and optimizing array management. These changes aim to improve runtime efficiency by reducing unnecessary operations and checks, which directly impacts CPU consumption and battery life.

commit_message:
[GH] [Room][Compiler Processing] Support getting non type elements

## Proposed Changes
Currently the Room compiler processing abstraction is set up to use the pattern of processing steps, including using auto-common for javac processing. Our existing processors (and I assume many other people's) do not use this approach and I'd prefer not to have to convert to that pattern. While annotated elements can be looked up directly with XRoundEnv, that currently only works for type elements.

This PR adds to XRoundEnv a new function to look up annotated elements of all kinds, not just type elements. This allows processors more flexibility in how they use the compiler processing (and mirrors the patterns we use with javac processing in our libraries).

The implementation is fairly straightforward, I just created new functions to wrap each possible type of Element and Symbol, leveraging existing classes.

I believe this could benefit from caching, but that could be added in the future.

I also did not attempt to modify XProcessingStep to support elements of all kinds, since the pattern it enforces seems directed towards starting with just type elements.

If this direction is OK I can also update the readme to cover this use case.

## Testing
Test: XRoundEnvTest

Wrote new unit tests that check annotated functions and properties in both java and kotlin are wrapped with the appropriate `X` class.

## Issues Fixed

Bug: b/184438287

This is an imported pull request from https://github.com/androidx/androidx/pull/154.

Resolves #154

GitOrigin-RevId: 3c4b68da712b6273b16023edf97255ce00df4e34

commit_message:
Split ArraySet into separate Jvm and Native implementations for toArray compatibility

It is required to provided a separate Java implementation of `.toArray` for
ArraySet for compatibility, due to:
  1. Ignoring nullity of the generic type T to maintain the behavior of setting the n+1'th item to null
  2. Allowing .toArray(array: Array<T>) to be called without making T a reified type
  2. Delegate the array copy implementation to System.arraycopy which is implemented natively on JVM

Instead of providing a less-performance version for native / non-jvm platforms,
it was removed in this CL because it was always only ever needed for
compatibility. Users should instead prefer to follow the Kotlin stdlib standard
of using `.toTypedArray` or one of its primitive relatives, e.g., `.toIntArray`.

Relnote: "`ArraySet.toArray` is now only available on JVM as it was always only
  included for compatibility with Java. Kotlin users should generaly use the
  stdlib's `.toTypedArray` helpers instead, which provides the exact same
  functionality with added type safety."
Test: :collection:collection:test
Change-Id: I2c59be76341e284936d6da16a3feb582d1f66c51
llm_reason: The commit refactors ArraySet by splitting it into separate JVM and native implementations, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability. This addresses high complexity debt by separating concerns and making the codebase more modular.

It is a memory leak to store the activity reference.
It can also cause crashes (e.g., when the service continues running in the
background but the activity is already killed).

It is better to use a local context.

commit_message:
Various optimizations.

Optimize array management:
- Introduce fastCopyOfRange() to reduce the amount of generated code
  (exception/stack trace + string builder) by skipping parameters
  validation we don't need.
- Introduce fastCopyInto() to eliminate unnecessary checks and
  exception throwing code introduced by the compiler after inlining.
- Don't use copyOf() in the color space code, just create the array
  we need with the known values we need. It produces a lot less
  code.
- Remove extra branches and instructions from IntStack.

Other optimizations:
- Branchless calls in ScrollNode
- Skip static initialization checks in OpArray by removing the
  companion object we don't need.
- Revert Constraints.bitsNeed...() and .maxAllowedForSize() to
  the original implementation. The dependent add operation we
  had to add is actually slightly worse on modern devices.
  It's better to keep the code simpler.
- Unify padding checks in Padding.kt. Removes 60% of the
  branches on initialization.
  Optimize for the common case by switching to and instead
  of && in the test to go branchless. Factor method calls.
- Use "fast" coerce implementations where possible.
- Don't compare dps to Dp.Unspecified, use Dp.isSpecified
  instead (or Dp.isUnspecified). Same result, but much
  cheaper since we know Dp.Unspecified is NaN.
- Cleanup: address various warnings.

Relnote: N/A
Test: ui, foundation, graphics tests
Change-Id: If0a9b4367b271efd8b5eb6daf4aea5d23296f22a
llm_reason: The commit introduces several optimizations to reduce code complexity and improve maintainability. Changes include replacing fastCopyInto with copyInto, simplifying padding checks by switching from && to and, removing redundant conditions, and refactoring array management to eliminate unnecessary validations. These changes streamline the codebase, making it more efficient and easier to understand.

commit_message:
Migrate the remaining converters from the NullAwareTypeAdapterStore to XPoet

Also add more tests for custom type converters, including provides ones. This lead me to find that the getter for the provided converter was not quite right and ended up refactoring similar to dao impl getters, where we use a kotlin.Lazy when generating Kotlin otherwise the getter has memoization logic. Due to the lazy, Dao impl constructors had to be moved to the primary constructor, hence all Kotlin codegen tests update.

New XPoet API:
* XTypeSpec.Builder.setPrimaryConstructor() - For setting the primary constructor of a type, note that this has no real effect when generating Java, but will do when generating Kotlin.

commit_message:
Simplify and optimize tracing-driver event lifecycle

Test: Tests in tracing-driver
Test: TracingDriverBenchmark
Relnote: N/A

This significantly improves the producer side trace event recording by:

- Removing all atomic/lock access on producer (outside of emitting packet arrays)
- Removing all pools except for the primary tracing packet array
- Removing or inlining function calls on the recording critical path
- Avoiding nullable proto fields by marking `required` where possible
- Greedily emitting preamble packet

This makes average recording about twice as fast, measured on clock

commit_message:
Implement direct boot awareness

To do direct boot, important files need to be moved to device storage (i.e. not as safe storage). The only thing sensitive is user credentials (server settings in each profile) and the config file passed to libss-*.so. In order to implement direct boot, at least the active profile has to be moved/copied to device storage.

This commit keeps profile information (and temporary data used by ProfileConfigFragment) and Shadowsocks config in credential storage and move everything else to device storage. In order to make direct boot work, active profile is also copied into device storage and Shadowsocks config file is created in device storage during direct boot. Cleanup of those information is also implemented.

Fixes #1457.

NB:

* `android:directBootAware` can't be used on application element. That seems to be used by system apps.
* Works fine with multi-user;
* Old GSM files are not migrated (see App.kt) to device storage.
llm_reason: The commit moves sensitive data to device storage, reducing redundant files and optimizing storage usage.

commit_message:
Migrate KeepInView logic to pull based mechanism

The initial implementation of KeepInView logic in foundation had to use an observer to find the bounds of the focused child due to a limitation between `ui` and `foundation` layers. This limitation does not exist anymore and we can simply switch to a pull based mechanism where `ContentInViewNode` can just query its focused child to find its bounds.

Completely getting rid of this mechanism unfortunately also requires us to revert the solution that fixed reverse scrolling container problem. The new solution adjusts the container size and child placement for a single frame when a reverse scrolling viewport shrinks.

Bug: 392901531
Bug: 220030968
Test: existing tests for ContentInViewNode
Test: FocusTargetModifierNodeTest
Relnote: "Added `FocusTargetModifierNode.getFocusedRect()` that provides the focused area under this node's subtree, relative to the node's coordinates."
Relnote: "Added `ComposeFoundationFlags.isKeepInViewFocusObservationChangeEnabled` that enables the new way of keeping focused items in view for scrollable containers. If you run into a problem with the new approach, please file a bug and disable this flag."
Change-Id: I7a3dba71cc4bb2a193fa127bebdbe7b76b95110a
llm_reason: The changes refactor and simplify the ContentInViewNode by removing legacy code, reducing method complexity, and improving readability. This reduces technical debt.

commit_message:
AppCleaner: Improve ACS based cache clearing resilience by detecting storage entries "on-the-fly" via app size values

Most "Storage" entries in system details have a summary row that says something like "604 MB in internal storage"
While we don't know the translation of "in internal storage", we know the app size, so we can look for "604 MB".
There is a slight chance for false-positives if the data-usage of the app is the same as the app size, but that's pretty rare.
If that happens SD Maid just keeps retrying as usual.

With 218 apps this had a 99% success rate.
There was one app ("Digital Wellbeing", a system app), that showed the wrong app size (36,06 MB) on first load.
Only when SD Maid retried and reloaded the settings details, the size displayed correct (35,96 MB).
No idea why the size was displayed incorrectly the first time...

When clearing caches via ACS we now have 3 tier process:
1: Try to get the storage entry label via string resource ID
2: Try hard coded labels by language code
3: Try to find the summary row by looking up the app size

This should make it a lot easier to automatically support ROMs after updates, where the texts change.
llm_reason: The changes improve cache clearing resilience by detecting storage entries using app size values, reducing unnecessary retries and improving efficiency.

Refactor FocusOwner and FocusInvalidationManager

The FocusOwner and FocusInvalidationManager constructors
have a lot of lambda parameters, which result in unnecessary
Allocations. Over time we have added a lot of parameters and
the names of these parameters are confusing and the code is
hard to understand.

This CL introduces an internal interface that is passed as a
parameter to FocusOwner. AndroidComposeView implements
this interface that is passed to the FocusOwner constructor.

Similarly, all the lambdas needed by the FocusInvalidationManager
are replaced by functions added to FocusOwner.

Includes other minor code simplifications.

commit_message:
Manual drawing reordering

Originally we tried to use the auto redrawing based on RenderNode's elevation which proved to be tricky to support and caused a number of bugs. Instead of relaying on this mechanism we decided to decouple shadows size from z index as features and this CL is a first part of it. Here we still use DrawLayerModifier's elevation as a zIndex, but we do the drawing reordering manually.
This should also improve the drawing performance as we invalidate less(on apis < 29) and do expensive enableZ calls less often.

Fixes: 152600006
Fixes: 152696056
Bug: 151818355
Bug: 152417501
Test: new tests, manually
Change-Id: I45aa0e751b9d3bba74babcd25a835d8b34b99c24
llm_reason: The changes decouple shadow size from z-index, reducing unnecessary invalidations and expensive enableZ calls on APIs below Android 10. This improves drawing performance by minimizing redundant operations.

commit_message:
Add pixels for importing google passwords metrics (#5284)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/608920331025315/1208787586139232/f 

### Description
Adds metrics around the import password flow. To support this in a flexible way, it also allows for the url mappings to be defined remotely for which stage of the flow a user dropped out at if they didn't go all the way through; if we ever need to, can override the default url mappings.

Logcat filter: `message~:"Pixel sent: autofill_import_google_passwords"`

### Steps to test this PR

**Setup**
- [x] Fresh install

**Cancelling the user journey**
- [x] Visit `Passwords` screen, and verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_shown` in logs for the import password button being shown
- [x] Tap on `Import Passwords From Google` button; verify`autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_tapped` in logs
- [x] Verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_preimport_prompt_displayed` in logs
- [x] Dismiss the import dialog; verify`autofill_import_google_passwords_result_user_cancelled` in logs
- [x] Tap on `Import Passwords From Google` button again. 
- [x] This time, tap on the `Open Google Passwords` button; verify`autofill_import_google_passwords_preimport_prompt_confirmed` in logs
- [x] Tap the ✖️ button to quit the import flow; verify`autofill_import_google_passwords_result_user_cancelled` in logs, with `stage=webflow-pre-login`
- [x] Dismiss the dialog that is still showing, and verify there is not a further cancellation pixel in logs for when the dialog closes, as this is already covered by the ✖️ button cancellation
- [x] Launch the import flow again. This time tap on the `Sign in` button. Then ✖️ to exit the flow; verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_result_user_cancelled` with `stage=webflow-authenticate`
- [x] Launch the import flow again. This time, actually sign in, then ✖️ to exit the flow; verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_result_user_cancelled` with `stage=webflow-post-login-landing`
- [x] Launch the import flow again, and you'll be on the screen with the export button. tap ✖️ to exit the flow; verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_result_user_cancelled` with `stage=webflow-export`
- [x] Launch the import flow again. Tap the export button, and agree on the dialog. Then when prompted to authenticate, tap ✖️ to exit the flow; verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_result_user_cancelled` with `stage=webflow-authenticate`

**Succeeding**
- [x] Launch the import flow and fully complete it. Verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_result_success`, with the correct _bucket_  for `saved_credentials` and `skipped_credentials` based on bucket rules [defined here](https://app.asana.com/0/72649045549333/1207437778421216/f)
- [x] Repeat that, and you should get duplicates this time. Verify again, ensuring that `skipped_credentials` bucket is accurate.

**Overflow menu**
- [x] Now that you have saved passwords, `Import Passwords From Google` will appear in the overflow menu. Tap on that. Verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_overflow_menu_tapped` and `autofill_import_google_passwords_preimport_prompt_displayed` in logs

**Encrypted passphrase scenario**
- [x] Apply [patch](https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1208796814221367/f)
- [x] Launch import flow and you'll see the error screen. Tap ✖️ to exit flow. Verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_result_user_cancelled` with `stage=webflow-passphrase-encryption`

**CSV parsing scenario**
- [x] Discard local changes
- [x] Apply [patch](https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1208796814221368/f)
- [x] Launch import flow and carry on until you've exported. The patch will simulate an error. Verify `autofill_import_google_passwords_result_parsing` in logs.

**URL mapping via remote config**
- [x] Discard local changes
- [x] Apply [patch](https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1208799215631036/f) to override the URL mappings
- [x] Fresh install. Launch password import flow and then ✖️ to exit. Verify in logs with filter `urlMappings` that the mappings from the patched jsonblob are used and override the local defaults. 

**Selectively disabling JS injection**
- [x] Discard local changes

- [x] Complete the flow; verify it still works as expected

commit_message:
Remove compose plugin frontend dependencies in backend

Remove calls to `FunctionDescriptor.allowsComposableCalls` in the
compose IR plugin. These calls implicitly depend on the frontend
analysis through binding context slices which will not be
available with the FIR frontend.

The main change in this CL is to enable IrInlineReferenceLocator to
detect lambdas capable of composer capture. It also fixes a few minor
issues along the way:

- Detect inline lambda arguments to the two argument Array constructor
  calls. (This still needs support in the frontend part of the Compose
  plugin!)
- Don't transform the block arguments to remember in
  ComposableFunctionBodyTransformer, since they are marked with
  @DisallowComposableCalls
- Mark the block argument to Composer.cache with
  @DisallowComposableCalls, since such calls occur after the remember
  intrinsic transformation and could otherwise confuse later passes

Apart from that, the frontend analysis information can be incomplete,
since it is generated on demand in response to composable calls in
ComposableCallChecker. This can lead to some missed optimizations in the
backend. This is fixed by recomputing the relevant information in the
backend.

Bug: 229716591
Fixes: 229719233, 229719460
Test: ./gradlew :compose:c:c-h:i-t:tDUT
Change-Id: Iaa7a5cfffbf6cdcc23c4e3cd90adee649c627d86
llm_reason: The commit refactors code to remove dependencies on frontend analysis by eliminating calls to FunctionDescriptor.allowsComposableCalls and introduces IrInlineReferenceLocator. This reduces coupling and technical debt, improving maintainability.

defaults pattern for ButtonColors, TextButtonColors and
IconButtonColors. The idea is to reduce the use of CompositionLocals by
removing the call to `.value` from the default color methods.

instances instead of calling the CompositionLocal for each invocation.

commit_message:
Migrate `ViewModelProvider` class to KMP

* `ViewModelProvider`, all nested types and classes with cyclic dependency have been converted to Kotlin Multiplatform.
* All methods that expects a `java.lang.Class<T>` have been converted to `expect`/`actual`, and received a new `KClass<T>` overload.
* A new constructor for `ViewModelProvider` has been added to avoid breaking binary compatibility with the Android source: the original Android source would generate overloaded constructors and a public synthetic constructor with last parameter as `DefaultConstructorMarker`, but when combining `JvmOverloads` with `expect`/`actual`, the synthetic constructor has not been generated. That is a binary compatibility.
  * The `@JvmOverload` constructor has been left as an Android-only API for now.
* Kotlin Multiplatform does not support expect class with default implementation yet. Hence, the common logic has been extracted to an internal `ViewModelProviderImpl` (for stateful logic) and `ViewModelProviders` (for stateless logic).

RelNote: "`ViewModelProvider` is now Kotlin Multiplatform. To accommodate this change, compatibility methods have been added for handling `java.lang.Class<T>` and `kotlin.reflect.KClass<T>`."

commit_message:
Refactor and formalize states to handle extensions transactions

This CL aims to fundamentally address the issue stated below:

1. Currently, every use case transaction is done synchronously, meaning
   that every attach/detach/reset/activate/deactivate call is handled
   in their entirety, with all UseCaseCamera components and CameraGraph
   created before the return of the calls (except for concurrent camera
   use cases, but the API guarantees make it more manageable).
2. However, extensions requires us to get the Surfaces from
   DeferrableSurfaces before initializing the session, and it cannot be
   done or block the main thread. That means the use case transactions
   can no longer be done synchronously.

This CL addresses this by having the following major changes:

1. Add necessary and delicate synchronization mechanisms so that we can
   safely defer the creation of the new set of UseCaseCamera components.
2. Add a formal finite state machine in SessionProcessorManager so that
   every call to SessionProcessor follows their correct lifetime.

The detailed mechanisms are included in comments, but the high level
idea is that:

1. Say, we started initializing for a new set of use cases with
   extensions enabled.
2. If a new set of use cases is attached or detached, we "close" the
   current SessionProcessorManager (de-initialize it correctly) and
   create a new one that supercedes the current session.
3. If a new use case is activated (marked as active) or detactivated,
   ignore it since the use cases are going to be refreshed after the
   initialization finishes anyway.

Bug: 324321553
Test: Run the test
Change-Id: I0a8802704019aa51d16e8491d36b722439aee2ba
llm_reason: The commit refactors session management to handle asynchronous transactions properly, introducing synchronization mechanisms and a finite state machine. This addresses potential concurrency issues by ensuring thread safety and correct ordering of operations.

commit_message:
Fix SwipeToDismissBox sometimes not intercepting swipes.

Implements `canScrollVertically` and `canScrollHorizontally` in
`AndroidComposeView` to support the `SwipeDismissLayout` at the top of
WearOS windows when it asks if any views in the tree are scrollable to
determine whether to intercept touch events for swipe detection. Because
the `canScroll*` methods don't respond to a particular offset and are
meant to query the view as a whole, the initial down event is recorded
as the position to check for scrollability. This works because
`ScrollDismissLayout` only asks its children if they're scrollable after
touch slop has been reached.

On the Compose side, the semantics tree is used to determine whether
there is a scrollable node at a given offset in the compose view. The
Wear-specific implementation of `Swipeable` is given fake scrollable
semantics to support this.

This entire solution is admittedly pretty hacky, but it should unblock
basic swipe navigation in WearOS for now.

Test: Tests added for Wear `Swipeable`, `AndroidComposeView`, and
`AndroidComposeViewAccessibilityDeletgateCompat`.
Fixes: b/199908428
Relnote: "Fix WearOS SwipeToDismissBox sometimes not handling swipes."

Change-Id: I9387e6584e9d04d7ffda3a4319e3da2da13788b5
llm_reason: The changes primarily address a functional issue related to swipe handling on WearOS by modifying how scrollability is determined. The code modifications do not introduce or fix any of the defined code smells such as energy, concurrency, storage bloat, security vulnerabilities, or high complexity debt.

commit_message:
Move type converters to be one way

Previously, TypeConverters were enforced to be 2 way which does not make
sense in all cases because it might be necessary just for reading or just
for writing.

This CL refactors the TypeAdapterStore to make them one way. This opens up
the path for potentially serializing / deserializing a value via different
paths.

This should usually be OK but to avoid possible issues, for adapters, we
try to reverse the forward path (the path that takes user's data into
database). If that works, we use it. If not, we try to find a path from the
last node of forward path to the user's value.

For query parameter adapters, we don't really need the logic to read from
cursor. To support then, I've divided ColumnTypeAdapter into 2 interfaces.
StatementValueBinder, CursorValueReader.

TypeAdapterStore now has a specific function to get StatementValueBinder
which does not search for the path to read a cursor value. A similar logic
will be introduced for fields of pojos.

Bug: 32342709
Bug: 34610784
Test: existing tests pass + TypeAdapterStoreTest
Change-Id: I09789e4c0e97debaaa1d9ce422437f401d8fd310
llm_reason: The changes refactor type converters to be one-way, reducing code complexity by separating concerns and improving maintainability. This simplifies the conversion logic and makes it easier to extend or modify individual paths.

commit_message:

commit_message:
Remove `hintMediaTypes` from `Modifier.contentReceiver`

ReceiveContent framework consolidates 3 different input systems through a single API. It's expensive to filter out incoming items according to their media type since it requires doing at least 2 IPC calls. Therefore `Modifier.contentReceiver` does not have an enforced "accepted media types" parameter. However, it required a `hintMediaTypes` due to the existing contract between IMEs and the app where apps can hint to the IME what type of content that they support so that the IME can hide unsupported functionality.

This CL removes `hintMediaTypes` since a sanity check is always recommended for the received item. Even if the received content's source happens to be IME, there's still no guarantee that the IME sent an appropriate content.

We will re-evaluate the hinting capability on the API if we see enough demand in the future.

Fix: b/329643432
Test: Updated tests
Relnote: "Removed `hintMediaTypes` parameter from `Modifier.contentReceiver`. Developers were already encouraged to check the received `TransferableContent`'s media type for themselves."
Change-Id: I82f995187934e7d62ff2c6312869964ba7ca3b6a
llm_reason: The changes remove a parameter and update related code, but do not introduce or fix any code smells.

commit_message: Never save passwords for this site: Update Save Dialog UX and Password Management Screen (#3999) Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/0/1205190310390165/f ### Description This stack of PRs will offer users a way of saying to never ask to save a password for this site again. This PR updates the `Save Password` prompt to include a `Never Ask for This Site` option: - If the user dismisses the dialog using the ✖️ button, the back button or by tapping outside of the dialog, then we treat that as a "not now" action (same as before this PR) - If the user taps on `Never Ask for This Site` button, we securely store the e-tld+1 for the site so that we won't prompt them to save a password for this site again In addition, we update the password management screen (`Settings->Passwords`) such that if we have >= 0 never saved sites, then we add to the overflow menu to allow the user to reset the list. We do not provide any way for the user to see the list of sites that are excluded or to individually delete them; it's a global reset only. ## Save prompt ## Resetting never saved list from management screen ### Steps to test this PR #### Never save prompt, dismissing using the various "not now" options - [ ] Visit https://fill.dev/form/login-simple - [ ] Enter username and password (>= 4 characters) and tap Login button - [ ] Dismiss the dialog using ✖️ button. repeat steps to visit site and enter details. Verify the prompt shows again. - [ ] Dismiss the dialog using back button. repeat steps to visit site and enter details. Verify the prompt shows again. - [ ] Dismiss the dialog by tapping outside the dialog. repeat steps to visit site and enter details. Verify the prompt shows again. #### Never save prompt, choosing "never again" option - [ ] Visit https://fill.dev/form/login-simple - [ ] Enter username and password (>= 4 characters) and tap Login button - [ ] Tap on the `Never Ask for This Site` button - [ ] Go back and refresh the page. Enter username and password (>= 4 characters) and tap Login button. - [ ] Verify there is no prompt to save again for this site. ℹ️ The below tests all require fill.dev to be on the "never save" list, which should be the case if you've followed the tests in order. #### Registration, password generation disabled - [ ] Visit https://fill.dev/form/registration-username - [ ] Verify there is no 🗝 icon on the password fields - [ ] Verify that we do not offer to generate a password when they are tapped/focused #### Email protection - [ ] Login into email protection, then visit https://fill.dev/form/registration-email - [ ] Tap the Dax icon on the email field, then choose `Generate private duck address` - [ ] Visit `Overflow->Passwords` and verify that the private duck address **did not** get auto-saved as a login #### Password management screen, resetting list - [ ] Visit password management screen (`Settings->Passwords`) and verify the overflow menu is visible, and that `Reset Excluded Sites` is the only option - [ ] Choose it, and verify the confirmation dialog appears - [ ] Verify that dialog works as expected when you `Cancel` it - [ ] Repeat to see the dialog again, and this time choose to `Reset` - [ ] Verify the overflow menu disappears (as per business logic since there are no excluded sites to reset) - [ ] Exit the management screen and return to it; verify the overflow menu is still gone
llm_reason: The changes primarily focus on adding a new feature to allow users to never save passwords for specific sites. The modifications include UI updates, dialog handling, and repository interactions but do not introduce or fix any code smells as per the provided categories.

commit_message:
Correctly fixup linked list nodes after tombstone cleanup

When a SieveCache contained enough tombstones to require
dropDeletes() to run and if the entries have hash collisions,
the existing code could corrupt its internal mapping of
older indices to new indices post rehash.

Instead of tracking a simple mapping of previous/new index
to be able to fixup the nodes linked list, this change
tracks a more complex mapping consisting of a pair of
(source, destination) indices per entry. When entries are
moved around the arrays, we use the source mapping to go
update the original mapping to properly track where the
value ends up at the end of the rehash.

See comments in dropDeletes() for more details.

The new behavior is validated by a new unit test that
triggers numerous hash collisions. The test fails without
this CL.

This change also fix the way values were swapped in the arrays
for all "scatter" collections to avoid "leaking" keys.

commit_message:
Take min dimensions in DP for SplitPairRule/SplitPlaceholderRule

Before, we take minWidth, minHeight, minSmallestWidth in pixels, which
doesn't get updated when the display density is changed. Now, we take
them in DP, so that we can convert it to pixels later with up-to-date
density.

Also, refactor to take minWidth, minHeight, minSmallestWidth as
optional for SplitRule Builder so that:
1. Allow the app to use the library default value if not set.
2. Allow us to introduce more bounds related options in the future.

Relnote: "
1. Refactor SplitRule#minWidth/minHeight/minSmallestWidth to
   SplitRule#minWidthDp/minHeightDp/minSmallestWidthDp to take DP
   instead of pixels.
2. For SplitPairRule/SplitPlaceholderRule Builder, introduce
   Builder#setMinWidthDp, Builder#setMinHeightDp and
   Builder#setMinSmallestWidthDp to take min dimensions as optional.
"
Bug: 229656253
Test: pass existing

Change-Id: I95f17ce89844d18708e6fb03924d38a00af115ac
llm_reason: The changes refactor the code to use DP units consistently, replace setters with optional parameters, and simplify method calls. This reduces redundancy and improves maintainability.
