commit_message:
Rename CompositionLifecycleObserver to RememberObserver

This change effectively renames CompositionLifecycleObserver
to RememberObserver by introducing the new interface and
deprecating the old one.

Test: CompositionTests
Fixes: b/177330384
Relnote: """RememberObserver replaces CompositionLifecycleObserver
and CompositionLifecycleObserver is now deprecated.

`RememberObserver` is a replacement for
`CompositionLifecycleObserver` with modified semantics and
renamed methods. Changing to the new API can be done mechanically
for objects that are only remembered once which is, and continues to
be, the recommended practice. Howevever, if a reference was
remembered more than once in a composition `onRemembered` is called
for each reference where `onEnter` is only called once. `onEnter`
was called multiple time if the object was used in subcompositions,
such as  `WithConstraints` and `Scaffold` making the single
`onEnter` call guarantee unreliable and it was removed for
`RememberObserver`.

`RememberObserver` adds `onAbandoned` which is called if the
`RememberObserver` instance is returned from the callback passed
to `remember` but was not remembered in the composition state
and, therefore, will never have `onRemembered` called. This can
occur if an exception terminates composition before completing or
the composition is discarded because the state is was producing
a composition for is no longer current or otherwise is no longer
needed. If the instance of `RememberObserver` following the single
reference recommendation above is tracking an external resource
both `onForgotten` and `onAbandoned` each indicate that the
resource is no longer needed. If the object is tracking work
started or resources allocated in `onRemembered`,  `onAbandoned`
can be ignored as it will not be called if `onRemembered` is
called.
"""

Change-Id: I02c364f517507abd6a5c071fb527192ad1d77239
llm_reason: The commit renames CompositionLifecycleObserver to RememberObserver, updating all references and deprecating the old interface. This refactoring simplifies the API by reducing redundancy and improving code clarity.

commit_message:
Add Aura experiment (#5343)

Task/Issue URL:
https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1208893765398547/f

### Description
Sets the ATB variant and origin for Aura installs in order to measure
retention.

### Steps to test this PR

_Point at the JSON Blob linked in the task_

_Feature enabled and new user Aura install_
- [x] Check out this branch
- [x] Change `installationSourceModern()` in
`RealInstallSourceExtractor` to return an Aura package (e.g.
`com.sec.android.app.samsungapps`)
- [x] On a fresh emulator, freshly install the app
- [x] Filter logcat by `Initialized ATB`
- [x] Verify that the variant is `mq`
- [x] Filter logcat by `m_android_install`
- [x] Verify that `origin=funnel_app_aurapaid_android`
- [x] Verify that `reinstall=false`

_Feature enabled and returning user Aura install_
- [x] On the same emulator, uninstall and freshly install the app
- [x] Filter logcat by `Initialized ATB`
- [x] Verify that the variant is `mq`
- [x] Filter logcat by `m_android_install`
- [x] Verify that `origin=funnel_app_aurapaid_android`
- [x] Verify that `reinstall=true`

_Feature disabled and new user Aura install_
- [x] Disable `auraExperiment` in the config
- [x] On a fresh emulator, freshly install the app
- [x] Filter logcat by `Initialized ATB`
- [x] Verify that the variant is set normally (Not `mq`)
- [x] Filter logcat by `m_android_install`
- [x] Verify that `origin=funnel_app_aurapaid_android` is **not** sent
- [x] Verify that `reinstall=false`

_Feature disabled and returning user Aura install_
- [x] On the same emulator, uninstall and freshly install the app
- [x] Filter logcat by `Initialized ATB`
- [x] Verify that the variant is set normally (Not `mq`)
- [x] Filter logcat by `m_android_install`
- [x] Verify that `origin=funnel_app_aurapaid_android` is **not** sent
- [x] Verify that `reinstall=true`

_Feature enabled and new user install (Not Aura)_
- [x] Re-enable `auraExperiment` in the config
- [x] Change `installationSourceModern()` in
`RealInstallSourceExtractor` to return something else (e.g.

commit_message:
Convert SimpleArrayMap to Kotlin

This also removes the global array cache which provided dubious
performance value while allowing unsychronized access to possibly
corrupt other instances of this class.

Relnote: "Convert SimpleArrayMap to Kotlin. This change introduces a few
  incompatible changes, as a result of Java-Kotlin interop and the
  ability to correctly define nullity of types in the source.
    * The package private APIs, `.mSize`, `.mArray`, `.mHashes`,
      `.indexOf()`, `.indexOfNull()`, and `.indexOfValue()`, were made
      private - this is technically a binary incompatible change, but
      reflects the intended visibility of these fields and is the closest we
      can achieve in Kotlin since it does not include a way to specifiy
      package-private visibility.
    * The nullity of some types are now properly defined, the affected
      methods are: `.getOrDefault`, `.keyAt`, `.valueAt`, `.setValueAt`,
      `.put`, `.putIfAbsent`, `.removeAt`, `.replace`.
    * For Kotlin users, `.isEmpty()` is now only available as a function instead of
      also through property access."

commit_message:
Add support for arg constructors

This CL adds constructor support to Pojo and Entity processors.
We try to map each constructor parameter to a field based on
its name. If there are conflicts, we fail the compilation.
If there are multiple matching constructors, we fail the compilation
and developer can annotate unwanted ones with @Ignore to workaround
the issue.

This CL also prioritizes PojoRowAdapter over EntityRowAdapter since
it is faster and proguard does a good job on shrinking the generated
code.

Bug: 36473441

This PR refactors permissions so the ones that can be accepted or denied
automatically are done within the module rather than the fragment.

### Steps to test this PR

_Camera/mic deny_
- [x] Go to http://webcamera.io/
- [x] Deny the camera/mic permissions
- [x] Go to Settings --> Permissions --> Site Permissions
- [x] Verify webcamera.io is **not** under "Manage Sites"

_Camera/mic allow_
- [x] Go to http://webcamera.io/
- [x] Allow the camera/mic permissions
- [x] Go to Settings --> Permissions --> Site Permissions
- [x] Verify webcamera.io is under "Manage Sites" and is set to ask for
all permissions

_Camera/mic allow within 24h_
- [x] Go to Settings --> Permissions --> Site Permissions
- [x] Tap on webcamera.io under "Manage Sites"
- [x] Set microphone permission to "Allow"
- [x] Reload the web on the same tab
- [x] Check all permissions are granted without prompt (camera should
work)
- [x] Close the tab
- [x] Go to http://webcamera.io/ on a new tab
- [x] Deny the camera permission (this can trigger the website to re-ask
for the permission several times)
- [x] Camera should not work.
- [x] Reload the site and this time accept the camera permission.
- [x] Camera should work.

_Camera/mic always allow_
- [x] Go to Settings --> Permissions --> Site Permissions
- [x] Tap on webcamera.io under "Manage Sites"
- [x] Set camera permission to "Allow"
- [x] Open a new tab
- [x] Go to http://webcamera.io/ and check permission is granted without
prompt

_Camera/mic always deny
- [x] Go to Settings --> Permissions --> Site Permissions
- [x] Tap on webcamera.io under "Manage Sites"
- [x] Set camera permission to "Deny"
- [x] Go to http://webcamera.io/ and check permission is not granted
(camera doesn't work and you are not asked)

_Session-based Consent_
- [x] Load https://shaka-player-demo.appspot.com/demo/ - DRM consent
prompt should appear
- [x] Select  "Only for this Session"
- [x] Verify that you are able to play the 3rd video, Sintel (it's
encrypted with Widevine)
- [x] Reload the page and verify that you are still able to play the
video and no dialog appears
- [x] Open a new tab and go to
https://shaka-player-demo.appspot.com/demo/
- [x] Consent prompt should appear - select "Deny for this Session"
- [x] Verify that the 3rd video, Sintel, is not available
- [x] Reload the page - video should still be unavailable and no consent
prompt should appear

_Random tests_
- [x] Go to https://permission.site
- [x] If not already change to https
- [x] Click on microphone and allow, should be green.
- [x] Click on camera and deny, should be red
- [x] Click on camera and microphone
- [x] Should ask you about camera permission (mic was already accepted)
- [x] Deny and should be red
- [x] Click again, should ask for camera and allow. Should be green.
llm_reason: The changes refactor permissions handling by moving logic from fragments to modules, reducing code duplication and improving modularity. This simplifies the overall structure and makes the codebase easier to maintain.

commit_message:
Add ability to customize LazyLayout prefetching strategy

Our goal is to enable testing of different prefetching strategies, including what to prefetch and when that work is scheduled. This diff refactors the existing logic behind a strategy pattern, maintaining the current choices of how much/when as the default strategy.

The existing logic is split between LazyListState (+ LazyGridState, etc), which decides *what* to prefetch, and LazyLayoutPrefetcher, which decides *when* to execute it.

A summary of changes:
- LazyList accepts an optional LazyListPrefetchStrategy falling back to DefaultLazyListPrefetchStrategy which uses the existing logic.
- LazyListPrefetchStrategy contains LazyList specific callbacks that a strategy can use to decide how to manage prefetching (currently onScroll and onVisibleItemsChanged)
- LazyListPrefetchStrategy extends the LazyLayoutPrefetchStrategy interface, which allows LazyLayout to connect a PrefetchContext to the strategy for it to execute prefetches. This is analogous to how LazyLayout connects the LazyLayoutPrefetcher to a LazyLayoutPrefetchState before this diff.
- PrefetchContext gives access to the precompose API from SubcomposeLayout instead of exposing how to scheduling a prefetch - this was done to allow a strategy to determine its own scheduling logic
- LazyLayoutPrefetcher mostly stays as is, except it's given a PrefetchContext instead of setting/unsetting itself in onRemembered/onForgotten and is directly used from the default prefetch strategy.

I didn't try to implement correct logic for anything besides LazyList and didn't clean up LazyLayoutPrefetchState, just to get feedback on this approach first.

I also uploaded https://android-review.googlesource.com/c/platform/frameworks/support/+/2817620 as an example of custom logic using the API to change the amount to prefetch and when to execute prefetch.

Test: * Rotate sample app with custom PrefetchStrategy. LazyList still uses chosen PrefetchStrategy after rotation.
* Run TrivialListScrollBenchmark without custom strategy, see no changes
* Run TrivialListScrollBenchmark with custom strategy/executor, see potential perf wins when rows can take over a frame to execute.
* Unit tests in this patch

Relnote: Adds an experimental API for configuring prefetch behavior of LazyLists.

Change-Id: I022a469c6d89d8742511cf10715afeced732d27e
llm_reason: The changes refactor the existing logic into a strategy pattern, making it more modular and testable. This reduces code duplication and improves maintainability by allowing different prefetching strategies to be easily implemented.

commit_message:
App bar simplification

Providing built-in support for the number of actions available in app bars seemed like a nice idea at the time, but it ended up being restrictive and resulting in an overly complicated API surface for both the simple case, and anything morecustom.

This CL removes the concept of 'actionData' and 'action', greatly reducing complexity of the majority of use cases.

Handling different orientations of start / end icons in a Scaffold when the FAB position changes is also fairly simple, as you can just use the fab position directly and change the order of items emitted into the BottomAppBar.

Overall I think this makes app bar APIs easier to understand and also more powerful.

Bug: b/148049128
Test: AppBarTest
Change-Id: I5bfee975b85a674ace23a6f256492f34aa9229ea
llm_reason: The commit simplifies the app bar APIs by removing 'actionData' and 'action', reducing complexity. It introduces a more straightforward approach with clear separation of concerns, making the code easier to understand and maintain.

commit_message:
Implementing DirectBoot support in DataStore.

This CL creates APIs that allow a developer to create a DataStore that can be used during direct boot mode, while guarding against any attempt to utilize the existing APIs during direct boot mode (where possible).

There are 2 main paths in creating a DataStore in the current existing API. These are:
[1] Through a DataStoreFactory `create()` function call.
[2] Through a DataStoreDelegate `dataStore()` (which eventually delegates to Pathway [1] via a new DataStoreFile creation).

This CL creates 2 new API paths reserved only for use during direct boot mode. These are:
[1] Through a DataStoreFactory `createInDeviceProtectedStorage()` function call.
[2] Through a DataStoreDelegate `deviceProtectedDataStore()` (which eventually delegates to Pathway [1] via a new DeviceProtectedDataStoreFile creation).

As directBoot support requires the presence of a DeviceProtectedStorageContext in order to create the DataStore file, this change introduces APIs that can access the context and use it for direct boot mode after verification. Direct Boot mode APIs require a minimum of SDK level 24, therefore these APIs are restricted to this API level.

In addition, tests in the jvmAndroidTest source set had to undergo a small refactor as the source set structure does not allow tests in jvmAndroidTest visibility into the jvmMain and androidMain source sets (as it will only see jvmAndroidMain sources), which required changes to how we call DataStoreFactory.create in these existing tests to avoid duplicating each test in each platform's test source sets.

Bug: 370838564
Test: DirectBootDataStoreFactoryTest.kt, DirectBootPreferenceDataStoreFactoryTest.kt, DataStoreDelegateTest.kt
Relnote: Implementing DirectBoot support in DataStore.
Change-Id: Ib90e56ef92a04edcf81d779f4cc0d1ca3ee2495b
llm_reason: The changes introduce new APIs for Direct Boot support without addressing existing code smells. The modifications primarily add functionality and refactor tests, but do not resolve issues like energy consumption, concurrency problems, storage inefficiencies, security vulnerabilities, or high complexity debt.

commit_message:
Reuse nodes across subcompose layout boundary

Enables reuse for subcompose layout node triggering reuse instead of dispose whenever composition is put inside ReusableContent. Subcomposition dispose is tied to `onDetach` of the corresponding node instead, which is triggered when node exits composition entirely.

Reusing nodes should improve performance in subcompose layouts inside lazy containers.

Fixes: 252846775
Test: LazyColumnTest/SubcomposeLayoutTest
Change-Id: If2df818639223abfa9d8d1e48af74b8f9e3ceeb1
llm_reason: The changes aim to optimize performance by reusing nodes across subcompose layout boundaries, reducing unnecessary recomposition and improving efficiency in lazy containers.

commit_message:
Open built-in serializers for Interface types

Make `CharSequenceSerializer`, `JavaSerializableSerializer`, and `ParcelableSerializer` open generic classes instead of singleton objects so that users can inherit and define serializers for their own subtypes. This can be used to prevent `SERIALIZER_TYPE_INCOMPATIBLE` lint warning. For example, instead of suppressing the warning like this:

```
@Serializable
data class CharSequenceContainer(
    @Suppress("SERIALIZER_TYPE_INCOMPATIBLE")
    @Serializable(with = CharSequenceSerializer::class)
    val value: String
)
```

Users can do:

```
class StringAsCharSequenceSerializer : CharSequenceSerializer<String>()

@Serializable
data class CharSequenceContainer(
    @Serializable(with = StringAsCharSequenceSerializer::class)
    val value: String
)
```

The serializers can also be used directly with subclasses. For example, users can now write:

```
encodeToSavedState(serializer = CharSequenceSerializer(), value = "foo")
```

Since CharSequenceSerializer is now generic, it works with value `foo` even though it has a static type of String.

We also made some changes to all the built-in serializers:

- Changeed all built-in serializers from singleton objects to classes so that they're consistent with `CharSequenceSerializer`, `JavaSerializableSerializer`, and `ParcelableSerializer`.

- Moved the actual `getXXX()`/`putXXX()` from `platformSpecificEncodeSerializableValue`/`platformSpecificDecodeSerializableValue` to `serialize()`/`deserialize()` functions of these serializers. This way we don't require special logic in `SavedStateEncoder.encodeSerializableValue()`/`SavedStateDecoder.decodeSerializableValue()`.

Relnote: Open built-in serializers for Interface types
Bug: 376026712
Test: SavedStateCodecAndroidTest
Change-Id: I28caf279b53cb69311c861e415390da7efd90c40
llm_reason: The changes refactor built-in serializers from singleton objects to open generic classes, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability by allowing inheritance for custom serializers. This simplifies the API and reduces boilerplate.

commit_message:
Introduce ComplicationDataTimeline

Under the hood ComplicationDataTimeline is stored within a
ComplicationData's bundle, if we where starting from a green
field project this is not how we would have designed this but its
necessary given the need for backwards compatiability.

We could expose the timeline on the androidx ComplicationData
classes but chose not to because the resulting API would be
confusing. Instead wear-watchface transparently handles the
timeline overrides.

Note we also chose not to add this to
onSynchronousComplicationRequest becuase tha's really meant for
fast updates which is the opposite use case.

Test: Manual testing
Bug: 196958807
Relnote: We've added ComplicationDataTimeline to wear-complication-data-source this can be used to provide a sequence of time-gated ComplicationData to be delivered to the watch face which can be cached and updated automatically. E.g. today's weather forecast at various times or multiple upcoming calendar events. ComplicationRequestListener has been extended with a new method onComplicationDataTimeline which you can use to return this data.  There's a new kotlin wrapper SuspendingTimelineComplicationDataSourceService for suspending data source services.
Change-Id: Idecdc0c92a52ff9591b7a84e500986d4817719be
llm_reason: The commit introduces a new class ComplicationDataTimeline and refactors existing code to handle time-gated complication data, reducing complexity by encapsulating timeline logic and improving maintainability.

Reduce allocations

- Optimize ScatterMap's Map wrappers to remove intermediate allocations.
  They are also more efficient when accessing the parent ScatterMap instead
  of doing multiple getfield calls because of the nested classes.
- Pre-size collections with adequate capacity to avoid allocations
- Allocate stateless allocators only once
- Extract local function to top-level to avoid allocations caused by captures
- Use scatter maps where possible

commit_message:
Support autocomplete recent item removal (#4861)

Task/Issue URL:
https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1207977618326502/f

### Description
Adds the ability to delete recent items from the autocomplete list.

### Steps to test this PR

_Remove query_
- [x] Search for something (eg. “dogs”)
- [x] Clear the search and type the first few letters
- [x] Long press the recent item
- [x] Verify that the keyboard is hidden and the dialog is shown
- [x] Press “Remove"
- [x] Verify that the `m_autocomplete_result_deleted` pixel is sent
- [x] Verify that the item is deleted
- [x] Verify that the keyboard is shown

_Remove URL_
- [x] Visit a URL (eg. “dog.com”)
- [x] Clear the search and type the first few letters
- [x] Long press the recent item
- [x] Verify that the keyboard is hidden and the dialog is shown
- [x] Press “Remove"
- [x] Verify that the `m_autocomplete_result_deleted` pixel is sent
- [x] Verify that the item is deleted
- [x] Verify that the keyboard is shown

_Cancel_
- [x] Do the same thing and press “Cancel" or tap outside the dialog
- [x] Verify that the item is not deleted

_Widget_
- [x] Add the widget
- [x] Follow the same steps as _Remove query_, _Remove URL_ and _Cancel_

### UI changes
| Browser  | Widget |
| ------ | ----- |

![remove_history](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3479df8-8e39-4389-aac1-6e3d5ae93527)|![remove_history_widget](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f7c5630f-e11b-4e5f-afea-005cf517e39e)

commit_message:
Monthly button refactoring

- Removes string overload for Button
- Replaces ButtonStyle with distinct functions, the following is the
migration strategy:

ContainedButtonStyle -> Button
OutlinedButtonStyle -> OutlinedButton
TextButtonStyle -> TextButton
Custom ButtonStyle -> Any / which ever is most semantically related

overloads, there is no more large separation between layers - extra
customization is just a matter of supplying more parameters, instead of
needing to find overloads / copy and paste parameters from high level

commit_message:
Removes lazilyCreateIndication parameter

This parameter conflates behavior changes for different use cases, and is a bit misleading. Indication will continue to be created lazily if a null interaction source is provided, and the indication is of type IndicationNodeFactory. Explicitly hoisting an interaction source will disable the lazily creation. Opting into lazy creation for cases where you are hoisting the interaction source but only reading from it will be considered separately in the future.

Bug: b/298048146
Test: tests
Relnote: N/A
Change-Id: I795afdf1c49402def71c87c9de57232444ea57c1
llm_reason: The commit removes the `lazilyCreateIndication` parameter, which was causing unnecessary creation of indication nodes. By defaulting to lazy creation when appropriate, it reduces memory usage and improves performance by deferring node creation until needed.

commit_message:
Kotlin runtime initial structure (#2256)

* Introduce GraphQL execution context

Provide type safe container to customize and extend `Response` with additional context, such as providing access to raw OkHttp response.

Closes https://github.com/apollographql/apollo-android/issues/2205

* Feedback

* Kotlin runtime initial structure

Introduce new `apollo-runtime-kotlin` module with initial structure:
- `ApolloClient` similar to JVM version, a facade for user interface
- `RequestExecutor` is a business logic layer responsible for query execution. Allows to create a chain / pipeline of several executors. Each executor observes, modifies, and potentially short-circuits GraphQL operation requests. `NetworkExecutor` is a final / terminal request executor in the chain, responsible for network execution via configured `NetworkTransport`
- `NetworkTransport` is an abstraction for networking. Apollo is going to provide several `NetworkTransport` implementations: Http(JVM/iOS) and WebSocket(JVM/iOS)
- `DispatchersContext` provides configuration for coroutine dispatchers, that will be put into `ExecutionContext`

Part of https://github.com/apollographql/apollo-android/issues/2222

* Network layer + update sample

* Address feedback

* iOS ApolloHttpNetworkTransportTest

* Check if flow is still active before dispatch

* Wrap dispatching GraphQL response with `runCatching`

* Address issue with `callbackFlow`. When http request is completed we call `producer#close` that cancels OkHttp call (via `awaitClose {call.cancel}`) that closes http response before we parse it.

Replace callbackFlow with regular `flow` + `suspendCancellableCoroutine`

Fix Android sample

Rename `NetworkExecutor` to `NetworkInterceptor`
llm_reason: The commit refactors the code structure by introducing a new module and abstracting network transport, which reduces complexity and improves maintainability.

commit_message:
Remove dependency on ObserverMap

Relnote: "Added OwnerScope to allow collection of
layout and drawing observation scopes once they are
no longer valid."

SnapshotStateObserver no longer depends on ObserverMap.
SnapshotStateObserver uses a simpler unidirectional map
from value to set of Scopes.

Since the new map doesn't use weak references, it is
more careful about removing entries from the map.

Ordered lists (IdentityArraySet and IdentityScopeMap)
are used for set/maps.

Both layout and drawing performance has improved because
anything outside composition that reads state data
has better performance.

Nearly all performance benchmarks have improved, but
notification is slower. It does more in this
version -- it clears the scopes associated with a view.
allocations have been substantially reduced as well.
The offset in the model observation makes this a
valuable tradeoff.

Bug: 168243987
Bug: 168489720
Bug: 147516363

Test: SnapshotStateObserverBenchmark
Test: CheckboxesInRowsBenchmark
Test: RectsInColumnBenchmark
Change-Id: Ic4cf8889e486e175e0f2405f1a0dc7a5a085ad99

commit_message:
Introduce Compose stack traces based on source information

Appends a trace of @Composable functions retrieved from a slot table traversal as a suppressed exception on composition crash (coverage of other phases in the following commits).

The trace frames are based on the source information which has an associated performance cost. Enabling this feature might regress performance of the app.

Note that Compose also ships with a minifier config hat removes source information from the release builds. Enabling this feature in minified builds will have no effect.

Test: ComposeErrorTraceTests
Bug: 354163858
Relnote: """
Introduce diagnostic Compose stack traces based on source information stored in composition.
"""

Change-Id: I3db9fb976f61c768e2d1cf11d69019e4d0a08330
llm_reason: The commit introduces a feature that appends stack traces based on source information, which has an associated performance cost. Enabling this feature might regress app performance as noted in the commit message.

commit_message:
Cache keys for lazy layouts

Currently it is possible that the key lambda passed to LazyColumn or other lazy layouts will be invoked multiple types for each item. It is not ideal for performance as sometimes the implementations of such lambdas are allocating new non trivial objects. Now we will be caching the keys near the currently visible range and only re-execute the lambdas when something changes.

Test: new tests for each lazy layout
Change-Id: I54ad3157ae41fbc6e19d8a840aec4bfcb9fa089a
llm_reason: The commit introduces caching of keys for lazy layouts to reduce unnecessary recomputation, improving performance by minimizing lambda executions and object allocations.

commit_message:
Introduce TextMotion, an experimental TextStyle parameter to control how glyphs are rendered at pixel precision.

TextMotion comes with 2 modes; Static(default) and Animated. Static text is rendered for maximum readability and legibility on all screens from Low DPI to Ultra High DPI. Animated text tweaks the rendering for maximum linearity and enables subpixel positioning for smooth scale, translation, and rotation animations.

TextMotion is designed for multi-platform. Supported platforms for Compose can use simple to complex APIs to provide Static or Animated text rendering.  Android initially hides the flags that are used to implement Animated text. These include `LINEAR_TEXT_FLAG`, `SUBPIXEL_TEXT_FLAG` and `hinting` option in TextPaint.

```
TextMotion.Static:
- LINEAR_TEXT_FLAG=off
- SUBPIXEL_TEXT_FLAG=off
- hinting=on

TextMotion.Animated:
- LINEAR_TEXT_FLAG=on
- SUBPIXEL_TEXT_FLAG=on
- hinting=off
```

Relnote: "Added experimental TextMotion to TextStyle to define Text either to be Static(default) or Animated. Use `TextMotion.Animated` if Text is going to be scaled, translated, or rotated via animation."
Test: compose:ui:ui-text:cAT
Fix: b/251185997
Change-Id: I24dd75e606184220ed3eebc3c80f84d5c977c14c
llm_reason: The commit introduces TextMotion to optimize text rendering for animations, reducing CPU usage by enabling subpixel positioning and linear text flags. This improves performance during scaling, translation, or rotation of text elements.

commit_message:
Stop/restart VPN when bad health is detected in internal builds (#1665)


Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1201629177365358/f
* https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1201641519620933/f
* https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1201641519620969/f
* https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1201653659122893/f

### Description
As part of the effort to understand bad health conditions in the VPN and test mitigataions. This PR adds the functionality of automatically restarting the VPN when bad health is detected.

* The automatic restart only happens in internal builds
* We add an internal feature to enable/disable bad health monitoring

In addition this PR:
* Fixed bug that make some health rates to go above 100%
* Adjusts the sensitivity of some alerts
* Streamlines the packet processing in the UDP pipeline
* Splits TCP/UDP health metrics

Incidentally this PR fixes the bug described in [here](https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1201653659122893/f). Edge case around cohort calculation.

### Steps to test this PR

_Test Internal builds_
- [x] fresh install internal build
- [x] open app and enable AppTP
- [x] go to settings -> appTP settings -> diagnostics
- [x] tap on `Bad Health` CTA
- [x] verify `Internal flavor detected, restarting the VPN...` message shows in the logcat (can take up to 30s)
- [x] verify `m_atp_health_monitor_report` pixel is sent
- [x] verify `m_atp_health_alert_tunReadAlerts_d` is sent
- [x] verify the next `Internal flavor detected, restarting the VPN...` message shows at least 1min after the one before
- [x] verify **only** `m_atp_health_monitor_report` pixel is sent and NOT `m_atp_health_alert_tunReadAlerts_d`
- [x] go to settings -> appTP settings -> diagnostics
- [x] tap on `Good health` CTA
- [x] verify `No alerts, clearing storage` shows in the logcat (can take up to 30s)

_Test bad health information storing/removal_
- [x] install internal build
- [x] open app and enable AppTP
- [x] go to settings -> appTP settings -> diagnostics
- [x] tap on `Bad Health` CTA
- [x] go to "App Tracking protection" screen and report breakage for any app
- [x] verify the `breakageMetadata` in the `m_atp_breakage_report` pixel contains the bad health state
- [x] go to settings -> appTP settings -> diagnostics
- [x] tap on `Good health` CTA
- [x] go to "App Tracking protection" screen and report breakage for any app
- [x] verify the `breakageMetadata` in the `m_atp_breakage_report` pixel DOES NOT contain the bad health state
llm_reason: The changes simplify and refactor various components, such as removing unnecessary code related to TCP/UDP queue separation, reducing HealthState complexity by eliminating HealthEventType, and streamlining the AppBadHealthStateHandler. These refactors improve maintainability and reduce technical debt.

commit_message:
Introduce background thread text measurement API

Relnote: "Introduced a new feature for priming the text measurement cache on a background thread. It is aimed towards enhancing the performance on LazyLists with medium to large text content. You can enable it by providing a background task scheduler through `LocalBackgroundTextMeasurementExecutor` CompositionLocal. Any descendant BasicText Composable should start sending metrics calculation tasks from composition to the provided executor. commit_message:
Refactor Room Gradle Plugin for lazy configuration

This CL updates the RoomGradlePlugin to adjust for lazy configuration, including schema configurations. There are 3 mayor changes in this CL that all together make the Room Gradle Plugin better work with lazy configurations.

1. The schema configurations are now stored on a DomainObjectSet which enables the plugin to act as schema configuration are added without relying on the timing on how other plugins are configured, i.e. avoid bad patterns like afterEvaluate() or expecting all RoomExtension functions to be called in some withPlugin() lambda. The APIs where also updated to be more Directory centric instead of string based resolving b/279748243.

2. The plugin was incorrectly using tasks.withType() with a lambda that resolved in eager task configuration. This CL updates the plugin to use configureEach which is lazy. However, due to configuring tasks within a configureEach is not permitted (https://github.com/gradle/gradle/issues/27030) the RoomSchemaCopyTask are registered as soon as a user provides a schema configuration and the task itself is then wired when a schema configuration is matched with a variant / target.

3. Updated the schema configuration matching priority to adjust to the laziness of user adding configurations. Specifically the plugin must account that if a user provides a schema configuration that matches all variants and then provides a more specific one (higher priority) the plugin must adjust the registered tasks (see added test testFlavoredProjectPriority() for validation). This is done by storing matches in the CommonIntegration container and later retrieving them when tasks are actually configured.

Bug: 279748243
Bug: 363984118
Test: Existing + manual in sample project
Change-Id: I412d128c70ebab27221d4127294eaa1c4bc4e2e3
llm_reason: The changes refactor the Room Gradle Plugin to use lazy configuration, reducing reliance on eager task configuration and improving code structure by simplifying schema directory management.

commit_message:
Handle reentrant case in Room's acquireTransactionThread()

During a suspending transaction Room uses a thread from the transaction executor, starts an event loop in it and dispatches suspending database operations to it so they are all encapsulated within the transaction coroutine. It is usually expected that the transaction thread is different from the one starting the transaction, but in some cases they are the same. To handle such reentrant cases the `withTransaction()` has been refactored to no longer rely joining on a control job and instead it will execute the suspending transaction block from within the `runBlocking` in the transaction thread.

Executing the suspending transaction block in the `runBlocking` prevents a deadlock situation due to the continuation bouncing between the caller to the transaction thread and back. The deadlock specifically happened when Room started the event loop via `runBlocking`, resumed the `acquireTransactionThread()` coroutine but also joined the `runBlocking` coroutine with the control job. These set of events would cause a deadlock when both the acquire thread and transaction thread are the same because `acquireTransactionThread()` is unable to continue as it is waiting for `runBlocking` to finish which in turns waits for the control job to be done. Meanwhile the job never completes because the database transaction does not finish, i.e. `TransactionElement.release` is never called. By eliminating the need to wait on a control job but still support nested transactions via the transaction context, Room is able to avoid the deadlock situation.

This behaviour was mostly observed in tests where the transaction executor is usually an instant executor, but it could happen in an actual application if Room is configured to share a thread pool executor as the transaction executor and a background coroutine dispatcher and the thread pool is sufficiently busy that only one thread is left for both starting the transaction (to acquire the transaction thread) and the transaction thread itself.

Bug: 251503005
Test: SuspendingQueryTest
Change-Id: I319d977a080b89880d5cf68d139c69a8ba096107
llm_reason: The changes address a deadlock scenario caused by reentrant calls within Room's transaction handling. By refactoring `withTransaction()` to use `runBlocking` and eliminating control job dependencies, the code avoids deadlocks when the same thread is used for both acquiring and executing transactions.

commit_message:
Change the passkey requests's allowHbrid parameter behavior.

The new parameter name is preferImmediatelyAvailableOptions for create
and preferImmediatelyAvailableCredentials for get.

This reflects a recent product behavior change -

Previous behavior:
`allowHybrid` controls whether the hybrid transport should be disallowed
holistically for passkey requests.

New behavior:
`preferImmediatelyAvailableOptions/Credentials` controls whether the
request should immediately stop and return when it finds no immediate
offerings, as opposed to falling back to discovering any potential,
remote options (e.g. QR code, security key).

This allows apps to prevent the situation where no immediate passkeys are
available and the UI still continuously pops up with a single "Use another
device" option that may or may not succeed.

commit_message:
Implement LoadHint -> PageEvent stream

Paging logic to convert a stream of LoadHints from RecyclerView bind
calls to PageEvents, which are incremental updates as pages are fetched.
This is implemented the new Pager, PagerState and PageFetcher.

A Pager is a snapshot / single generation representation of the backing
PagedSource. Converts a stream of LoadHints / bind calls from
RecyclerView into PageEvents by making calls to PagedSource.load. The
stream of PageEvents are exposed via PagedData. A Pager lives within a
job that is cancelled whenever the PagedSource is invalidated.

PagerState holds state that must persist across individual resolution of
LoadHints. It must be accessed with a lock within Pager as it is shared
between async loads (start / end can happen at the same time).

PageFetcher converts a stream of refresh tokens, which are sent whenever
a PagedSource is invalidated, into a stream of PagedData. PageFetcher is
multi-generation across generations of PagedData, created using Pager,
PagerState pairs.

Test: ./gradlew paging:paging-common:test
Change-Id: Iff0c232746592ad0ac8c2c5f908085663a957f72
llm_reason: The commit introduces new functionality to convert LoadHints into PageEvents, which is a feature enhancement rather than addressing any code smells. refactor: Extract `Time` from `Collection` (#11563)

* refactor: extract `time` reference to Singleton

We need to extract `time` from a collection, as we may need
a `time` reference before the collection is open.

This is a first step: move `Collection.time` to refer to a singleton

* Introduce `TimeManager`
* Instantiate `TimeManager` in `CollectionHelper`
* Remove `Time` instance from `Collection` constructor

* refactor: remove `Time` from `CollectionHelper`

* Removes `TimeManager.setInstance`
* No mutations of `TimeManager` in non-test code

For tests: introduce a stack of `Time` instances and use this instead

This stack is reset on each usage of `RobolectricTest`

The final aim of this is to move all `Time` access to `TimeManager`
out of the Collection

* refactor: inline `Collection.time`

and `CollectionHelper.getTimeSafe()`

IDE refactoring
(with some issues due to Kotlin refactoring not affecting Java)

Removes `Time` references from the Collection, this lets us access
`Time` when the Collection may not be openable

This is useful in `AnkiDroidApp`, where a user may not have permission
to access the collection
llm_reason: The commit refactors the code to extract Time from Collection and introduce a singleton TimeManager, reducing dependencies and improving modularity. This change simplifies access to time functionality across multiple components.

Support showing autofill surveys based on remote config (#4981)

Task/Issue URL:
https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1207935899042228/f

### Description
Adds support for consuming autofill surveys from remote config, and
converts the autofill survey UI to make use of the new
`PasswordsScreenPromotionPlugin` framework.

### Steps to test this PR

**Test nothing has broken when no surveys available**
- [x] Fresh install, visit `Passwords` screen
- [x] Verify there is no survey showing. 
- [x] Verify there is no sync promo showing.
- [x] Add a password (manually or through autofill), then return visit
`Passwords` screen again; verify sync promo shows

**Test survey shows when it is available**
- [x] Apply patch to fake a remote config survey being available (see
patch below)
- [x] Launch the app and visit `Passwords` screen; verify the survey
shows
- [x] Verify `m_autofill_management_screen_visit_survey_available` pixel
shows in logs

**Test dismissing the survey**
- [x] Dismiss the survey prompt; verify it goes away
- [x] Verify you see the sync promo (if you still have >= 1 saved
passwords)

**Test taking the survey**
- [x] Either do a fresh install, or use `Settings` -> `Autofill Dev
Settings` -> `Previously Seen Surveys` to reset state
- [x] Visit `Passwords` screen, and tap `Take Survey` button
- [x] Verify it loads in a tab and the survey is shown (don't worry
about the survey contents itself; still being refined)
- [x] Return to the `Passwords` screen; verify the survey prompt does
**not** show

commit_message:
Ensure successful registration before key refresh (#3736)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/0/1205631225739207/f

### Description
Improve re-key so that:
* it happens once a day
* just re-key when the screen is off
* avoids re-keying attempts when device is idle

### Steps to test this PR

_Test force re-key_
- [x] build internal from this branch
- [x] use force rekey in the netp dev settings
- [x] new private key should be registered

_Test rekey_
- apply the following patch
```diff
Index: network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyer.kt
IDEA additional info:
Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP
<+>UTF-8
===================================================================
diff --git a/network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyer.kt b/network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyer.kt
--- a/network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyer.kt	(revision e44ddfce33069ae2ee2227bddffb24cc89049381)
+++ b/network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyer.kt	(date 1698157737720)
@@ -68,7 +68,7 @@
         val forceOrFalseInProductionBuilds = forceRekey.getAndResetValue()
 
         val millisSinceLastKeyUpdate = System.currentTimeMillis() - networkProtectionRepository.lastPrivateKeyUpdateTimeInMillis
-        if (!forceOrFalseInProductionBuilds && millisSinceLastKeyUpdate < TimeUnit.DAYS.toMillis(1)) {
+        if (!forceOrFalseInProductionBuilds && millisSinceLastKeyUpdate < TimeUnit.SECONDS.toMillis(5)) {
             logcat { "Less than 24h passed, skip re-keying" }
             return
         }
Index: network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyScheduler.kt
IDEA additional info:
Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP
<+>UTF-8
===================================================================
diff --git a/network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyScheduler.kt b/network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyScheduler.kt
--- a/network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyScheduler.kt	(revision e44ddfce33069ae2ee2227bddffb24cc89049381)
+++ b/network-protection/network-protection-impl/src/main/java/com/duckduckgo/networkprotection/impl/rekey/NetPRekeyScheduler.kt	(date 1698157737723)
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@
         job += coroutineScope.launch(dispatcherProvider.io()) {
             while (isActive && vpnFeaturesRegistry.isFeatureRegistered(NetPVpnFeature.NETP_VPN)) {
                 logcat { "Start periodic re-keying attempts" }
-                delay(TimeUnit.HOURS.toMillis(1)) // try to re-key every 1h
+                delay(TimeUnit.SECONDS.toMillis(10)) // try to re-key every 1h
 
                 netPRekeyer.doRekey()
             }
```
- build internal build
- launch app and enable NetP
- [x] with mobile device screen ON, see `Device not locked, skip re-keying` logcat appear every 10 seconds or so
- [x] with mobile device screen OFF, see `Restarting VPN after clearing client keys` logcat appear every 10 seconds or so
llm_reason: The changes introduce a WorkManager-based scheduling for re-keying, which is more efficient than the previous coroutine approach. This reduces battery consumption by leveraging Android's job scheduler.

commit_message:
Commonize Room auto migration validation and initialization.

This change moves auto migration spec validation and auto migration initialization into common code. Generated auto migration related functions had to be mirrored into common code using kotlin.reflect.KClass instead java.lang.Class with a backwards compatible path for old generated code. Reflection assignment checks had to be migrated to an actual/expect function that only does FQN comparison in native which is not quite equivalent as that would fail if a sub-class of the spec is provided, but this might be an unrealistic case. Why would someone provide a subclass of their @ProvidedAutoMigrationSpec? If this is an issue we can enforce @ProvidedAutoMigrationSpec to be final in processor (b/317210564). Regardless of, there is no reflection in native code, so this will do.

Other related changes:
 * Added onPostMigrate(connection: SQLiteConnection) but note that such callback is not invoked yet (need to change gen code).
 * Migrated relevant concrete collection types in gen code to use Kotlin's Stdlib builders as those are KMP compatible.
 * Add XCodeBlock.ofKotlinClassLiteral() to emit KClass literals, note that in Java it converts the java.lang.Class using JvmClassMappingKt but such case should never be generated.

Bug: 299168035
Bug: 309993778
Test: Existing
Relnote: "Add onPostMigrate(connection: SQLiteConnection) to AutoMigrationSpec"
Change-Id: I3b7c9219f6ba79056633530b052d3ce54f013940
llm_reason: The changes refactor code to use common logic for auto migration validation and initialization, reducing redundancy and improving maintainability.

commit_message:
feat: Refactor AndroidTtsEngine, Remove MsTts

This commit introduces the following changes:

-   **feat**: Refactor AndroidTtsEngine:
    -   Rewrites `AndroidTtsEngine` with new features:
        -   Support for getAudio
        -   Support for getStream
        -   Support for play
        -   Support to specify engineName when init
-   -   Removed `MsTTS`, `MsTtsAudioFormat`, `MsTtsFormatManger`, `MsTtsApiType` and related code.
-   -   Removed `LocalTtsServiceTest`.
-   -   Added `AndroidTtsEngineTest` to test the `AndroidTtsEngine`.
-   -   Added `AudioUtils` for audio track and wav header creation.
-   -   Added `DecoderInfo` for decoder parameters.
-   -   Added `SpeechParameters` for speech parameters.
-   -   Added `TtsEngineError` for error handling.
-   -   Refactor `LocalTtsProvider`:
-       -   Removed some old code.
-       -   Utilize the new `AndroidTtsEngine` to provide new features
-   - Refactor BaseAudioFormat to BasicAudioFormat
-   - remove old LocalTTS
+   - Refactor LocalTtsProvider: Utilize the new AndroidTtsEngine
+   - Refactor BaseAudioFormat to BasicAudioFormat
+   - Refactor TextToSpeechSource with shouldDecode
+   - Added AudioUtils: provide audio-related utility functions.
+   - Added DecoderInfo: An interface for providing decoder parameters.
+   - Added SpeechParameters: A data class for handling speech parameters.
+   - Added TtsEngineError: An interface for error handling in the TTS engine.
+   - Removed MsTTS, MsTtsAudioFormat, MsTtsFormatManger, MsTtsApiType and related code.
+   - Removed LocalTtsServiceTest.
+   - Added AndroidTtsEngineTest: Test suite for AndroidTtsEngine.
+   - Refactor audiotrack config: Use a method from AudioUtils.
+   - Use isNeedDecode() instead of shouldDecode()
+   - Use shouldDecode() instead of isNeedDecode()
+   - Remove old LocalTTS.
llm_reason: The commit refactors AndroidTtsEngine by removing MsTTS and related code, simplifies class hierarchies, reduces method complexity, and improves maintainability.

commit_message:
Complete rework of the key data structure + popups

This rework is a very important one, as it solves many conflicts which
arised while implementing special keyboard layouts like dvorak, etc.

With the new data structure, popup keys carry around way less baggage,
but at the same time the structure around the keys have improved a lot and provide more useful information for the popup manager.
This includes that smart hint/accent prioritization is now working
theoretically, now it just needs to be defined correctly in the
json files.

Also the layout and extended popup json files got a authors field
which indicates who the author is. At the current time not very
important, but this is a small step in modularization into assets,
which are the very base of extension support (but that's still a
looong way).

Also some parts of the LayoutManager got a code cleanup, which makes
especially the merging of popups and hints better readable.
llm_reason: The commit refactors key data structures and popup management, reducing complexity by simplifying KeyData and PopupSet classes. It removes unnecessary group IDs and hint modes, improving maintainability.

commit_message:
[Semantics] SCN -> PassthroughLayout(modifier) and remove caches

This is the first big Semantics modifier patch.  This changes the
implementation details of semantics heavily but without touching
API or tests at all yet (except 3 tests that are sensitive to
PassThroughLayout).  I plan to follow up by patches to change the
Semantics API later in April.

A) This updates the implementation details of semantics to internally
use a modifier, without changing the API surface at all yet.  The only
supported use of the new modifier is
"PassThroughLayout + single Semantics modifier".
Any other usage, for now, is not expected to work.

Modifier chaining is not supported yet and it still
uses a single-child-composable-style approach.  In particular,
semantics nodes still apply to the closest "non-semantics" layout node
underneath them, and "collapsing" semantics works the same as before.

B) To simplify the transition to a new design in the future, this patch
also removes all cached state -- it now always constructs derived
values at the last minute from the original source of truth.
Specifically, I deleted caches involved in
1) collapsing (topNodeOfConfig/_children/_unmergedChildren)
2) merging (_mergedChildren, mergedParent, mergedConfig)
3) attaching/detaching (SemanticsOwner MutableMap<>s), a concept
which has been removed in favor of always doing depth-first-search.

This means in turn that no cache invalidation/markNeedsUpdate is needed,
which I noticed was the main source of correctness bugs.

Fixes: 147606016
Fixes: 147606282
Fixes: 150777826
Test: existing tests
Change-Id: I64879097cb05cf08491adec4b599619fd6a8e3de
llm_reason: The changes refactor the Semantics implementation to use a new modifier-based approach, removing caches and simplifying state management. This reduces complexity by eliminating unnecessary cache invalidation logic and improving code structure.

commit_message:
Autofill: Fix net.sqlcipher.database.SQLiteException in WebsiteLoginCredentialsDao (#2124)

ask/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/0/488551667048375/1202696696022583/f

### Description
This PR includes:
- Check SecureStorage.canAccessSecureStorage when determining if autofill is available. There's no point in enabling autofill if we can't decrypt/encrypt data correctly.
- Separate autofillEnabled and autofillAvailable since they are 2 separate things and have different side effects to the autofill feature.
- Handle scenarios where encryptedsharedprefs failed to load AND room database creation. We want to avoid encrypting / decrypting the database when the l1key is null. Moreover we don't want to automatically regenerate the L1key and use it to encrypt the database since it is possible that the encryptedsharedprefs failed and just can't access the already existing L1key.

### Steps to test this PR

_Autofill is not available for non-internal test user_
- [x] Use a non-internal build
- [x] Confirm that autofill isn't triggered when logging into any site
- [x] Confirm that autofill isn't available in settings

_Autofill is disabled via toggle_
- [x] Use an internal build and disabled autofill via credential management screen
- [x] Confirm that autofill isn't triggered when logging into any site
- [x] Confirm that autofill IS available in settings


_Autofill is works as expected_
- [x] Use an internal build and enable autofill via credential management screen
- [x] Confirm that autofill save dialog is show when logging in to a website
- [x] Confirm that autofill select dialog is shown logging in to a website
- [x] Confirm that credential management works as expected

_Simulated failure case_
- [x] Use an internal build
- [x] Confirm that autofill is triggered and is present in settings
- [x] Modify RealSecureStorageKeyStore and set encryptedPreferences to null
- [x] Re-run app and confirm that autofill is no longer available both when logging in and in settings
- [x] Verify no crash happens (previous crash point)
- [x] Remove changes in RealSecureStorageKeyStore
- [x] Re-run app and confirm that autofill is back and works as expected again
llm_reason: The changes primarily address Autofill functionality and SQLiteException handling, focusing on availability checks and internal test user differentiation. These modifications don't directly relate to code smells like energy inefficiency, concurrency issues, storage bloat, security vulnerabilities, or high complexity debt.

commit_message:
Add latest bad health state to the breakage reports (#1654)


Task/Issue URL: 
* https://app.asana.com/0/0/1201610160581882/f
* https://app.asana.com/0/0/1201614005394586/f
* https://app.asana.com/0/0/1201618744517710/f

### Description
We added the runtime bad health detection to internal builds and we have been testing the concept internally, resulting in something valuable. So we want to promote it to production.

Up until now, the health detection was triggering a notification whenever a bad health was detected in AppTP. The (internal) user could click through the notification to get to the diagnostics screen where the information about the AppTP health is displayed.
The diagnostics screen also contains a CTA to send a pixel with the health information.

Some things that we observe during the time we ran this in internal builds:
* memory metrics were too sensitive and the majority of the cases resulted in false possitive
* all other metrics (related to network queues and tracer packets) spot on and every time that triggered they caught a real bad health state

As we move the runtime health detection to production, this PR:
* enables the runtine health monitoring in production builds
* lowers the sensitivity of the memory metrics
* when the user sends an app breakage report, IF the system is in bad health, we will also include in the report the relevant metrics

Regarding privacy (see [asana task](https://app.asana.com/0/69071770703008/1201619382018201/f))
* the data included in the reports does not contain any private information
* the data included in the reports are never static so that different reports can't be connected

**Work in this PR**
* the low memory event has to be sustain for 15min for it to trigger a bad health alert
* we have added the capability to `redact` any raw metric that may present a privacy concern so that they are not sent
* added a `AppHealthCallback` that can be implemented for anybody that wants to get system health updates. Two components implement such public API:
  * `AppHealthCallbackNotification` -> only for internal builds that will trigger a notification upon bad health
  * `AppBadHealthStateHandler` -> for all builds that will store the latest bad health state or clear the store when the system is in good health
* added a `AppHealthStateCollector` that will be called when submitting the app breakage report
  * will append the bad health data to the breakage report **only** if the system is currently in bad health


### Steps to test this PR

_Test bad health in internal builds_
- [x] install the internal debug build
- [x] open app and enable AppTP
- [x] go to "app tracking protection" screen and click on "diagnostics" in the overflow menu
- [x] scroll down and click on the "Bad health" CTA
- [x] verify the bad health notification appears (it can take up to 30s)
- [x] verify the `m_atp_health_monitor_report` pixel is send with `badHealthData` param that contains the health state (base64)
- [x] use [this website](https://onlinestringtools.com/convert-base64-to-string) to decode the report and verify only the `fake` metrics are added to the report and that the only alert is the `tunReadAlerts`
- [x] go back to "app tracking protection" screen and click on "please report an issue"
- [x] select any app from the screen
- [x] click through the "report issue" screens and submit a breakage report
- [x] verify the `m_atp_breakage_report` pixel is sent and that the `breakageMetadata` contains the health information under `latestAppBadHealth`
- [x] go back to diagnostics screen
- [x] scroll down and click on the "good health" CTA
- [x] verify that the bad health notification is gone (can take up to 30s)
- [x] submit another breakage report
- [x] verify the `latestAppBadHealth` is no longer contained in the report

_Test bad health in production builds_
- [x] install the play debug build
- [x] open app and enable AppTP
- [x] go to "app tracking protection" screen and click on "diagnostics" in the overflow menu
- [x] scroll down and click on the "Bad health" CTA
- [x] verify the bad health notification DOES NOT appears (wait around 30s)
- [x] verify the `m_atp_health_monitor_report` pixel is send with `badHealthData` param that contains the health state (base64)
- [x] use [this website](https://onlinestringtools.com/convert-base64-to-string) to decode the report and verify only the `fake` metrics are added to the report and that the only alert is the `tunReadAlerts`
- [x] go back to "app tracking protection" screen and click on "please report an issue"
- [x] select any app from the screen
- [x] click through the "report issue" screens and submit a breakage report
- [x] verify the `m_atp_breakage_report` pixel is sent and that the `breakageMetadata` contains the health information under `latestAppBadHealth`
- [x] go back to diagnostics screen
- [x] scroll down and click on the "good health" CTA and wait until the :( icon becomes :) (it can take up to 30s)
- [x] submit another breakage report
- [x] verify the `latestAppBadHealth` is no longer contained in the report

_Test memory metric sensitivity_
- [x] go to `HealthClassifier:determineHealthMemory` and all `percentageHeap` to be >= 10. This should immediately trigger low memory health alerts
- [x] install the internal debug build
- [x] enable AppTP
- [x] go to diagnostics screen
- [x] verify that the :( appears indicating we're in bad health
- [x] verify the bad health notification does not appear (wait at least 30s)
llm_reason: The changes primarily add new functionality to include bad health state data in breakage reports, modify existing interfaces and classes for better health monitoring, and adjust sensitivity thresholds. There is no clear indication of addressing code smells such as energy inefficiency, concurrency issues, storage bloat, security vulnerabilities, or high complexity debt.

commit_message:
Fix performance issue in BeyondBoundsLayout

Currently when BeyondBoundsLayout is called for a direction opposite to the
orientation of the LazyList, we still add beyondBoundsItems. This causes
a performance issue for focus search where we have to compose all the items
before we exit a focus group. This CL fixes that issue by not adding any
items if BeyondBoundsLayout is called with a direction opposite to the
orientation of the LazyList.

Bug: 249990124
Fixes: 249990124
Test: ./gradlew compose:foundation:foundation:cC -P android.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=androidx.compose.foundation.lazy.list.LazyListBeyondBoundsTest
Change-Id: I723a3a287573b831a0e3a3d2e33edd04aadd7c01
llm_reason: The changes optimize performance by avoiding unnecessary composition of beyond bounds items when the direction is opposite to the LazyList orientation, reducing CPU usage and improving focus search efficiency.

commit_message:
feat: Permission screen (#14130)

* refactor: add some Permissions utils

* feat!: change text primary disabled color

Use transparency to take account of the text background, so it isn't invisible in gray backgrounds

* feat: PermissionItem

Layout object that can be used to display a permission and get it.

Ideally, it would use material components to deliver a better appearance, but we don't have material themes yet

* feat: PermissionsFragment

* feat: PermissionsUntil29Fragment

* feat: Full30and31PermissionsFragment

* feat: TiramisuPermissionsFragment

* refactor: enumerate permissions and permissions screens

Using an enum to manage permissions has some advantages
- can associate permissions with their screens
- is exhaustive, so all permissions possibilities are known statically
- is parcelable, so it can be passed as an activity extra, or a fragment argument

AnkiDroidFolder is converted to a sealed class in order to handle permissionSets

* feat: PermissionsActivity

Ideally, it would use material components to deliver a better appearance, but we don't have material themes yet

* feat: Enable/disable the permissions screen continue button

if all permissions have been granted

* test: Each screen starts normally and has the same permissions of a PermissionSet

* feat: Request permissions with a permission screen

Simplifies things by not having to handle callbacks or blocking UI elements/background tasks while getting a permission.

Also, it can be easily launched in other screens if necessary, which can avoid crashes like #13518

Additionally, adds a better UI/UX for the user

* refactor: remove unused Permissions files

got unused after permissions started being handled by permission screens

With the new permission screens, the "Please grant AnkiDroid the 'All files access' permission to continue" string isn't necessary anymore

* fix: use compat method in hasAnyOfPermissionsBeenDenied()

* Adopt API34 changes

- activity recreation via ActivityCompat.recreate
- dynamic instantation via getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance()
- onBackPressed deprecation + call super

* remove unused string

---------

Co-authored-by: Mike Hardy <github@mikehardy.net>
llm_reason: The commit refactors permission handling by introducing an enum and sealed classes, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability. It also removes unused files and simplifies permission management logic.

Create AudioStream and implementations

 This CL is a refactoring of AudioSource for the purpose of writing unit tests with more coverage.
 AudioSource will use AudioStream interface to read audio data. AudioStreamImpl wraps android AudioRecord and is the default instance in AudioSource. AudioStream interface makes it possible to do more granularly control for unit testing.

commit_message:
Run macrobenchmark in baseline profile modules

This PR adds support to run macrobenchmark in baseline profile modules.
A new benchmark build types is added with minification enabled, that
uses the same baseline profiles generated for the release build types.
As a result it also follows the same configurations. For example setting
`automaticGenerationDuringBuild` true will force baseline profiles to be
regenerated for any code change, while keeping it false will require
manually call `generateBaselineProfile` in order to use the most updated
baseline profile during benchmark. This feature is also only supported
from AGP 8.1-alpha7 and above due to b/265438201 fixed in that version.

Test: ./gradlew :benchmark:integration-tests:baselineprofile-library-consumer:testBaselineProfileGeneration :benchmark:integration-tests:baselineprofile-flavor-consumer:testBaselineProfileGeneration :benchmark:integration-tests:baselineprofile-consumer:testBaselineProfileGeneration :benchmark:benchmark-baseline-profile-gradle-plugin:test
Bug: 262070052
Change-Id: I40e1201f3d72e84d41fea8ce8cc1d23704c7909f

commit_message:
Remove OnNavigatorNavigatedListener entirely

Instead of requiring each Navigator to send
dispatch events, have Navigator.navigate()
return which destination was navigated to
(or null if the navigate failed).

This simplifies the logic of all Navigators,
removes a common source of confusion in
implementing a Navigator, and gives
NavController a more direct way to update its
back stack.

With this change, NavController now always
ensures that all parent NavGraphs are on
the back stack after a navigate() event,
avoiding IllegalArgumentExceptions.

Test: lots of updated tests
BUG: 111278637
Change-Id: I66f6ee3ab37b56961388bd1f607b14a713f830fe
llm_reason: The commit removes OnNavigatorNavigatedListener by modifying Navigator.navigate() to return destination information directly, simplifying navigator implementations and reducing potential confusion. This change reduces code complexity by eliminating listener management and centralizing navigation state updates in NavController.

commit_message:
Select Across Multiple Pages

Implements multi-page text selection for the Jetpack PDF library. Allows
users to select text across multiple pages by dragging selection handles
beyond page boundaries.
- Enable continuous text selection across multiple pages.
- Handle drag interactions beyond single-page limits.
- Optimize performance by minimizing API calls.
- Ensure accurate retrieval of selected text content.

Bug: 386398335
Test: local
Change-Id: I7be26b7dc433d9add9c98b35dd14a82b8893df84
llm_reason: The changes simplify and refactor code to support multi-page text selection, reducing complexity by removing redundant classes and methods. The removal of DocumentSelection and merging its functionality into SelectionModel improves code structure and maintainability.

commit_message:
Jetifier ProGuard improvements

1) Adds support for cases when a single ProGuard rule needs to be
replaced by multiple rules because mappings are not always 1:1.

One of the examples is:
android/support/v4/media/** -> androidx/media/**, android/support/v4/**

solve some of the simple rules that contain wildcards. The automated
solution sucessfully maps around 80% of the wildcard rules.

This was tested on 600 ProGuard rules aggregated from wild. Single unit
tests also added.

Move use case combination supported logic from CameraUseCaseAdapter to CameraInfoInternal

  This lays the foundation to multiple future works, e.g. we need to check if use case combination is guaranteed to be supported or unsupported through the current CameraX logic before checking with feature combination query APIs. Since CameraX Feature Combination Query API needs to be workable before binding flow, we needed to move this logic out of CameraUseCaseAdapter since CameraUseCaseAdapter is created during the binding flow. Besides, design-wise, it makes more sense to check if use case combination is supported in a CameraInfo or separate class, not CameraUseCaseAdapter.

  Note that this CL does not focus on doing a full refactoring of the use case combination supported logic. Rather, it just moves the current logic to dedicated/appropriate classes without changing any logic flow.

llm_reason: The changes refactor code to move use case combination logic from CameraUseCaseAdapter to CameraInfoInternal, improving modularity and separation of concerns. This reduces complexity by placing the logic in a more appropriate class, aligning with better design principles.

Implement strict frame rate enforcement for SessionConfig API

 This change enforces a strict behavior for frame rate settings configured via the SessionConfig API. Unlike UseCase target frame rates, which are flexible and allow fallback to the closest supported device frame rate, SessionConfig will only accept explicitly supported frame rates without any fallback mechanism.

commit_message:
Add Modifier.composed and materialize

Modifier.composed allows the creation of modifier-factory modifiers using
composition. Before the modifier is applied to a LayoutNode the
ComposedModifiers are materialized into real modifier instances,
allowing for the element-specific injection of hoisted state, or
remember{}ing element-specific modifier instances altogether.

Test: ComposedModifierTest; :ui:ui-core:test
Change-Id: I492e83caa4f2c52b0d0151013f2640f674797b71
llm_reason: The changes introduce a new Modifier.composed function and materialize method, which improve code structure by enabling stateful modifiers. This refactoring reduces complexity by allowing element-specific modifier instances while maintaining a clean API.

commit_message:
Refactor TextStyle: delegate to Paragraph/SpanStyle

TextStyle had its own properties that are also properties of SpanStyle
and ParagraphStyle.

commit_message:
Switch to Double for all metrics

Test: ./gradlew benchmark:benchmark-common:cC benchmark:benchmark-macro:cC
Test: TrivialKotlinTest / TrivialStartupBenchmark # verified in Studio
Fixes: 197008210

Relnote: "Switch to floating point metrics throughout the benchmark
libraries (truncated in the Studio UI)."

Floating point metrics enable tracking smaller increments over time,
and avoid the hiding of instability which comes from rounding. This is
particularly important in faster benchmarks (very fast functions in
microbenchmark, or sub-ms frame timings in FrameTimingMetric in
Macrobenchmark (once we switch to perfetto-based frame timing).

Studio output is truncated to tenths of a ns / ms (micro/macrobench
respectively) for readability.

As part of this change, merged Stats/MetricResult as the point of the
wrapper is further reduced when all callers provide List<Double>.

Change-Id: I69249339c0a2ccedd015e45c6ab528cb114d7103
llm_reason: The commit changes various metrics from Double to Long, which reduces memory usage and improves performance by avoiding unnecessary floating-point operations. This is particularly beneficial for energy efficiency as it minimizes CPU cycles spent on computations.

commit_message:
Address ActivityEmbedding APIs feedback

1. Add descriptions on layoutDirection
2. See below Relnote
RelNote: "
1. Align the API naming - Use add/remove for multiple instances:
  - registerRule changes to addRule
  - unregisterRule changes to removeRule
2. Replace getSplitRules with getRules since ActivityRule is not a split rule
3. Add RuleController#setRules to set a bunch of rules
4. Extract rule related APIs from SplitController to singleton RuleController. They are:
  - addRule
  - removeRule
  - getRules
  - setRules
  - clearRules
  - parseRules
5. Extract #isActivityEmbedded from SplitController to singleton
   ActivityEmbeddingController. They are:
  - isActivityEmbedded
6. Remove SplitController#initialize. To set rules from XML file, please
   use RuleController#parseRules and #setRules.
   Before this change:
     SplitController.initialize(context, R.xml.static_rules)
   After this change:
     val ruleController = RuleController.getInstance(context)
     val rules = ruleController.parseRules(R.xml.static_rules)
     ruleController.setRules(rules)
7. We don't distinguish static rules with runtime rules anymore. That said,
   calling #clearRules results to clear all rules no matter they are
   registered with static XML rule definitions or at runtime. To have
   the legacy behavior of `SplitController#clearRegisteredRules`, please
   call `RuleController#parseRules` with the XML resources id and call
   `RuleController#setRules` to set back the rules again.
   Before this change:
     SplitController.getInstance(context).clearRegisteredRules()
   After this change:
     val ruleController = RuleController.getInstance(context)
     val rules = ruleController.parseRules(R.xml.static_rules)
     ruleController.setRules(rules)
"
Test: presubmit
Bug: 255952916

Merged-In: Ib39671fb22a9ccef3b0aa8967ee18c3168c54c63
Change-Id: Ib39671fb22a9ccef3b0aa8967ee18c3168c54c63
llm_reason: The commit refactors code to improve modularity and separation of concerns by moving rule management from RuleController to SplitController, reducing redundancy and improving API clarity.

commit_message:
Use StateFlow for publishing migration progress

Splitting this into several commits would be too hard, so sorry about
this commit doing a lot of things at once.

* Migration service is refactored in a major way. For progress,
  StateFlow is used. Progress now includes not only the transfer stage,
  but also the stage of calculating transfer size and success/failure
  states. UI reads this progress directly.

* Binding to the service is performed using coroutines, which should
  simplify integrating services with UI.

* The ongoing-migration toolbar button in DeckPicker now starts at 5% so
  that the user sees at least some of the circular progress indicator in
  the very beginning.
llm_reason: The commit replaces polling with StateFlow, reducing unnecessary background activity and improving battery life.

commit_message:
Add source to import flow pixels (#6310)

Task/Issue URL: https://app.asana.com/1/137249556945/task/1210421589932466 

### Description
Updates the pixels around password import prompts/buttons/dialogs, namely to include a `source` parameter indicating where the import was launched from.

Possible values: 
- `password_management_promo`
- `password_management_empty_state`
- `password_management_overflow`
- `autofill_settings_button`
- `unknown` (should never happen in reality)

### Steps to test this PR

Suggested logcat filter: `message~:"Pixel sent.*source="`

#### Password management view
- [x] Fresh install, and visit Passwords from the browser overflow menu
- [x] Verify: `autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_shown with params: {source=password_management_empty_state}`
- [x] Verify: `autofill_import_google_passwords_preimport_prompt_displayed with params: {source=password_management_empty_state}`
- [x] Verify: `autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_tapped with params: {source=password_management_empty_state}`
- [x] Dismiss the dialog. Verify: `autofill_import_google_passwords_result_user_cancelled with params: {stage=pre-import-dialog, source=password_management_empty_state}`
- [x] Manually add a password and return to the list
- [x] Verify you see the password import promo, and `autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_shown with params: {source=password_management_promo}`
- [x] Tap the promo CTA; verify: `autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_tapped with params: {source=password_management_promo}`
- [x] Complete or cancel the import flow to return to the password list. Tap on overflow and choose `Import Passwords From Google`. 
- [x] Verify: `autofill_import_google_passwords_overflow_menu_tapped with params: {source=password_management_overflow}`
- [x] Verify: `autofill_import_google_passwords_preimport_prompt_displayed with params: {source=password_management_overflow}`

#### Password settings screen
- [x] Leave management screen, and visit app settings, then `Passwords & Autofill`
- [x] Verify: `autofill_import_google_passwords_import_button_shown with params: {source=autofill_settings_button}`

commit_message:
Improve PaneScaffoldMotionData API

This CL simplifies the scope structure of pane scaffolds and improves
the implementation of PaneScaffoldMotionScope by:

1. Renames PaneScaffoldMotionScope to PaneScaffoldMotionDataProvider,
   makes it a generic interface, and provide accessors to the
   PaneMotionData it holds to avoid direct accessing to the underlying
   data structure.
2. Moves the motion scope to be a field under
   PaneScaffoldTransitionScope instead, to simplify the scope
   inheritance structure.

Relnote: "Remove PaneScaffoldMotionScope and turn it into a field under
  PaneScaffoldTransitionScope. Also rename it to
  PaneScaffoldMotionDataProvider and remove the access to the underlying
  data structure of PaneMotionData - instead, provide getter and looping
  methods for accessing the data."

Test: existing tests, should be no-op
Change-Id: Id8884de1cf9f74f006bc2cc3b8a515666e431976
llm_reason: The changes refactor the PaneScaffoldMotionScope to simplify its structure by renaming it, making it generic, and providing getter methods instead of direct access. This reduces complexity and improves encapsulation.

commit_message:
Add experimental support for a version of Surface that handles click and make a regular Surface to block touches behind it.

Clickable Surface allows us to handle many issues at once:
1. Allows to define clicks on the Surface layout node, making a11y to be correct
2. Allows to define clicks in the single layout node without the need to transfer indication hnadling to the node below (because of the clipping inside the surface)
3. Allows for a desired default behaviour in a non-clickable Surface when we block pointer events from going under the surface. Throughout the many components it is seens like a desiged behaviour and often leads to bugs (snackbar, Drawer, modal sheet & card being norable examples of surfaces that shouldn't allow touches behind it).

To make this work properly, I refactored ModalBottomSheetLayout to work with the sheet-based swipeable (instead of content-based), since sheet is not blocking clicks.

With this one overload we solve many issues so it seems like a worthy experiment.
Relnote: BEHAVIOUR-BREAKING: Surface now consumes clicks, making clicks added via Surface(Modifier.clickable) to be a no-op. Please, use new experimental overload of Surface that accepts onClick.
Relnote: Added a new Surface overload that handles clicks as well as other clickable functionality: indication, interactionSource, enabled/disabled. It wasn't possible to use a regular non-clickable Surface with the Modifier.clickable because the Surface will not clip the ripple indication in those cases.
Test: Added new for an overload
Fixes: 183775620

Change-Id: I73e6c76ed6389040d824a5dcc82dc84dc4fea0c7
llm_reason: The changes refactor the Surface component to add an experimental clickable overload, reducing code duplication and improving maintainability. The refactoring simplifies the implementation by consolidating click handling logic and removing redundant code.
