commit_message:
Allow merging popups of hints #618

A new configuration was introduced to allow showing the popup keys of
the hint keys of a given character key in addition the character key's
normal popup keys.

The previous change allowed both number and symbol hint to be merged at
the same time, with the number hint being shown as popup only.
Therefore, when allowing the popups of the hint key to be shown as
popups, both hint keys need to be taken into account.

To ensure this and also take into account the separate key hint
settings for number and symbol hints, the MutablePopupSet was extended
to contain both hint keys as well as both lists of popup keys in
addition to the existing main key and relevant list. The logic that
chooses the key prioritization when rendering the popup has now also
been moved from the PopupManager to the PopupSet.

For performance, the prioritized collection of popup keys is generated
once and then cached for a given configuration in a new PopupKeys
object. This class now has the collection semantics previously present
in the PopupSet class. Different from before, the PopupKeys object now
explicitly contains the prioritized keys (those that should be shown
directly above the original key for easier reach) in order of priority.

The PopupManager now only needs to take the number of prioritized keys
(maximum 3: main key, number hint, symbol hint) when calculating the
key positions in the popup.
llm_reason: The changes refactor and simplify the PopupSet and related classes, reducing code complexity by removing duplicate logic and consolidating responsibilities. The introduction of a cached PopupKeys object improves performance and readability.

commit_message:
Multi-resume (active error recovery) support in CameraPipe

This CL is a large-scale change that adds multi-resume, or active error
recovery support in CameraPipe.

High-level changes include:

- GraphLifecycleManager: This oversees the lifecycle of
  CameraControllers, and make sure the operations on them are atomic.
  This is also connected to CameraStatusMonitors, to monitor camera
  access priority changes as well as camera availability changes.
- CameraStatusMonitor: A new interface that monitors status changes of
  cameras, as mentioned above.
- A state model for Camera2CameraController: This is needed in order for
  us to know the exact state the controller is in, specifically when a
  camera is disconnected. This allows us to restart the controller only
  when necessary.

Bug: 263201241
Test: Switch between 2 apps in multi-window mode.
Change-Id: I2a2158781bbbadc01872194f25f83703c7c98b10
llm_reason: The changes remove several concurrency-related issues by simplifying state management and eliminating unnecessary locks. For example, the CameraControllerSimulator no longer has a 'tryRestart()' method which could lead to race conditions, and the removal of ControllerState enum reduces complexity in state transitions.

commit_message:
Room Suspending Transaction

Create an API for performing database transaction with coroutines. When
starting a transaction coroutine a thread of the database query executor
is hold for performing all database operations within the coroutine. It
is recommended that all DAO functions invoked are also suspend
functions. In the case they are blocking DAO methods, then invoking
those on a different context other than the transaction context will
throw an exception, preventing possible deadlocks.

Bug: 120854786
Test: ./gradlew room:room-compiler:test
                room:integration-tests:room-testapp-kotlin:cC
                room:integration-tests:room-testapp:cC

Change-Id: I30f76db29a3882968f43e1dd1c1038b5afd7f299
llm_reason: The commit introduces a new API for performing database transactions with coroutines, ensuring that blocking DAO methods throw exceptions if called outside the transaction context. This addresses potential concurrency issues by preventing deadlocks and ensuring proper thread management.

commit_message:
Change CoreTextField to use BringIntoViewRequester.

CoreTextField needs to ensure the cursor is in the visible bounds of all
scrollable parents, including the one in the text field itself.
Originally, it tried doing this by going through some of the text
service abstractions and ultimately calling
`View.requestRectangleOnScreen`. However, composable parents also need
to be aware of the request, so the correct way to do this is to use the
relatively recent `BringIntoViewRequester`. I'm not sure why all the
indirection through text services was done, it isn't necessary and
obscures the code, so I just made `CoreTextField` directly call
`BringIntoViewRequester.bringIntoView()`. This helps with b/192043120
but doesn't completely fix it, due to b/216842427.

Bug: b/192043120
Fixes: b/178211874
Test: Tested manually.
Test: Refactored TextFieldDelegate unit tests to use the new code path:
    ./gradlew :compose:f:f:test
Relnote: "`notifyFocusedRect` methods in `TextInputSession` and
    `TextInputService` are now deprecated and won't be called. Use
    `BringIntoViewRequester` instead."

Change-Id: Ia4302c5f6ee79eec30a9f42c149da8775e1ed57e
llm_reason: The changes refactor CoreTextField to directly use BringIntoViewRequester, simplifying the code by removing unnecessary indirection through text services. This reduces complexity and improves maintainability.

commit_message:
First pass at an option to generate response based code with jsExport (#4907)

* First pass at an option to generate response based code with jsExport

This allows us to performantly fetch data on the JS side and then
unsafeCast() to our response based generated models, which, in turn,
allows us to use the same code to handle JS-based responses that we use
to parse non-JS based responses, and obviates the need for apollo-jsei.

commit_message:
Feature/home screen call to action (#304)

* Add permission required to create home screen shortcuts

* Add class to allow downloading favicons

* Add "add to home" functionality as a first pass.

* Choose better shortcut title; appropriately enable/disable menu item

* Add support for two distinct layout states; browser and blank tab

These have slightly different requirements in terms of their scroll-observing behaviours. We do want the browser layout mode to respect the toolbar scrolling for instance, but we don't want the same behaviour for the new tab view. Trying to use the same `app:layout_behavior="@string/appbar_scrolling_view_behavior"` behaviour in both modes means that the new tab layout is essentially forced off screen vertically and it makes it difficult to add a button that anchors to the bottom.

Splitting these into two distinct modes lets us better control both modes without trying to find one set of views and flags which works well enough for both.

* Further work on UX of call to action; wired to show screen when clicked

* Rename "time based" feature analyzer class to something more generic

* Add in feature for home screen call to action

* Only show call to action at appropriate time for active variants

* Remove "add to home" commits

* Improve testing to ensure user dismissing c2a doesn't impact banner

* Add static import for better readability

* Undoing rename of dagger parameter

* Remove unused function

* Add light ripple effect to call to action

* Code formatting

* Adding activity transition for call to action

* Upgrading tooling

* Add fix to ensure only Nougat or newer devices get experiment

* Fix centering of call to action button

* Add correct menu reference for tools:menu link

* Move new tab layout into its own layout file

* Programmatically hide DDG logo when there's not enough space for it

* Layout tweaks around call to action button

* Add @Synchronized to getVariant to prevent concurrent allocations

* Tweaking animations and minor UI changes to call to action

* Formatting

* Fix ddgLogo visibility problem after setting DDG as default browser

* Add comment to an otherwise unclear piece of code

* Add new default browser illustrations

* Configure variants
llm_reason: The changes refactor multiple classes and methods to reduce redundancy and improve code structure. For example, merging banner and home screen call-to-action notifications into a unified system simplifies the codebase.

Add support to render GraphicsLayer into a bitmap

Relnote: "Added GraphicsLayer#toImageBitmap suspend
method to support rendering contents of a bitmap
into a GraphicsLayer. This is a hardware accelerated
rendering operation on API level 22+ (inclusive) which
supports over 99% of all Android devices.
On Android API level 21 this falls back onto software rendering."

tests to sample against pixels obtained by toImageBitmap method

llm_reason: The commit adds support for rendering GraphicsLayer into a bitmap, which is a hardware-accelerated operation on API level 22+ and falls back to software rendering on API 21. This reduces unnecessary CPU usage by leveraging hardware acceleration where available.

commit_message:
Optimize animations

- Remove ComplexDouble and compute real/imaginary parts directly to
  avoid allocations in spring computations
- Wrap exceptions with function calls
- Removed unnecessary tests (SpringSimulation.finalPosition is never
  set to UNSET and is a private API)
- In SpringSimulation, don't go through a complicated one-time init()
  that must be checked on every frame, just do the computations. They
  are cheap enough that they cost less than the function call and
  branches
- Factored a couple of computations (that R8/AOT can take care of
  but it helps interpreted mode/pre-JIT) in SpringSimulation
- Switch to fast variants of a couple of functions (coerceIn,
  isFinite)
- Stop using lateinit in VectorizedAnimationSpec to avoid generating
  code based on Kotlin properties/exceptions. Just use simple null
  checks/default values instead
- Don't lookup values twice in maps (contains() followed by get()),
  just lookup the value directly and check if it's not null since
  we expect non-null types

Also:
- Removed unused code (Motion.copy())
- Removed unnecessary generic parameters
- Fixed a few formatting issues

These changes yield the following improvements on a Pixel 2:

With R8:
- Tween animations are 9.3% faster
- Keyframe animations are 22.9% faster
- Over-damped spring evaluation is 21% faster
- Under-damped spring evaluation is 48.8% faster
- Critically damped spring evaluation is 16.1% faster

Without R8 (JIT only):
- Tween animations are 17.3% faster
- Keyframe animations are 24.1% faster
- Over-damped spring evaluation is 23.7% faster
- Under-damped spring evaluation is 49.4% faster
- Critically damped spring evaluation is 14.4% faster

Test: compose:animation-core instrumented and unit tests
Relnote: N/A
Change-Id: I8c0ff927ddd437782e2720c58bc19184271908cd
llm_reason: The commit includes several optimizations aimed at reducing computational overhead, such as removing unnecessary allocations by eliminating ComplexDouble and directly computing real/imaginary parts. Additionally, simplifying SpringSimulation initialization and avoiding function calls improves performance, leading to faster animations.

commit_message:
Fix baseline profiles and benchmarks on android multiuser

Benchmark and Baseline profile generation require transfering files
between storage accessible by shell and storage accessible by the user.
Shell runs on user 0. When the android selected user is also zero, shell
can see both storages. When the android selected user is not zero, shell
cannot see user storage.

As a result we cannot invoke shell commands operating in user storage
to copy and move files. Instead we need to utilize the running
instrumentation process to access user storage and shell to access shell
storage. In order to communicate between these two, the class
VirtualFile implements a number of methods to copy streams between the
two. When in shell storage we can use commands like `cat` and `cp` from
`/dev/stdin` to a file to write. When in user storage we use the java
api of the running process.

Test: Added and manually. Note we'll need to enable multiuser devices in CI.
Bug: 356684617
Bug: 373641155
Relnote: (Experimental) Enable Baseline Profile generation, and benchmarking on apps installed to a secondary user, such as all apps on headless Android Auto devices. This support has been tested in some scenarios, but let us know with a bug if it doesn't work for you.
Change-Id: I9fcbecf2a9c9075d161de69118c443d7a37102c0
llm_reason: The changes primarily address multiuser support and file handling, which don't directly relate to code smells like energy inefficiency, concurrency issues, storage bloat, security vulnerabilities, or high complexity debt. The modifications improve functionality but do not introduce or fix common code smells.

commit_message:
Implement Annotation support in KSP

This CL implements the AnnotationBox in KSP.

Unlike Java, we don't have 1 base class for elements in KSP.
Moreover, with synthetics, how we read annotations is not just
based on the KSAnnotated. For instance, when reading annotations
for a property, we should ignore all other annotations with
use sites other than field (or null, but that depends on the
declaration of the annotation).

To make the implementation manageable, I've created a new
XAnnotated interface for all annotation related methods and
KspAnnotated to implement them via delegates.
KspAnnotated also has built in filtering for use sites to
make them more declarative when specifying the delegate.

Unfortunately, KSP's java implementation has some known
bugs (https://github.com/google/ksp/issues/96) so I couldn't
add tests with java sources but I've added kotlin tests that
run with both kapt and ksp.
The same bug also prevents us from implementing use-site-inference
properly. When use-site is not declared, Kotlin uses the @Target
annotation on the annotation declaration, which has a list of
types hence cannot be read until #96 is fixed. For now, I've
implemented an approximation in KspAnnotated.

Bug: 160322705
Test: XAnnotationBoxTest
Change-Id: Ia4afffc0730ad346f46ac532e345d6006704c381
llm_reason: The commit refactors code to implement annotation support in KSP by creating a new XAnnotated interface and KspAnnotated class, which improves modularity and reduces code duplication. This change simplifies the handling of annotations across different elements, making the codebase more maintainable.

commit_message:
Updates tv-material components to expose a nullable MutableInteractionSource

Components that previously exposed an API such as:

interactionSource: MutableInteractionSource = remember { MutableInteractionSource() }

Have been updated to instead expose a nullable MutableInteractionSource that defaults to null. This allows for some components to never create and remember a MutableInteractionSource, and allows for some other components to lazily create one only when needed. Some components always need a MutableInteractionSource currently, so if null they will just create a new one internally - so for those cases there are no changes, but it allows for future optimizations.

Test: updateApi, existing tests
Bug: b/298048146
Relnote: "tv-material components exposing a MutableInteractionSource in their API have been updated to now expose a nullable MutableInteractionSource that defaults to null. There are no semantic changes here: passing null means that you do not wish to hoist the MutableInteractionSource, and it will be created inside the component if needed. Changing to null allows for some components to never allocate a MutableInteractionSource, and allows for other components to only lazily create an instance when they need to, which improves performance across these components. If you are not using the MutableInteractionSource you pass to these components, it is recommended that you pass null instead. It is also recommended that you make similar changes in your own components."
Change-Id: I309b436d212ef53897979df40da9b1768377893f
llm_reason: The changes expose a nullable MutableInteractionSource that defaults to null, allowing components to avoid unnecessary allocations and improve performance by lazily creating instances only when needed.

commit_message:
Add sample-based metric result format

Fixes: 199940612
Test: ./gradlew benchmark:benchmark-common:cC benchmark:benchmark-macro:cC
Relnote: "Add new type of sampled metric to UI and JSON output, focused on
    percentiles of multiple samples per iteration."

Creates a second type of metric result, for percentile-focused metrics
like frame time. These metrics will record multiple values per
iteration, and display distributions (P50,90,95,99) from the entire
collected range of values.

This will drastically simplify the new frame metrics measurements,
both in terms of numbers of values reported in UI, but also in
avoiding showing distribution stats... of distribution stats, as we
did before.

Previous:

  frameTime50thPercentileMs   min   6.0,   median   6.0,   max   7.0
  frameTime90thPercentileMs   min   7.0,   median   7.0,   max   9.0
  frameTime95thPercentileMs   min   7.0,   median   7.0,   max   9.0
  frameTime99thPercentileMs   min  14.0,   median  14.0,   max  14.0
            totalFrameCount   min 294.0,   median 294.0,   max 294.0

New:

                frameTimeMs   P50  7.1,   P90  9.4,   P95  9.6,   P99  12.0

Note that this is only used in the FrameTimingTraceMetrics, so isn't
on by default yet.

Similarly, in JSON, we output this data in a new way, as a separate
"sampledMetric" map, e.g.:

    "sampledMetrics": {
        "frameTimeMs": {
            "P50": 7.072010000000001,
            "P90": 9.4366065,
            "P95": 9.62164825,
            "P99": 12.00158825,
            "runs": [
                [
                    16.025587,
                    8.140102,
                    7.137429,
                    7.380798,
                    ...
                ],
                ...
            ]
        }
    }

Change-Id: I56247e421890537d27f1e3f284209aa590472377
llm_reason: The changes refactor the code to simplify handling of metrics by introducing a new sampled metric format, reducing redundancy and improving data structure organization.

commit_message:
Add general settings (#4783)

Task/Issue URL:
https://app.asana.com/0/1200204095367872/1207857476165688/f

### Description
Adds a "General" section to settings containing:
- Autocomplete Suggestions
- Recently Visited Sites
- Private Voice Search

### Steps to test this PR

_General setting_
- [ ] Go to “Settings” > “General”
- [ ] Verify that “m_settings_general_open” pixel is sent

_Autocomplete Suggestions_
- [ ] Toggle the setting on
- [ ] Verify that the “m_settings_general_autocomplete_on” pixel is sent
- [ ] Verify that autocomplete suggestions are shown
- [ ] Toggle the setting off
- [ ] Verify that the “m_settings_general_autocomplete_off” pixel is
sent
- [ ] Verify that autocomplete suggestions are no longer shown

_Include Recently Visited Sites_
- [ ] Toggle the setting on
- [ ] Verify that the “m_settings_general_recent_sites_on” pixel is sent
- [ ] Verify that recently visited sites are shown
- [ ] Toggle the setting off
- [ ] Verify that the “m_settings_general_recent_sites_off” pixel is
sent
- [ ] Verify that recently visited sites are not shown

_On a device that supports voice search_
- [ ] Verify that the “Private Voice Search” setting is shown
- [ ] Toggle the setting on
- [ ] Verify that the “m_settings_general_voice_search_on” pixel is sent
- [ ] Go back and verify that the voice search microphone is visible in
the omnibar
- [ ] Toggle the setting off
- [ ] Verify that the “m_settings_general_voice_search_off” pixel is
sent
- [ ] Go back and verify that the voice search microphone is not visible
in the omnibar

_On a device that does not support voice search (Emulator)_
- [ ] Verify that the “Private Voice Search” setting is not visible
- [ ] Verify that the divider is not visible

### UI changes
| Before  | After | General |
| ------ | ----- |  ----- |

![general_before](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a38dc57f-beca-48c4-aee0-44b820ce1396)|![general_after](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/027c8171-3a42-48e0-92c5-4fc09ec9cb6e)|![general_settings](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f8fd0c21-a0c8-403c-bdc4-52f4f9291edf)

commit_message:
Benchmark and allocation reduction for JankStats

This CL adds benchmark tests for JankStats operations. These tests
report the number of allocations caused by specific JankStats actions,
such as adding states or reporting per-frame data. A manual test,
AllocationTester, is also added to the integration tests. That test
allows further analysis of where those allocations occur (a detail that
is not part of the benchmark results).

Also, this CL includes fixes for allocations that could be eliminated through
object pooling internal to JankStats.

Bug: 205131448
Test: Added new tests as part of this CL, also ran JankStatsTest
Relnote: "Added benchmark tests to track allocations, eliminated some
internal allocations related to state management and reporting.
Note that FrameData passed to listeners is now considered volatile;
that structure will be reused for the next frame and the data is
only reliable until the listener returns.
Removed Executor from constructor for JankStats; listeners are now
called on whatever thread the internal data was received upon."

Change-Id: I1274320bf29c171b82578868e657a3b01f7805c7
llm_reason: The changes introduce benchmark tests to track allocations and reduce internal object creation through pooling, which optimizes memory usage and reduces garbage collection overhead. This directly impacts CPU efficiency by minimizing unnecessary allocations.

commit_message:
Introduce ClientContext, RequestContext and ResponseContext (#2958)

The current execution context flow is:

* ApolloClient is built with some ClientContext
* A new request is built with its own RequestContext
* Before execution, the request context with be merged with the client
  context. This allows to access the store or dispatcher from any point
in the interceptor chain
* The response context is initialied with the full context and the
  populated as it bubbles up the chain

I'm a bit unsure about that last part as it potentially gives access to
some internals but maybe it's hidden enough that we don't care much

commit_message:
Make androidx.room and androidx.sqlite target Kotlin 2.0

This required the following changes:

 * Suppress 'ACTUAL_ANNOTATIONS_NOT_MATCH_EXPECT' in SQLiteException.android.kt and ThreadLocal.jvmAndroid.kt due to being impossible to match annotations in expect when actual is a typealias to a platform type. Meanwhile in room-common repeatable annotations in JVM use @JvmRepeatable while expect uses Kotlin's @Repeatable and while trying to match actual with expect, an error is produced due to @JvmRepeatable and @Repeatable being used together.

 * Fix various annotations mismatch between actual / expect.

 * Removing @JvmField from common code as expect properties have no backing field and @JvmField targets FIELD.

 * Smart-cast is not possible for expect properties, therefore first store in local variable before smart-case usage. Observed in TableInfo, FtsTableInfo, ViewInfo and MigrationUtil.

 * Adding load() and getRefreshKey() to expect LimitOffsetPagingSource to conform to modality of actual overriding those functions.

 * Make a XProcessingEnvConfig a normal class (instead of a data class) since it has a private constructor with a public copy() usage which is now invalid.

 * Set useKsp2=true on projects with KSP and Kotlin language 2.0 (Room KMP test apps).

 * Workaround KSP2 issue with default value of annotations not appearing in value list. Added a get() that returns nullable XAnnotationValue.

 * Workaround KSP2 issue of projecting value class constructor as member of (to support generics).

 * Workaround XPoet creating JavaPoet types of invalid methods (internal) when XProcessingEnvConfig.excludeMethodsWithInvalidJvmSourceNames is set to true due to Kotlin codegen.

Bug: 315461431
Bug: 384600605
Test: Existing
Relnote: "Native APIs updated due to Kotlin 2.0"
Change-Id: I8efb015c88682921780370c8bed5931d9933a319
llm_reason: The changes involve refactoring annotations and code structure to align with Kotlin 2.0 standards, such as suppressing specific compiler warnings, adjusting annotation usages, and modifying class structures which reduces technical debt by improving maintainability.

commit_message:
Updating Fenix to work with the new logins API

Switched to always using `Login` instead of the `SavedPassword` alias.

Made `MasterPasswordTipProvider.saveLogins()` call
`importLoginsAsync()`.  This is needed because it's the only method that
inputs a `Login` rather than a `LoginEntry`.

Moved the `SavedLoginsStorageController.kt.syncAndUpdateList` call
to inside `add()` and `update()`.  This simplifies the error handling a
bit.

Refactored dupe-checking code to use findLoginToUpdate()

Refactored `AddLoginFragment` / `EditLoginFragment` to put the username
error handling code all in 1 method.  I think it's easier to follow the
logic of showing/hiding the error labels when it's all in one place.
This fixes issues #24103 and #24104.  I would love to address #24102,
but I'm not sure what the correct behavior is there so I just kept that
the same.
llm_reason: The changes refactor duplicate checking to use findLoginToUpdate(), simplify error handling, and consolidate username error logic into a single method. This reduces code duplication and improves maintainability.
